volume.h1:
SOUTH INDIAN INSCRIPTIONS
VOLUME XXV Part 1

inscription.p: Stone lying on the bund of the tank.
This is dated in the month of May in the year 1852 A.D.

This Telugu verse inscription records the rebuilding of the lake which
was built by Gōṁpanavoḍiyalu.

It states that Nadi Varadarājaṁma got a portion of the lake being
carefully rebuilt. The lake was completed with the money brought from
Nēkhunāṁkhān, for the merit of the king. It mentions the names of
Mahaṁmadu Agharapu Bāhubaluḍu, Engineer Major Ḍibeṭsu, Sundara-
modalār, etc.

inscription.p: Stone in the Choultry in the village.

Damaged. It refers to Udayagiri-rājya and the names of individuals
viz., Nāgi-nāyani and Kōsināyani.

inscription.head:
No. 3.
(A.R. No. 3 of 1908.)
CUMBUM, CUMBUM TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Another slab in the same choultry.

This inscription is dated in Śaka •••••, Chaitra, śu 2, Monday which
details are insufficient for verification.

This record does not give the name of the king. Palaeographically
it may be assigned to the 15th-16th century. It records the provision of
grains and pepper for worship and food-offerings to god Vīrabhadradēva
by the merchant guild Ubhaya-nānādēśa Ayyāvo[ḷe] •• mūlamukhyamaina-
samasta-pekkaṁḍru and their group when they meet at the mukha-maṇḍapa
of the temple of Vīrabhadradēva at Maṭṭipeṁṭa. The grant is stated to
have been made by Pāṁmayyaṁgāru, son of mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
aṁtyaṁbaragaṁḍa Vallabhayyadēva-mahārāju at the request of Suṁkaṁ
Mallana-pekkaṁḍru. Pekkaṁḍru Mallana is stated to have issued this.

inscription.head:
No. 4.
(A.R. No. 4 of 1908.)
GARIKEPĀḌU-AGRAHĀRA, SATTENAPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Circular cup of white marble in a private house.

This Brāhmī inscription, partly embossed and partly engraved may be
assigned to the first century B.C., on grounds of palaeography. It seems
to record that the work (koḍaṁbaṁ) of a rail (sūchilā) was of a merchant
(āpaṇika) named Datta (Data). It mentions that it was his gift.

The first two letters do not form part of the inscription. They were
by mistake chiselled in the wrong way. The sentence too did not convey
the correct idea of the gift it runs.

Dabhaka Apanikasa dānaṁ-The gift of Dabha, the merchant (āpaṇika)
What follows is the correct record and runs.

Dabhasa Āpanikasa sūchikōḍabaṁ-The last word is not clear. Sūchi
is the railing. The translation must be the cup which fits into the top of
(the post in) the railing (a gift) of Dabha, the merchant.

edition.l:
• It is significant that the first letter is engraved while the succeeding letters are in
embossed charters, though the anusvāra in naṁ is engraved. datasa āpaṇikasa dānaṁ [|*] The rest of the record is engraved. datasa āpaṇikasa The form of sU is interesting and is met with in the Bhārhut inscription. sūcilā koḍabaṁ Read koḍaṁbaṁ[||*]

inscription.p: Slab lying in a mound in the Sāhebu Bazaar.

The details of date given in the record viz., Śaka 1055, Āshāḍha ba,
Monday, Dakshiṇāyana-saṅkrānti corresponds to 1133 A.D., June 26.

It records a gift made by Vāsēna, son of Prōle-nāyaka, for the merit
of his father. The nature of gift is not clear.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 92.

l.note: The rest is damaged and illegible.

inscription.p: Another slab lying on a mound in the Sāhebu Bazaar.

This is dated Śaka 1049 which may correspond to 1127 A.D. Other
details of date are not given.

It seems to record the grant of lands for the worship (havibali-
archana) and food-offerings to the deity Trikōṭīśvaradēva of Vellaṁṭūra
on the occasion of Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti. It mentions the sthānapati
Īśānaśiva-paṇḍita who is endowed with a portion of the land.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 85.

inscription.p: On the four faces of a pillar in Sāhebu Bazaar.

This record commences with a praśasti Samasta Guṇagaṇālaṅkāra etc.,
of the king. This record refers to the deity Trikōṭīśvara.
On the four faces of a pillar.

It records a gift of sheep by Tarumbaṟiti Mārāya-nāyaka for
maintaining a lamp to the god in the Trikōṭīśvara temple at Velaṁṭūru,
for the merit of his father Gauri-nāyaka.

The next face contains a number of grants inside the same temple
by different persons:—

inscription.p: Pillar in the Sāhebu Bazaar.
This is dated Śaka 1055 which may correspond to 1133 A.D.

This inscription records the gift of 55 sheep entrusted into the hands
of Gāde-bōya, son of Pēri-śeṭṭi for a perpetual lamp to the deity
Trikōṭīśvaradēva by Vāsi-nāyaka, son of Prōli (Brōli)-nāyaka on the occasion
of Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti. The shepherd was required to measure out
annually a māna of ghee for the lamp.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 94.
Pillar in the Sāhebu Bazaar.
This inscription is dated Śaka 1060 which may correspond to 1138 A.D.

It records the gift of 55 sheep on the day of Vishu-saṅkrānti for
burning a perpetual lamp to the deity Trikōṭēśvaradēva by Koṇḍapa-nāyaka,
son of Maṭṭe-nāyaka, for the merit of his father. The gift was left in
the hands of Eṟeya-Bōya's son (name not clear) who agreed to maintain
the lamp with one māna of ghee daily.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 104.

l.te: caṁ dā¨rkamu nitya māneṁḍu neyi vōyaṁga-

inscription.head:
No. 11.
(A.R. No. 11 of 1908.)
SATTENAPALLE, SATTENAPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the four faces of a pillar in the same place.

The details of date given in the record namely Śaka 1060, Uttarāyaṇa
do not admit for verification.

It records a gift of 55 sheep by Pedda-Koṇḍapa-nāyaka, son of Paṟiti
Nāvi-nāyaka, for maintaing a lamp.

l.te: ṁḍu danapu trānu pauṭhraka mācaṁ dā-

inscription.p: Pillar in the Sāhebu Bazaar.

This inscription is dated in Śaka 1091 which is said to correspond to
1169 A.D.

It records a gift of 55 sheep left in the hands of a shepherd Navedana,
son of Kalle-bōya, for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Trikōṭīśvaradēva on the day of Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti, for the merit of his
parents. The shepherd undertook to supply a māna of ghee daily to maintain
the above endowment.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 169.

l.te: ku sapadana putrānu pau trika mā-

inscription.head:
No. 13.
(A.R. No. 13 of 1908.)
KUMBAKŌṆAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the shrine of the goddess in the Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple.

This incomplete inscription is dated in the 4th year opposite to the
fourth year of the Pāṇḍya king Māṟañjaḍaiyaṉ and records the gift of 138
cows by the king for the supply of milk and ghee to the temple of Bhaṭāra
of Tirukkīḻk-kōṭṭam in Tirukkuḍamukku i.e., Kumbakōṇam. It also records
a gift of 100 kāśu for burning two perpetual lamps in the same temple.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 8.

inscription.head:
No. 14.
(A.R. No. 14 of 1908.)
KUMBAKŌṆAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the Sūryanārāyaṇa shrine in the Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple.

This record commences with the praśasti Tiṅgaḷēr-tarut-ten-toṅgal, etc.,
of the king.

The details of date are Māgha, ba, Wednesday, Āyilya-nakshatra in
the year 36.
The king is credited with the titles Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḷaṉ and Rājakēsari.

It records the grant of several lands as iṟaiyili by the mūlaparuḍai
of Tirukkuḍamukku to the deities Tirukkārōṇam-uḍaiya-mahādēva and
Kaṉṉiyāpiḍāri. This iṟaiyili-pramāṇa was executed when the members
assembled in the inner precinct (uḷḷāḷai) of the temple of Tirukkīḻkōṭṭam-
uḍaiyār.

In this record the king is claimed to have destroyed the city of
Kalyāṇapura and to have performed the annointment of heroes. The
conquests and the titles of king helps us to identify the king with Chōḷa
Rājādhirāja I.

inscription.head:
No. 15.
(A.R. No. 15 of 1908.)
KUMBAKŌṆAM, KUMBAKŌṆAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the Subrahmaṇya shrine in the Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple.

This incomplete and damaged record commences with the praśasti,
Tirumaṉṉi-vaḷara, etc., of the king.

This seems to record a grant for food offerings and lamps during
the three sandhis to the god Chandraśēkaradēva set up by Vēḷāṉ Malli
alias Uṇṇānaṅgai, probably in the temple of Tirukkīḻk-kōṭṭam-uḍaiya-
mahādēva in Tirukkuḍamukku in Pāmbūr-nāḍu in Uy[ya*]koṇḍār-vaḷanāḍu.
Other details are lost as it is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 16.
(A.R. No. 16 of 1908.)
DĀRĀŚURAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Below a belt of sculptures in the walls around the central shrine in
the Airāvatēśvara temple.

These label inscriptions may be assigned to the 12-13th century, on
grounds of palaeography.

These describe the sculptures represented above them which depict
the scenes from the lives of the devotees of god Śiva. Among them, a
few may belong to the group of 63 Śaiva-saints.

For the details of these labels and those that are painted on the outer
gōpura nearby, see A.R.E.p., 1908, part II, para 66-67, pp. 68-69.

l.note:
Besides these there are the following names of Śaiva devotees painted with red
but not cut:
1 cirappuliyāṇṭār |
2 comāci māṟaṉār |
3 eyar koṉ kalikkāmāṇṭār |
4 āḷuṭaiyapiḷḷaiyār |
5 naminanti aṭikaḷ |
6 cey nallūr piḷḷaiyār |
7 tirunāḷaip povār |
8 iḷaiyamāṟaṅkuṭi māṟar |

inscription.head:
No. 17.
(A.R. No. 17 of 1908.)
DĀRĀŚURAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
East wall of the first prākāra in the Airāvatēśvara temple and now
at the right of entrance in the same temple.

This damaged inscription may be assigned on palaeographical grounds
to 13th Century and records the grant of income from taxes on the trees
in the temple gardens deposited in cash in the Rājarājaṉ treasury
(Rājarājaṉ-karuvūgalam) (-Karuvūlagam) for food-offerings to the deity
in the temple. The gardens in the Rājarāja-viḷāgam were stated to have
been destroyed due to some calamity and the revival of the gardens was
arranged for, in a specialised manner with provision of some exemption
(details not clear) in the same area (viḷāgam).

inscription.head:
No. 18.
(A.R. No. 18 of 1908.)
DĀRĀŚURAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the first prākāra in the Airāvatēśvara temple.

This fragmentary inscription commences with the praśasti, Kaḍal-
śūḻnda-pārmādarum, etc., of the king who ascended the throne in c. 1166
A.D.

It seems to record a copy of the order of the king (tirumugappaḍi)
and refers to the king Tribhuvanachchakkaravartti Kō[nēriṉmaikoṇḍāṉ*].
The details of the record are lost.

kaṭal cūḻnta pārmātaṟām pūmātarum kalai

inscription.head:
No. 19.
(A.R. No. 19 of 1908.)
DĀRĀŚURAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the first prākāra in the Airāvatēśvara temple.

This inscription commences with the praśasti, Kaḍal-śūḻnda-
pārmādarum, etc., of Rājādhirāja II.

It refers to an order (tirumugam) issued on the 157th day of the 12th
year of the king.

This inscription is incomplete and damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 20.
(A.R. No. 20 of 1908.)
DĀRĀŚURAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the first prākāra of the Airāvatēśvara temple.

This inscription commences with the praśasti, Puyalvāyttu, etc., of the
Chōḷa king Kulōttuṅga III. The order is dated in the 64th day of the 8th
year of the king.

It records an order of the king issued to his officials Kaḷappāḷarāyaṉ
and Villavadarayaṉ on the application of Maṇalūr Vāduli Ārāmudu Śrī.
•• alias Madhurāntaka-brahmārāyaṉ of Rājarājach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in
Nittavinōda-vaḷanāḍu who in turn ordered the authorities of the temple of
Rājarāja-Īśvaram-uḍaiyār including those in-charge of the temple treasury
called Rājarājaṉ-karuvūgalam (-vūlagam) appointing the jewel-makers in
the above temple and fixing the remuneration to be paid to such people
on a par with the garland-makers (tiruppaḷḷittoṅgal-uḍaiyār) along with a
site (maṉai) as jīvita so that the former could reach home early.

The order was drafted by Neṟiyuḍaich-chōḻa-mūvēndavēḷāṉ and
attested by Nandiyaraiyar, Nuḷambādirāyar, Pāṇḍyarāyar, Vēsāliyarayar,
Virāṭarāyar, Vayirādarāyar, Viśaiyarāyar, Kanakarāyar and Aṉakarāyar.

inscription.head:
No. 21.
(A.R. No. 21 of 1908.)
DĀRĀŚURAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the first prākāra in the Airāvatēśvara temple.

This inscription is assignable to the 14th century on palaeographical
grounds. The details of the date viz., 3rd regnal year and 292nd day are
not sufficient for verification.

It records an order of the king issued by him from his seat called
Kālingarāyaṉ in his palace at Kaṇḍiyūr in Pāṇḍikulapati-vaḷanāḍu in Śōḻa-
maṇḍalam granting twenty vēli of tax-free land in Teṉkuṟukkai alias
Rājanārāyaṇapuram in Tirunaṟai-paṟṟu in Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu as the
capital for conducting the services in the temple of Rājarāja-Īśvaram-uḍaiya-
nāyaṉār at Rājarājapuram, a western hamlet of Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanādu.
This order was signed by Villavarāyaṉ and others.

inscription.head:
No. 22.
(A.R. No. 22 of 1908.)
DĀRĀŚURAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Inner gōpura of the Airāvatēśvara temple, right of entrance.

The details of the date of the record viz., Śaka 1408, the cyclic year
Krōdhana, Makara-saṅkramaṇa which details are not sufficient for
verification.

It records the grant of land in Śividapaṟṟu at Rājarājaśuram (i.e.,
Rājarājēśvaram) to meet the expenses of worship of the deity
Rājarājēśuram-uḍaiya-nā[yaṉā]r also called Tambirāṉār by Ayyaṉ Vīramaraśar.

This chief may be identified with his namesake who figures as a
subordinate of the Vijayanagara king Virūpāksha III, who ruled between
1465 and 1486 A.D.

l.note: This word is expressed by symbols.

l.ta: ṟku pūcaikku ayyaṉ viramācar taṉmamāka rājarāje
l.note: This word is expressed by symbols.

mā •••••

inscription.head:
No. 23.
(A.R. No. 23 of 1908.)
DĀRĀŚURAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Inner gōpura of the Airāvatēśvara temple, right of entrance.

This incomplete epigraph may palaeographically be assigned to
13-14th century. The portion referring to the regnal year is damaged. The
extant details of date are the fourteenth day of the month of Tai of a
regnal year (not clear).

It states that the nāṭṭavar of Ūṟṟattūr met at the temple of Rājarāja-
Īśuram and agreed to provide for the services and festivals in the temple
from out of the income from taxes collected. The tax collectors were
empowered to collect the toll tax of half a paṇam per head; tax on cattle,
one palam, per head; tax on head-load, half a paṇam per load; tax on import
and export; 5 per cent and the like or else to break open the locked doors
of the houses and to destroy and confiscate the vessels therein. Their
remuneration is stated to have been specified.

nā[ṭ]ṭavarom irā rā

inscription.head:
No. 24.
(A.R. No. 24 of 1908.)
DĀRĀŚURAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Below the image of a dvārapālaka set up close to the inner gōpura
of the Airāvatēśvara temple, left of entrance.

This label inscription in characters of c. 11th century states that this
image (dvārapālaka image) was brought by the king from Kalyāṇapuram
after sacking it.
(cf: K.A.N. Sastri: Cōḷas, Vol. I, p. 275 n).

inscription.head:
No. 25.
(A.R. No. 25 of 1908.)
DĀRĀŚURAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Above the empty dēvakōshṭhas on the walls of the outer gōpura of
the Airāvatēśvara temple.

These small label inscriptions assignable to about the 12th Century
contain the names of the gods and goddesses whose images have been
installed therein.

edition.l: brahmāṇī Read brāhmaṇi [|*]
patmanidhi Read padmanidhi||-

inscription.head:
No. 26.
(A.R. No. 26 of 1908.)
DĀRĀŚURAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On two stones lying in the court-yard of the Airāvatēśvara temple.

These two pieces of stone are of one and the same record of the
king. It contains a belittle portion of the praśasti commencing with Pugaḻ-
śūḻnda-puṇari, etc.

It refers to a transaction of the peruṅguṟi-mahāsabhai of a village
(name lost) and to the boundaries of land, the details of which are lost.

l.ta: ⎯⎯ p paṭuttu maṟṟavar tammai vanacarar tiriyu—

edition.l: ⎯⎯ ṭuttuk koṇṭu End is lost.—

niṟaṉ—

inscription.head:
No. 27.
(A.R. No. 27 of 1908.)
DĀRĀŚURAM, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone lying in the court-yard of the Airāvatēśvara temple.

This epigraph palaeographically assignable to c. 14th-15th Century
refers to a gōpura called Īgai-mūvēndiraiyar tiruk-kōpuram, probably
of the above temple.

inscription.head:
No. 28.
(A.R. No. 28 of 1908.)
TIRUPPANANDĀḶ, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the first prākāra of the Aruṇajaṭēśvarasvāmi temple.

This record commences with the praśasti of the king,
Pūmaruviya-poḻil-ēḻum, etc.

It records the sale of 375 vēli of wet and dry lands by Śēndaṉ
Kūttāḍuvāṉ alias Rājarāja-vaṅgāra-muttaraiyaṉ, of Peṇṇāgaḍam alias
Muḍigoṇḍaśōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya village in
Virudarājabhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu to one Aḻiyāviradaṅkaṇḍār-Tīyilumpiriyār
alias Kuṅgiliyakkalaiyar, for a value of 1000 aṉṟāḍu-naṟkāśu. The land,
thus sold, is mentioned to be in possession of the seller since it was purchased
in his name by his father Sēndar alias Vāṇakularāyar from one Vāḷavarāyaṉ
of Kīḻkarai-Kāṭṭuppaḷḷi of the same nāḍu. The purchaser is referred to
here as a worshipper of the famous Śaiva saint Tirukkaḍavūr-
Kuṅgiliyakkalaiyāṇḍār, who is described to have prepared the deity
Tiruttāṭakēśvaram-uḍaiyār at Tiruppanandāḷ in Maṇṇi-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 29.
(A.R. No. 29 of 1908.)
GAṄGAIKOṆḌACHŌḶAPURAM, UDAIYARPALAIYAM TALUK,
TIRUCHCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the central shrine in the Bṛihadīśvara temple.

This damaged and incomplete inscription contains parts of the kingds
praśasti beginning with Pugaḻ-śūḻnda-puṇari, etc.

The first of the three verses in Sanskrit language and written in
Grantha characters invoke the goddess Śrī (i.e., Lakshmī), while the other
two verses trace the genealogy of the Gāhaḍavāla kings of Kanauj viz.,
Yaśōvigraha, Mahīchandra and śrī Chandradēva.

edition.l:

••• ••• •••• Restore ‘pāramakrupāra pārē vyāpāritam’ sāpāritamyaśaḥ May also be read as u yaśā [7ḥ][||*]
tasyābhūttanayō [nayaikarasikaḥ*] krāntañciṣa nmaṇḍalō
viddhastō ddhatadhīrayōdhatimiraḥ śrī candra [dē]vōnṛpa[ḥ*]
[|*] Read ‘katutōº’

inscription.head:
No. 30.
(A.R. No. 30 of 1908.)
GAṄGAIKOṆḌACHŌḶAPURAM, UDAIYARPALAIYAM TALUK,
TIRUCHCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the central shrine in the Bṛihadīśvara temple.

The details of the date of the record except for the Śaka and cyclic
years, are lost as it is damaged.

This highly damaged record seems to register a grant of land as
māṉṉiyam. It refers to one Kachchirāyaṉ and the village Vaḻudilampaṭṭu.
All other details are lost.

edition.p: 1. The rest of the inscription is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 31.
(A.R. No. 31 of 1908.)
GAṄGAIKOṆḌACHŌḶAPURAM, UDAIYARPALAIYAM TALUK,
TIRUCHCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Bṛihadīśvara temple.

This inscription may palaeographically be assigned to c. 13th century.
The details are lost as it is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 31A.
(A.R. No. 31A of 1908.)
GAṄGAIKOṆḌACHŌḶAPURAM, UDAIYARPALAIYAM TALUK,
TIRUCHCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Bṛihadīśvara temple.

This damaged inscription This record is engraved below (A.R. No. 31 of 1908).may be assigned to c.13th century on
palaeographical grounds.

It records an order of the King (name lost) granting lands probably
in Gaggaikoṇḍachōḷapuram in Mēṟka-nāḍu, for worship, a sandhi named
after king Sundara-Pāṇḍya An inscription of Sundara-Pāṇḍya from the same place refers to a sandhi named
after the king instituted to the deity GaggaikoṇḍachōḷIśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉAr of
Gaggaikoṇḍachōḷapuram in his 2nd year (See S.I.I. Vol. IV, No. 524)., festivals etc., to a deity (name lost). The order
was issued to the variyilār making the gift land as tax-free (iṟaiyili) from
the month of Chittirai in his 4th regnal year. The document was attested
by Tuñjalūr-uḍaiyāṉ.

edition.p: 1. The ends of the lines are built in.
2. Rest of the portion of the record is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 32.
(A.R. No. 32 of 1908.)
GAṄGAIKOṆḌACHŌḶAPURAM, UDAIYARPALAIYAM TALUK,
TIRUCHCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Bṛihadīśvara temple.

This incomplete record commences with the praśasti Pugaḻ vā[y*]ttu
vaḷam-peruga, etc., of the king, of which only portions can be made out.

It records an order of the king issued on the 160th day of the thirteenth
year of his reign. It purports to be a copy of the king's order in connection
with some transaction of a dēvadāna land. Mentions the sub-division
Karavaḷa-nāḍu.

l.ta:
puyal vā[y]ttu vaḷam perukap poyyāta nāṉmaṟaiyiṉ
ceyal vā[y]ppa tirumaka(ḷ)ḷum ceyamakaḷum ciṟaṟ^1^⎯⎯

yum maṭamāṉu[m] vaṉtorutuṟaiṉiruṇṇak koḻ

inscription.head:
No. 33.
(A.R. No. 33 of 1908.)
GAṄGAIKOṆḌACHŌḶAPURAM, UDAIYARPALAIYAM TALUK,
TIRUCHCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Bṛihadīśvara temple.

This damaged inscription may be assigned to the 12th-13th century
on palaeographical grounds. The extant details of date available is 152nd
day of the king's reign period.

It records an order of the king issued to the priests (mūlappaṭṭuḍaip-
pañchāchārya)

inscription.head:
No. 34.
(A.R. No. 34 of 1908.)
GAṄGAIKOṆḌACHŌḶAPURAM, UDAIYARPALAIYAM TALUK,
TIRUCHCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
East wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Bṛihadīśvara temple.

This fragmentary inscription may be assigned on palaeographical
grounds to the 12th century. Except for the tithi and the week-day viz.,
trayōdaśi and Monday, the other details of date are lost.

It seems to record a gift of some villages, which were originally made
as dēvadāna, as tax-free (iṟaiyili) along with its income from taxes from
the date of the record. All other details are lost.

edition.l: svastiśrīḥ [||*] śrīmat kittikku mel tribhuvaṉac The ends of the lines are mutilated.⎯⎯

l.ta:
uḷḷiṭṭa ūrkaḷ iṉṉāḷ mutal mutalaṭaṅka iṟaiyi⎯⎯
⎯⎯

pūvum karuppum kamukum ceṅkaḻinirum vāḻai koḻuntu •
••• ••• •••• kku vaḻutalai⎯⎯

ṟai taṭṭoli taṭṭārappapāṭṭam veṭṭippāṭṭam i
••• ••• •••• ṭṭut tevaikaḷ uṭ⎯⎯

happeṟu maṟṟum eṟperppaṭṭa uḷ
••• ••• •••• iṉṉāḷ [mutali] muta •••

inscription.p:
One of the pillars in the Kambattaḍi-maṇḍapa in the Sundarēśvara
temple.

This is dated in the month of Pushya of the cyclic year Svabhānu
in the Śaka year 1505 which may correspond to January 1583 A.D.

It states that Vīrappa-nāyaḍu, son of Kṛishṇappa-nāyani caused to be
constructed a maṇḍapa, probably the Kambattaḍi-maṇḍapa, wherein the
inscription is found engraved on one of the pillars.

inscription.head:
No. 36.
(A.R. No. 36 of 1908.)
MADURAI, MADURAI TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up in front of the Perumāḷ temple.

This damaged and incomplete inscription gives the details of date viz.,
Śaka 1523 (mistake for 1524), Śubhakṛit, Mēsha, śu 13 are not sufficient
for verification.

It seems to record some shares of lands from different villages
purchased from several Vaishṇava individuals in favour of the lineage of
the jīyar serving the Bhāsyakārar (i.e., Rāmānuja) in the Madanagōpālan-
māḍat-tiru-vīdi in Madurai. The other details are lost.

edition.l: yūr • ti • tayaṅkāṟkku- kku ••• ••• ••••

l.note: The rest of the inscription is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 37.
(A.R. No. 37 of 1908.)
TIRUPPARAṄKUṈṞAM, MADURAI TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On one of the pillars in the rock-cut cave.

This Vaṭṭeḻuttu inscription is engraved in archaic characters of 8th
century A.D has been attributed to Jaṭila Parāntaka of the Āṉaimalai
inscription dated Kali 3871 (see S.I.I., Vol. XIV, Nos 1 and 2, Ep. Ind., Vol.
VIII, pp. 317 ff).

It records the construction of the Tirukkōyil (shrine) and of the Śrī-
taṭākam (tank) by Śāttaṉ Gaṇapati, a resident of Karavandapura who is
known as Pāṇḍi-Amṛitamaṅgalavaraiyaṉ, and is stated to have belonged to
the Vaidya family and to have been the mahāsāmanta of the king. The
shrines of Durgādēvī and Jyēshṭhādēvī were constructed by Nakkaṉ-koṟṟi,
who is described as the Dharmapatni, probably of this mahāsāmanta.

This general who was also called Sāmanta Bhīma and an important
member of the Vaidya family (vai(j)ya-mukhya) is stated to have excavated
a temple for Śaṁbhu and to have consecreated the image of the god in
the Kali year 3874 (773 A.D.). If this Śiva temple referred to in an inscription
from Tirupparaṅkuṉṟam (A.R.Ep., 1951-52, No. B. 143) and the tirukkōyil
(temple) stated to have been excavated by Śattaṉ Gaṇapati are identical,
as it is not unlikely, the date of the latter event, viz., the 6th year of
Māṟañjaḍaiyaṉ, must be equated with 773 A.D., the date of the former event.
This gives us the date of his accession as 768 A.D.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 3.

inscription.head:
No. 38.
(A.R. No. 38 of 1908.)
VARICHCHIYŪR, MADURAI TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a rock overhanging the Pañchapāṇḍavar-paḍukkai.

There are three fragmentary inscriptions engraved on this cave. They
record the beds to be carved. These inscriptions may palaeographically be
assigned to c. 2nd century B.C.
The first inscription reads ••••• paḷiy-koṭupi •••••

This refers to the gift of a paḷḷi (cave). The second inscription reads
•••• nūṟu kāchāṉa meaning the gift of one hundred kārshāpaṇa coins.

The third inscription reads ••••• nataṉ āk-iya nāllu irukai meaning
the four beds (seats) were caused to be made by ••••• nataṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 39.
(A.R. No. 39 of 1908.)
VARICHCHIYŪR, MADURAI TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a rocky outcrop of the village Kuṉṉattūr, three furlongs south of
the village.

This undated inscription can be palaeographically assigned to 15th
century A.D. It seems to record a gift of three villages viz., Ma[lai]-kkuḍi,
Palāṅguḷam and Añ[ja]ru as dēvadāna to the deity Vīrabhadra-nāyaṉār, by
Vīramarasayya, the kaṟtar of Madurai-maṇḍalam, for the merit of Nāgaṇa-
nāyakkar.

Vīramarasayya of this record is evidently identical with Ayyaṉ
Vīramarasa figuring in a record from Dārāśuram. (A.R.Ep., No. 22 of 1908)

l.note: Rest of the record is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 40.
(A.R. No. 40 of 1908.)
VĪRAŚIKHĀMAṆI, SANKARANAYINAR KOVIL TALUK,
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar of the rock-cut cave temple.

This damaged inscription seems to record a gift of land for maintaining
a lamp in the temple of Kayilāyattu-Perumāṉaḍigaḷ at Vīravinōda-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Kallaga-nāḍu in Muḍigoṇḍaśōḻa-
vaḷanāḍu.

The donor's name is not clear. However, from the extant portion
of the record, it may be said that the above gift was made by Maṇḍai
••• śōlai Śāttaṉ of the above village. The names of the Śivabrāhmaṇas,
who are probably to carry out this endowment are given.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 136.

l.note: The record is damaged in several places.

inscription.head:
No. 41.
(A.R. No. 41 of 1908.)
VĪRAŚIKHĀMAṆI, SANKARANAYINAR KOVIL TALUK,
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On one of the beds in the Pañchapāṇḍavar-paḍukkai on the hill.

This inscription is engraved in Grantha characters of about the 9th-
10th century and its language is an admixture of Sanskrit and Prākṛit.
It records an anāśana-vrata (fasting) evidently of some jaina monk.

l.note: The ends of the lines are badly mutilated.
This letter stands for the Sanskrit ‘puṇyam’ •

inscription.head:
No. 42.
(A.R. No. 42 of 1908.)
VĪRAŚIKHĀMAṆI, SANKARANAYINAR KOVIL TALUK,
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Near the foot-prints engraved on the hill.

This epigraph engraved in characters of about the 14th century A.D.,
records that the foot-prints of Śrī Sakasāṉandar (i.e., śrī Sahajānanda) were
caused to be carved by a certain Avaiyampukkāṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 43.
(A.R. No. 43 of 1908.)
KAḺUGUMALAI, MELUR TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab in the Kośakkuḍi street.

This record refers to an expedition of the Pāṇḍya army against
Śaḍaiyaṉ-Karunandaṉ of Malai-nāḍu, in which the fort of Arivāyūr was
destroyed. This Śaḍaiyaṉ-Karunandaṉ is identical with the Āy chieftain
whose records are found in the northern part of the Travancore region.
He may have originally been a Pāṇḍya vassal as indicated by the surname
Śaḍaiyaṉ and may have subsequently rebelled against the Pāṇḍya in
conjunction with the Vēṇāḍu ruler of this period, which action was probably
the cause of the expedition referred to in the present inscription. Two
soldiers of the battalion commanded by the Pāṇḍya subordinate Eṭṭi-Maṇṇaṉ
alias Maṅgala-Ēṉādi of Perunechchuṟam, named Viṉaiyantoḻu-śūraṉ of
Pūndaṉmali in Toṇḍai-nāḍu and Śāttaṉakkaṉ of Pēreyiṟkuḍi lost their lives
in this fight, and in commemoration of their services, some land valued
at 20 kaḻañju of gold was given apparently by this Ēṉādi. This endowment,
which was made over to the sabhai of Kumaramaṅgalam, may have been
in the nature of an udirappaṭṭi.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 31.

inscription.head:
No. 44.
(A.R. No. 44 of 1908.)
MĒṬṬUPAṬṬI, NILAKKOTTAI TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North, South and East walls of a temple on the hill.
The inscription is dated Śaka 1410, Kīlaka and Tai.

It records a gift of lands as dēvadāna to the deity Siddhamalai-nāyiṉār
by a certain Timmāchchi-nāyakkar Tirumalaiyaṉ of Toṇḍai-maṇḍalam. The
gift land is said to be in Kariyappaṉkōṭṭai which was situated in Puṟamalai
Kallaga-nāḍu. The donor is said to have obtained the same as a private
property (svaya-gata-dānam) from his overlord Mahābali-Bāṇādhirājar.

It may be said that this Bāṇa chief belongs to the later Bāṇa family
who were governing from Madurai.

l.ta:
toṇṭaimaṇṭalam timmācci
nāyakkar tirumalaiyaneṉ nāyiṉāṟ cittamalai uṭaiyāṟku
kuṭutta tevatāṉamāvatu puṟamalai kallakaṉā l eṉakku

inscription.head:
No. 45.
(A.R. No. 45 of 1908.)
MĒṬṬUPAṬṬI, NILAKKOTTAI TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the beds in the Pañchapāṇḍavar-paḍukkai in a cavern on the hill.

There are three fragmentary inscriptions which may palaeographically
be assigned to 2nd century B.C.

The first inscription reads chaiy-aḷaṉ vintai-ūr kāvi-y meaning the
cavern of Chaiyaḷaṉ (i.e., one form Ceylon) of Vintaiyūr. The second
inscription reads nākapērūr = atai chāttaṉ makaṉ meaning the son of
Chāttaṉ Atai of Nākapērūr.

The third inscription reads ••••• ti ••••• kkāttāṉ ma ••••• ṉam ••
ly ••••• This is a fragmentary and badly managed inscription.

Besides the above, there are nine other fragmentary inscriptions here.
They can be assigned on palaeographical grounds to c. 2nd century B.C.

The first inscription reads: antai ariyti meaning a personal name Antai
Ariyti.

The second inscription reads: antai iravātaṉ meaning a personal name
Antai Iravātaṉ.

The third inscription reads: matira antai visuvaṉ meaning Antai Visuvaṉ
of Matirai.

The fourth record reads: antai chenta-a-tāṉa meaning the gift of Antai
Chēntaṉ.

The fifth inscription reads: chantaitai chantaṉ meaning a personal name
Chantantai Chantaṉ.

The sixth record reads: patiṉ-ūr atai meaning a person A[n*]tai of
Patiṉūr.

The seventh inscription reads: kuvira antai chey-a tāṉa meaning the
gift made by Kuvira Antai.

The eighth inscription reads: kuvira antai vēḷ-a tāṉa meaning the gift
of Kuvira Antai, a vēḷ

The ninth record reads: tiṭi-il-a-tāṉa meaning the gift of (the people
of) Tiṭi-il.

inscription.head:
No. 46.
(A.R. No. 46 of 1908.)
MĒṬṬUPAṬṬI, NILAKKOTTAI TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the cavern containing the Pañchapāṇḍavar paḍukkai on the hill.
This inscription in characters of 14th century reads Tripurādēviyār.

inscription.head:
No. 47.
(A.R. No. 47 of 1908.)
MĒṬṬUPAṬṬI, NILAKKOTTAI TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the cavern containing the Pañchapāṇḍavar paḍukkai.

This inscription in characters of about the 14th century states that the
foot-prints of Sahajānandanādar were caused to be carved by
Selvajñānasambandar belonging to the merchant community of
Vikramaśōḻapuram (cf. No. 42 of 1908)

inscription.head:
No. 48.
(A.R. No. 48 of 1908.)
TIRUPPAṬṬŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Aiyaṉār
temple.

This inscription commences with the praśasti, Pūmēvu-vaḷar tiruppoṉ,
etc of the king.

It records a gift of four cows and a kuttuviḷakku weighing 40 (palam ?)
for maintaining one-eighth of a lamp for a sandhi in front of the deity
Piḷḷaiyāṟ (i.e., Aiyaṉār) of Tiruppiḍavūr, a brahmadēya in Tiruppiḍavūr-nāḍu,
a division of Tribhuvamamuḻuduḍai-vaḷanāḍu by a goldsmith (taṭṭāṉ) named
Gaṇapati alias Tiruchchiṟṟambalaperuntaṭṭāṉ, a nānādēśip-peruntaṭṭāṉ of
Gaṅgaikoṇḍachōḻapuram. The record was engraved by one Dāsan
Viḻikoṇḍān Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-uḍaiyāṉ of Pāmburam in Pāmbōr-nāḍu.

The goldsmith who figures as a donor in this record must have
belonged to a merchant guild called nānādēśi.

inscription.head:
No. 49.
(A.R. No. 49 of 1908.)
TIRUPPAṬṬŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Right wall of the entrance of the gōpura in the Aiyaṉār temple.

This epigraph engraved in characters of about the 11th century A.D.
mentions the name Araiyaṉ Rāja [Rā]jaṉ alias Madurāntaka Iḷaṅgōvēḷāṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 50.
(A.R. No. 50 of 1908.)
TIRUPPAṬṬŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Left wall of the entrance of the gōpura in the Aiyaṉār temple.

This inscription engraved in characters of about the 13th century A.D,
states that this gōpura was caused to be constructed by Teṟṟi Ādittaṉ alias
Ottaga[ne]rittap-pichchaṉ of Tiru Āyappāḍi-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 51.
(A.R. No. 51 of 1908.)
VAḺUVŪR, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Brahmapurīśvara temple.

The inscription commences with the praśasti, Maduraiyum Pāṇḍiyaṉ-
muḍit-talaiyuṅ-koṇḍ-aruḷiya etc., of the king.

It records a gift of six sandhi-lamps to the deity Tiruvayaṉīśuram-
uḍaiya-nāyaṉār of Vaḻugūr in Irumbāḍu-nāḍu in Veṇkuṉṟak-kōṭṭam in
Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam by Kālakāladēva, son of Umā, a dēvaraḍiyār of
the temple. The Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple received 18 paḻaṅkāśu,
evidently from Kāla-kāladēva himself, for maintaining the above
endowment.

l.ta:
[tiripuvaṉaccakkaravarttikaḷ] maturaiyum

coḻa

inscription.head:
No. 52.
(A.R. No. 52 of 1908.)
VAḺUVŪR, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Brahmapurīśvara temple.

This record may be assigned on c. pelaeographical grounds to 13th
Century.

It refers to an order (nirupam) issued to the tāṉattār of Vaḻugūr in
Chaḷukkippaṟṟu by the Gāṅgayar to meet the expenses of worship and
services to the debty Tiruv-ayaṉīśuram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār from out of the
income from the sarvamānya-iṟaiyili lands granted in the same village. Also
it mentions that the worship was not conducted properly and this
necessitated Gāṅgayar to purchase the lands as tirunāmattukkāṇi and assign
it to the god.

l.note: The intended word may be caḷukki

l.ta:
nirupam taṅkaḷūr poṉṉi ental iṉṉāḷvaraiyum
āḷuṭaiyār tiruvaya[ṉi] curamuṭaiyaṉāyaṉāṟkut ti[rup]-

l.note: This stands for an auspicious term ‘śivam’

inscription.head:
No. 53.
(A.R. No. 53 of 1908.)
VAḺUVŪR, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the South wall of the central shrine in the Brahmapurīśvara temple.

This incomplete record may be assigned on palaeographical grounds
to c. 14th century A.D.

The details of date viz., Pramōda, Aṟpaśi 27 are insufficient for
verification.

The record states that a sum of 25 paṇam was deposited into the
treasury in lieu of 685 kuḻi of different dēvadāna lands sold to
Ēṉāttaraipiḷḷai alias Sembiyadaraiyar and Poṉṉāṅkaṭṭi Kāṅgēyar.

tiruvāykkeḻvi muṉṉāka śrīvira viruppaṇṇa uṭaiyāṟku
cellāniṉṟa piṟa[m*]o tūta

irupattu añcu paṇam kuṭukka veṇṭiṉapaṭiyāle
innilam •••

inscription.head:
No. 54.
(A.R. No. 54 of 1908.)
VAḺUVŪR, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North, West and South walls of the central shrine in the
Brahmapurīśvara temple.

The record may be assigned on palaeographical grounds to about 13th
century. The details of date given viz., year 5, Tulā, śu 3, Saturday, Anusha-
nakshatra cannot be tallied in the absence of identification of the king.

It records the grant of lands, etc in the village Śāttaṉūr in Irumbēḍu-
nāḍu in Veṇkuṉṟak-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam by the nāṭṭavar
of Śāḷukki-paṟṟu as tirunāmattak-kāṇi-iṟaiyili to the deity Tiru-Ayaṉīśuram-
uḍaiya-nāyaṉār of Śōḻanallūr belonging to the same nāḍu. The kāṇi lands
of the village Śāttaṉūr were once sold as Ta(Cha)ṇḍēśura-peruvilai. It is
stated that for a long period they were left uncared in the absence of their
kāṇiyāḷar (ivvūr kāṇiyāḷar illāmal pulattalaiyāyk-kiḍaikkaiyil).

l.ta:

inscription.head:
No. 55.
(A.R. No. 55 of 1908.)
VAḺUVŪR, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
East wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Brahmapurīśvara temple.
This epigraph is in characters of thirteenth century.

It records the gift of 22(1/2) kāśu by the śivabrāhmaṇas (names
mentioned) of the temple who obtained from Āṇḍār, Poṉṉambalak-kūttar,
a devotee of Vaḻugūr for maintaining 4(1/2) sandhi lamps in the temple of
Āḷuḍaiyār Tiruvayanīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār of Vaḻuvūr in Irumbēḍu-nāḍu
in Veṇkuṉṟak-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḷa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
veṇkuṉṟak koṭṭattu irumpeṭu nāṭṭu vaḻukūr
āḷuṭaiyār tiruvayani

ṭṭaṉ peruntirukkoyi
luṭaiyāṉu mavan tampi tiruvekampa-

varai erikkakkaṭavomil muvom
kākku nāyakapaṭṭaṉ tiru-

yum ivvūr niyāya •• karil āṇṭār poṉṉampala
kūttar tanmam [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 56.
(A.R. No. 56 of 1908.)
VAḺUVŪR, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
East wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Brahmapurīśvara temple.

This incomplete inscription may be assigned to c. 12th century A.D
on palaeographical grounds.

It records the gift of 150 kalam of paddy by Tuṇavar, son of
Āṟpākkiḻāṉ Iḷaiyāḻvār, a native of Āṟpākkam in Māgaṟal-nāḍu in Kāliyūrk-
kōṭṭam in the same maṇḍalam for the tiruppaḷḷi-eḻuchchi service to the
deity Ayanīśvaram-uḍaiyār. The śivabr-hmaṇas of the temple of Tiru
Ayanīśvaram-uḍaiyār at Vaḻugūr in Irumbīḍu-nāḍu in Veṇkuṉṟak-kōṭṭam
in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam undertook to provide food-offerings lamp, etc.,
to the deity at the early morning service (tiruppaḷḷi-eḻuchchi).

l.ta:
vatu jayaṅ
koṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu veṇkuṉṟak ko-

ṭalattuk kāliyūrkkoṭṭattu mā[ka]ṟal nāṭṭu
āṟpākkattu āṟpākkiḻāṉ iḷaiyāḻvār makaṉ tuṇaivar
pakkal nāṅ-

inscription.head:
No. 57.
(A.R. No. 57 of 1908.)
VAḺUVŪR, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Brahmapurīśvara temple.

This epigraph may be assigned on palaeographical grounds to about
the 13th century A.D.

It records the gift of 32 cows and a bull by Śeṅgēṇi Vīrapperumāḷ
alias Kulōttuṅga-Śambuvarāyaṉ for maintaining a perpetual lamp to the
deity Tiruvayanīśuram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār of Vaḻugūr in Veṇkuṉṟak-kōṭṭam in
Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam. The maṉṟāḍis Periyanāṭṭukkōṉ, Ayanīśurakkōṉ
and Parambakkōṉ undertook the gift and agreed to maintain the lamp by
the supply of ghee measured by Rājakēsari-uḻakku.

inscription.head:
No. 58.
(A.R. No. 58 of 1908.)
VAḺUVŪR, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Brahmapurīśvara temple.

This epigraph may be assigned on palaeographical grounds to about
the 12th century A.D.

It records the gift of paddy from out of the dēvadāṉa lands like
Poṉṉiēndal at Puravarinallūr, the income from taxes such as śīkāriyappēṟu,
kāttigai-kāśu, taṟiiṟai from the maḍaiviḷāgam lands, etc., for food-offerings
and services (tiruppaṇi) to the deity Tiruvayaṉīchcharam-uḍaiyār of
Vaḻugūr in Irambēḍu-nāḍu in Veṇkuṉṟak-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-
maṇḍalam by Śeṅgēṇi Vīrarākkidas-śambuvarāya, who styles himself ‘a
Pallavaṉ’.

l.ta:

inscription.head:
No. 59.
(A.R. No. 59 of 1908.)
VAḺUVŪR, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Brahmapurīśvara temple.
This inscription is in characters of about the 13th century A.D.

It records the gift of three paḻaṅ-kāśu by Puttuḻāṉ Periyāṉ Śambu-
varāyap-pallavaraiyaṉ for maintaining a perpetual lamp to the deity
Tiruvayanīśuram-uḍaiyār of Vaḻugūr. The Śivabrāhmaṇas undertook to
maintain the endowment from out of three kāśu received from the donor.

l.ta:
nicuramuṭaiyāṟku puttūḻāṉ periyāṉ campuvarāyap
pallavaraiyaṉ vaitta cantiviḷakku

ṇar kaikkoṇṭa
paḻaṅkācu poṉ ippaḻaṅkācu muṉṟuṅ ko-

ṇṭu ivviḷakku oṉṟum cantirātittavarai erikka kaṭavom
ikkoyil

inscription.head:
No. 60.
(A.R. No. 60 of 1908.)
VAḺUVŪR, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in
the Brahmapurīśvara temple.

This inscription records the gift of dēvadana lands at Puravarāinallūr
for lamps to the deity Tiruvayaṉīśuram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār of Vaḻugūr in
Irumbēḍu-nāḍu in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśoḻa-maṇḍalam by the nyāyattār including
Kuṟuśēkaṟa Kaḷappāḷa Sāvakanāyaṉ Ammaiappasāvakan.

group.h3:
No. 61A-61B.
(A.R. No. 61 of 1908.)
VAḺUVŪR, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
group.p:
West wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Brahmapurīśvara temple.

p.b:
[Viruppaṇṇa] (?) A. Prabhava: 1387 A.D.
B. Pramōdūta: 1390 A.D.

group.p:
The inscription nos. A and B may palaeographically be assigned to
c. 14th century. No. A furnishes the details of date viz., Prabhava, Chittirai
20 which are insufficient for verification.

It records an order (ōlai) of the king issued to Ēṉāttan Piḷḷaiyāṉ alias
Sembiyadaraiyaṉ and Poṉṉāṅkaṭṭi Kāṅgēyarāyar of Vaḻugūr for the villages
Ponniēṉdal and Kīḻai-nattamēḍu made over as sarvamānya-iṟaiyili.

inscription.head: No. 61A.

iṟaiyili

yāka kāṇi ā[kki]k kallum veṭṭi rāja

inscription.head:
No. 62.
(A.R. No. 62 of 1908.)
VAḺUVŪR, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Brahmapurīśvara temple.

p.b: Bhūpati-uḍaiyar, son of Bokkaṇa-uḍaiyar Śaka 1332: 1410 A.D.

inscription.p:
This record belongs to the reign period of Bhūpati-uḍaiyar who is in
all probability be identical with Vijaya Bhūpatirāyar of another record from
this place (A.R. No. 63 of 1908) or Vīra-Bhūpati-uḍaiyar of Veḍāl record
(A.R. No. 75 of 1908) The details of date given viz., Śaka 1332, Vikṛiti, Kanni
ba 8, Monday may correspond to 1410 A.D, September 22, but the tithi
was Navami.

It registers the kāṇivilai-pramāṇam issued in the name of Ādichaṇḍēśvara
by two persons Ēṉādaṉ Virayaṉ Sembiyadaraiyaṉ and Maṇḍalapurushaṉ
(Tāya)dēvaṉ, for the lands excluding the dēvadāṉa portion in the area called
Kaḻudaimēṭṭuk-kollai at the kāṇi-āṭchi village Puravarai-nallūr to the
tānattār and kaikkōḷar of the village Vaḻugūr alias Kalikaḍiṉda-śōḻanallūr
in Irumbēḍu-nāḍu in Veṇkuṉṟa-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, for
a price value of 35 vāśipaḍā-naṟ-paṇam deducting the value from out of
kaḍamai for the cultivation made in the year Virōdhi. It also states that
they have agreed to pay the taxes for these lands and the tirunāmattukkāṇi
lands of the deity. This land transaction executed at the request of Maṉṟār
Vaṇikkar of Vaḻugūr was attested by a number of signatories.

ttu nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu

inscription.head:
No. 63.
(A.R. No. 63 of 1908.)
VAḺUVŪR, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Brahmapurīśvara temple.

p.b:
Śirigirinātha-uḍaiyār, son of Vijaya Bhūpati-rāyar
Śaka 1348 = 1425-26 A.D.

inscription.p:
This epigraph belongs to the reign period of Bhūpati-uḍaiyar who is
in all probability be identical with Vijaya Bhūpatirāyar of another
Śirigirinātha-uḍaiyār who is probably identical with Śrīgiri of the Madras
Museum Plates (Ep. Ind., Vol. VIII, pp 306 ff). It furnishes the date Śaka
1346 (1424 A.D).

The present record furnishes the details of date viz., Śaka 1348,
Viśvāvaśu, Makara śu 13, Monday, Tiruvādirai-nakshatra which regularly
correspond to 1426 A.D., January 21.

It records the gift of income from various taxes (āyam) from kaikkōḷar,
vāṇiyar, immigrant tenants, paḻavari and puduvari accrued from the vāśal,
ubayaṅ-kaṭṭuk-kuttagai of 70 paṇam per year, 10 paṇam for the clothes
(puḍavai), chekkupaṭṭai āyam and the amount collected from the market
(śandai) for maintaining perpetual lamp to the deity Tiruvayanīśuram-
uḍaiya-nāyaṇār of Vaḻugūr alias Kali[kaḍinda]śōḻanallūr in Irumbēḍu-nāḍu
in Veṇguṉṟak-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam by Mallarasa[r*].
Mentions ayaṉ Poṟkōyil-uḍaiyār at the end of the record.

pūpatirāyar kumāra cirikirināta uṭaiyaṟku cellāniṉṟa

inscription.head:
No. 64.
(A.R. No. 64 of 1908.)
VAḺUVŪR, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Kāmarasavalli-ammaṉ shrine
in the Brahmapurīśvara temple.

The details of date viz., śaka 14(90), Vibhava, Siṁha, śu 10, Monday,
Tiruvōṇam may be equated with 1568 A.D., August 2, Monday. However,
the nakshatra was Jyēshṭha.

This highly damaged inscription seems to record some gift for the
śrībali offerings, lamps etc., to the deity Tiru Ayanīśurattu-nāyaṉar of
Vaḻugūr probably by Śūrappa-nāyakkar, who is described as the agent
(karttar) of Kṛishṇappa-nāyakkar.

l.ta:
śrī maṉmakāmaṇṭalecura catācivateva
makārāyar piṟutivirācciyam paṇṇi aruḷāniṉṟa

itaṉmel cellāniṉṟa vipava
caṅvaṟcarattu ciṅkanāyaṟṟu pūṟuvapakṣattu
k[ku]m ••• ••• •••• ttukkum oru tiru viḷakku kkum

inscription.head:
No. 65.
(A.R. No. 65 of 1908.)
VAḺUVŪR, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the prākāra of the Brahmapurīśvara temple.

This damaged inscription may be assigned on palaeographical grounds
to the 14th century.

It seems to record the fixation of taxes on loom (taṟik-kaḍamai),
vāśaival-āyam and ūr-viniyōgam evidently payable to the temple by those
who had settled in the various streets at Vaḻugūr.

An incomplete record found below this contains some of the epithets
of the Vijayanagara king.

l.ta:
āyam araiye araikkā[l] taṟi viḻukkāṭu koḷḷak
kaṭavatu ākavum im(ma)-

koṉṟa pāvattile povaṉ cantiṟātittavaraiyum
naṭakkakkaṭavatu •• kacci araikku va •••

inscription.head:
No. 66.
(A.R. No. 66 of 1908.)
VAḺUVŪR, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the prākāra of the Brahmapurīśvara temple.

The details of the date of the record viz., Viḷambi, Mēsha śu. 12,
Uttiram, Wednesday regularly correspond to 1538 A.D., April 10, f.d.t. 13
f.d.n. 32.

This incomplete inscription seem to record some gift by the adhikāri
Tikkana-nāyaka for conducting a festival called pallakku-sēvai (Palanquin
procession) for the deity Tiruvayaṉīśuram-uḍaiyār of Vaḻugūr alias
Kalikaḍindaśōḻanallūr in Chalukkip-paṟṟu in Irumbēḍu-nāḍu in Veṇkuṉṟak-
kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam. It is said that the above gift was made
for the merit of one Kāḷattirājaiyaṉ, son of mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Iḷaṅgaiyadēvaśōḻa-mahārājaiyaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 67.
(A.R. No. 67 of 1908.)
VAḺUVŪR, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the prākāra of the Brahmapurīśvara temple.

The details of the date viz., Sarvadhāri, Māśi 30 are not sufficient for
verification. The record may be assigned to c. 16th century on grounds
of palaeography.

It records a deed of agreement (iśai ōlai) entered into by the ūravar
of Vaḻugūr in connection with an offence committed by a certain
Mīnavaraiyar. It mentions that the fugitive must be given the death sentence
on the date of the record that the ūravar would pay 20 paṇam to a person
who furnishes the information (paṟai) evidently regarding the whereabouts
of the fugitive that a person who withholds the information would be
excommunicated, that one who provides assylum to the culprit would pay
the necessary fine (rājagaram) and put up with the āvikka[ḍa*]ṉ (a
punishment ?).

l.ta:
tiyati vaḻukūr ūrava[r]
emmil porintiyicai ōlai[yiṭ]ṭu[k]koṇṭapaṭi [||*]
minavarāyar avappaṭākkāri kaṭaippaṟṟa perumāḷ
koyilile pūntu aṭaittukkoḷḷukaiyil

naṉmai tiṉmaiyum tuṟantu cātikkup pi-

ṟampākak kaṭaivaṉākavum yiṭaṅkuṭuttavaṉe intaccetam

āvikka[ṭa*]ṉaiyum paṭakkaṭaivaṉākavum ippaṭi cammatittu
yicai ōlaiyiṭṭuk koṇṭom iv-

vaṉaivaro(m) mippaṭikku ivai ūṟku camainta apimāṉa
pūṣaṇa veḷār eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivarkaḷ colla
eḻutiṉamaikku ūṟkku taṇṭakanāṭṭu

marāyar
tirumalaiyār makaṉ aḻakiya varataṉ eḻuttu-

inscription.head:
No. 68.
(A.R. No. 68 of 1908.)
VAḺUVŪR, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up near the sluice of a tank.

This inscription is engraved carelessly and records the construction of
a sluice at Vaḻugūr by Aṟamiḷippāru Vaḷagū[r*] of Maṉṉarmaṅgalam in
Taḻundūr-nāḍu.
(Published in S.I.I., Vol. XII, No. 62).

inscription.head:
No. 69.
(A.R. No. 69 of 1908.)
VEḌĀL, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Nīlakaṇṭhēśvara temple.

The regnal year and the other details of date viz., Kaṟkaṭaka śu 7,
Wednesday correspond to 1318 A.D., July 5.

It records tax-free gift of the village Naṅgaiyār-ēndal by the nāṭṭār
of Perumugaip-paṟṟu in Malaippaṟṟu for conducting worship in, and the
repairs of, the temple of god Kaṟaikaṇṭhīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār of Viḍāl
in Vikramapāṇḍya-vaḷanāḍu alias Viḍārpaṟṟu of Veṇkuṉṟak-kōṭṭam in
Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
koṉerimel koṇṭāṉ koṟcaṭapaṉmar
tirupuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ śrī cuntarapāṇṭiyatevaṟku
yāṇṭu patiṉmuṉṟāvatiṉ etir muṉṟāvatu kaṟkaṭakanāyaṟṟu

miyum putaṉkiḻamai peṟṟa
attattu nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu
veṇkuṉṟakko-

[ṭṭa*]ttu viṭāṟpaṟṟāḷa vikkirama pāṇṭiya vaḷanāṭṭu
viṭāl uṭaiyār kaṟaikkaṇṭīcuramuṭaiya nāyaṉārkku
malaippaṟṟil perumukaippaṟṟu nāṭṭavarom iṉṉāyaṉārkku
pūcaikkun tiruppaṇikkum eṅkaḷ nāṭṭil naṅkaiyār
ēntal tirunāmattukkāṇi yiṟaiyiliyākat tantom ivūr
nāṟpāṟkellaikkum

[naṭuvu*]paṭṭa naṉcey puṉcey māvaṭai kuḷavaṭai puṟavaṭai
erivāypuṟavaṭai nattantaricu kaḷartiṭar taṟiyiṟai kācāy
vaṟkam maṟṟum epperppaṭṭaṉavum tirunāmattukkāṇi
yiṟaiyiliyākattantu ivūr

titta varaiyuñ celvatāka
kalliluñ cempilum veṭṭi ivūr nāṉ

paṉamum paṇṇittantom
perumukaippaṟṟu nāṭṭavarom ippaṭikku nāṭṭavar
paṇiyāl ivai nāṭṭuk kaṇakku vicaiyarāyaṉ eḻuttu
[||*]

inscription.head:
No. 70.
(A.R. No. 70 of 1908.)
VEḌĀL, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Nīlakaṇṭhēśvara temple.

This inscription in characters of about the 13th century stops abruptly
after referring to the village Viḍāl and the deity [Kaṟaikkaṇṭhīśvaram-
uḍaiyār.]

l.ta: āvatu viṭāl kaṟaikka This record stops abruptly here.-

inscription.head:
No. 71.
(A.R. No. 71 of 1908.)
VEḌĀL, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Nīlakaṇṭhēśvara temple.

This inscription contains two fragmentary parts. The first piece refers
to the king and his reign period. It seems to record the gift of four cows
for a lamp to god Kaṟaikaṇṭīśuram-uḍaiyār of Viḍāl in Uttamaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu
by Mūlaṅkiḻattinittāṇḍi. It was entrusted to the śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple.

The next piece records a gift of four cows for a perpetual lamp to
the deity [Kaṟaika*]ṇḍīchchuram-uḍaiyār by a certain Periyāḷvi, daughter
of a śeṭṭi, whose name is lost.
The other details are not clear as they have been indifferently engraved.

l.ta:
ḻavaḷanāṭṭu viṭāl kaṟaikaṇṭi curamuṭaiyaṟkku mulaṅkiḻat-
tinittāṇṭi vaitta ca
l.note: This rest of lines are not traceable.

l.ta:
ṟukku viṭṭa pacu nāluṅ koṇṭu ikkoyil kāṇiyuṭaiya
civa
l.note: This rest of lines are not traceable.
This rest of lines are not traceable.

l.ta:
ṇṭiccaramuṭaiyāṟku-parivumai ceṭṭi makaḷ periyāḷvi
vacca naṉtāviḷakkoṉ-

inscription.head:
No. 72.
(A.R. No. 72 of 1908.)
VEḌĀL, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the central shrine in the Nīlakaṇṭhēśvara temple.

This inscription may be assigned on palaeographical grounds to c. 13th
Century. The details of date Kaṟkaṭaka, śu 1 are not sufficient for
verification. It stops after the date portion abruptly. The king of this
record may be identified with his namesake figuring in inscription No. 69
discussed above.

l.ta:
koṉerimel koṇṭāṉ koṟcaṭapaṉmar
tirupuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ śrī cuntarapāṇṭiyatevaṟku
yāṇṭu patiṉ muṉṟāvatu etir muṉṟāvatu karkaṭakanāyaṟṟu
l.note: This rest of lines are not traceable.

inscription.head:
No. 73.
(A.R. No. 73 of 1908.)
VEḌĀL, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the central shrine in the Nīlakaṇṭhēśvara temple.

The characters of this epigraph may be assigned to c. 13th Century
and hence, the king may be identified with Kulōttuṅga III. The other details
of date viz., Wednesday and Puṉarpūsam are not sufficient for verification.

It records the grant of lands in the village Siviṟi in the Viḍīlpaṟṟu
along with its income from taxes (named) to meet the expenses of worship
and the repairs in the temple of god Kaṟaikkaṇṭhīśuram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār of
Viḍāl in Viḍāṟpaṟṟu alias Vikramapāṇḍya-vaḷanāḍu in Veṇkuṉṟak-kōṭṭam
in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam by the nāṭṭavar of Teḷḷāṟṟup-paṟṟu of the same
nāḍu. It refers to Toṇḍaimaṇḍala-brahmārāyaṉ as a signatory.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ ci kulottuṅka
coḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu

koṭṭattu viṭāṟpaṟṟāṉa vikkirama pāṇṭiya vaḷanāṭṭu
viṭālil uṭaiyār kaṟaikkaṇṭicuramuṭaiya nāyaṉārkku
iṉṉāṭṭut teḷḷāṟṟupaṟṟu nāṭṭavarom
iṉṉāyaṉārkkup pūcaikkum tiruppaṇi-

kkum eṅkaḷ nāṭṭil civiṟi tirunāmattukkāṇi
iṟaiyiliyākat tantom ivūr nāṟpāṟkellaikkum
uṭppaṭṭa naṉcey puṉcey māvaṭai kuḷavaṭai puṟavaṭai
erivāy puṟavaṭai nattantaricu kaḷartiṭar taṟiyiṟai taṭṭā-

rappāṭṭam cekkiṟai kācāyavaṟkam maṟṟum
epperppaṭṭaṉavum uṭpaṭa tirunāmattukkāṇiyiṟaiyili
yākat tantu ippaṭikku

tittavaraiyum cellak
kallilum cempilum veṭṭi ivūr nāṉkellaiyi-

paṉamum paṇṇit tantom
teḷḷāṟṟup paṟṟu nāṭṭavarom ippaṭikku
nāṭṭavarom ippaṭikku nāṭṭavar paṇiyāl ivai
toṇṭaimaṇṭalap piramarāyaṉ eḻuttu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 74.
(A.R. No. 74 of 1908.)
VEḌĀL, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the central shrine in the Nīlakaṇṭhēśvara temple.

The record may be assigned on palaeographical grounds to c. 13th
century.

This refers to an order issued by Nallappaṉ Paḍiyaṉ Aṅgarāyan to
the tānattār of the temple of Kaṟaikaṇṭēśvaram-udaiya-nāyanār of Viḍāl
in Agalūr-paṟṟu recording the grant of lands as iṟaiyili to meet the expenses
of festival, food offerings and daily worship on the occassion of
Kōdaṇḍarāmaṉ-sandi instituted in his name.

A number of tax terms and the boundaries of the gift land are
mentioned. Mention may be made of a number of signatories who attested
the document.

l.ta:
vatti koṉerimel
koṇṭāṉ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu veṇkuṉṟak
koṭṭattu viṭālpaṟṟāṉa vikkirama pāṇṭiya vaḷanāṭṭu
akalūrppaṟṟil viṭālil nāyaṉāṟ koyiṟt tāṉat-

tāṟku iṉṉāyaṉāṟku nallappaṉpaṭiyaṉ aṅkarāyaṉ
namperāl kaṭṭiṉa kotaṇṭarāmaṉ cantikkum tirunāḷukkum
amutuppaṭi cāttuppaṭi uḷḷiṭṭu veṇṭum nitta
nimantaṅkaḷukkum tirunāḷ celavukkum ikkoyil

ittāl varum kaṭamai poṉvari kāṇikkai kāttikaip
paccai māvaṭai kuḷavaṭai kācāyavaṟkam kiṟṟuvari cantu
vikkiramappeṟu vācal viṉivokam kūṟṟi-

taṭṭuppaṭṭi taṭṭārappāṭṭam aricikkāṇam āyavaṟkam
maka[n]mai [ceṭṭip]peṟu taccuttāḻvu āḷamañci nāṭṭu

ttu iṟaikaḷum
upātikaḷum tevaikaḷum uṭppaṭa mutal aṭaṅkal
iṟaiyiliyāka variyilār kaṇakkilum kaḻittu itukku nic-

ceyippāṉa poṉṉum iṉṉāṭṭukku nicceyitta mutalile
celaviṭuvatākac coṉṉom ippaṭikku ivvolai
piṭipāṭākak koṇṭu ivvūr kaikkoṇṭu

tittavaraiyum cellak *kallilum cempilum
veṭṭuvittu ivūr[ṉān]kellaiyilum tiruccūla

paṉam
paṇṇikkoṇṭu mutalaṭaṅkal iṟaiyiliyāka aṉupavittup
pūcait-

tirunāḷum tiruppaṇiyum tāḻvaṟa naṭattip potuka ivai
neṭṭuruṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu ivai neṭṭuruṭaiyāṉ teva
viccātirapperumāḷ iḷaiyāḻvāṉ kāliṅkarāyaṉ eḻuttu
yāṇṭu
l.note: Probably the expression patiṉmūṉṟāvatiṉ etir mūṉṟu is to be found here.
This portion may conjectually be restored as viccātarap •

inscription.head:
No. 75.
(A.R. No. 75 of 1908.)
VEḌĀL, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
East wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Nīlakaṇṭhēśvara temple.

p.b: Bhūpatirya, son of Dēvarāya Śaka 1329: 1408 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the grant of sarvamānya-iṟaiyili lands
alongwith its income from taxes in Pūdēri in Poṉṉūṟ-paṟṟu by a certain
Ārkkārmuṟ Sembandai Tuṇaiyirundāṉ Virupparāya-Kāṅgayar to provide
for the Tuṇaiyirundāṉ-śandhi evidently instituted by the donor, after his
own name, and for burning two perpetual lamps to god Kaṟaikaṇṭīśvaram-
uḍaiya-nāyaṉār and to meet the expenses of providing food-offerings, a
perpetual lamp and the repairs of the deity Payyaga-piḷḷaiyār in the village
Viḍāl in Poṉṉūṟ-paṟṟu in Śiṅgapura-nāḍu in Veṇkuṉṟak-kōṭṭam in
Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam. This was signed by Virupparāyak-Koṅgayar.

edition.l:
pṛthivirājyam paṇṇi aruḷāniṉṟa kālattuc cellāniṉṟa
śakābdam 1329- ṉ

l.ta:
kkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa puṇarpūcattu nāḷ ārkkārmuṟ
cempantai tuṇaiyiruntāṉ virupparāyak kāṅkayar jaya-

varaiyum cellap poṉṉūṟ paṟṟup
pūteriyellaiyil uṭpaṭa kiḻpāṟkellai

l.note: Probably ilaṅkaya teva • is the intended reading.

l.ta:
ṉattukku vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai iṉṉāyaṉār
tirunāmattukkāṇi viṭārellaikkuk kiḻakkum vaṭapā-

ṟkellai neṟkuṉṟat tellaiyilantip puṟavaṭakkut teṟkum
āka iṉṉāṉkellaik kuṭpaṭṭa nilamuṅ ki-

ḻ ṉokkiṉa kiṇaṟum meṉokkiṉa maramuṅ kaikkoṇṭu
ellai muṟṟun tām veṇṭum payiṟcey-

tu koṇṭu kaṭamai poṉvari kāṇikkaiyupātiyum pala
poṉvaṟkamum pala nelvaṟkamumippaṟṟ[ai]
ṉokkik koḷḷum pala varikaḷum pala upātikaḷumuṭpaṭa

inscription.head:
No. 76.
(A.R. No. 76 of 1908.)
VEḌĀL, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
East wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Nīlakaṇṭhēśvara temple.

p.b: Viruppaṇa-uḍaiyar, son of Hariyaṇa-uḍaiyar: naka 1314: 1392 A.D.

inscription.p:
The details of date viz., Āṅgīra, Vṛiśchika •• 8, Friday and Magha
regularly correspond to 1392 A.D., November 8. The paksha intended was
bahuḷa.

This incomplete epigraph records the grant of lands in Tirumaṇi in
Poṉṉūr-paṟṟu by one Rāmadēvarāyaṉ, at the instance of Śivadēvar for
maintaining a perpetual lamp, worship, food-offerings and repairs to the
deity Kaṟaikaṇṭīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār of Viḍāl in Poṉṉūr-paṟṟu in
Śiṅgapura-nāḍu in ••••• kūṟṟam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḷa-maṇḍalam.

edition.l:
••••• ṉṟa śakābdam 1314- ṉ melcellā niṉṟa
āṅgiravarṣattu vraścika nāyaṟṟu • •

l.ta:
miyum veḷḷikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa makattu nāḷ
jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇ[ṭa] The rest of the record is not traceable.lattu

••• kūṟṟattu ciṅkapura nāṭṭu poṉṉūrpaṟṟu viṭāl
uṭaiyār kaṟaikkaṇṭi

kku amutupaṭi tirununtāviḷakku oṉṟukkum pūcait
tiruppaṇikkum irāmatevarāyaṉ

vatevarāṇai yā[l*] kuṭutta poṉṉūrpaṟṟu
tirumaṇiyil entalāṉa muṅ •• l [ni]lam

••• ntalum •• puṟavaṭaiyum iṉṉāyaṉāṟku taṉ ellai muṟṟum
tām veṇṭu[m]

••• ••• •••• koṇṭu kaṭamai poṉvari vācalpaṇam
taṟi kaṭamai ••••• kuṭimai

••• [mā]vaṭai ma[ṟa]vaṭai ••••• ippaṟṟil koḷḷum
pala varikaḷum pala upātikaḷum

l.note: The rest of the record is not traceable.

inscription.head:
No. 77.
(A.R. No. 77 of 1908.)
VEḌĀL, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
West wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Nīlakaṇṭhēśvara temple.

This inscription may be assigned on palaeographical grounds to c. 13th
Century. The details of the date viz., Mēsha śu 3, Monday and Mūlam
are irregular as the nakshatra Mūlam do not fall in the month of Mēsha
and on the śukla tritīya day. The nakshatra has been wrongly read as
Hasta which cannot fall in the month of Mēsha and on Śukla tritīya day.
The details of date given above may correspond with four dates viz., A.D.,
1204, 1231, 1258 and 1302 in this period. But his 25th year of rule do not
regularly agree with any of the first three dates. It corresponds to 1302
A.D.

It records a gift of lands in the village Aruḷi-ēttal in Attip-paṟṟu to
conduct a festival called Śeyyāṟṟu-veṉṟāṉ-tirunāḷ and to provide for the
repairs and perpetual lamp to god Kaṟaikkaṇḍīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār at
Viḍār in Viḍārpaṟṟu alias Vikramapāṇḍya-vaḷanāḍu in Veṇkuṉṟak-kōṭṭam
in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
cayaṅkoṇṭa coḻa maṇṭalattu veṇkuṉṟak koṭṭattu
viṭārpaṟṟāṉa

pāṇṭiya vaḷanāṭṭu viṭāril
uṭaiy[ār ka]ṟaik kaṇṭī

ṉāṟku ceyyāṟṟuveṉṟāṉ tirunāḷukkum tiruppaṇikkum
tirunantāviḷakkukkum attippaṟṟu aruḷi ettal

iṟaiyili tirunāmattukkāṇi āka iṉṉāyanāṟku
ivvūr nāṟpāṟkellaiyum tiruccūla

inscription.head:
No. 78.
(A.R. No. 78 of 1908.)
VEḌĀL, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Nīllakaṇṭhēśvara temple.

p.b: Kumāra Kampaṇa, son of Bokkaṇa: Sādhāraṇa: 1370 A.D.

inscription.p:
The beginning of the lines are lost. The record may palaeographically
be assigned to c. 14th Century. The details of date viz., Sādhāraṇa, Dhanus
śu 8, Wednesday, Uttiraṭṭādi regularly correspond to 1370 A.D., December
25, f.d.t. 68. The Śaka year being 1292 expired.

It records a grant of lands along with income from various taxes
(specified) in the village Umaiyāḷ-ēndal in Poṉṉūrp-paṟṟu to provide for
the worship, repairs and perpetual lamp to the deity Kaṟaikaṇṭīśvaram-
uḍaiya-nāyaṉār of Viḍār in Poṉṉūrp-paṟṟu in Śiṅgapura-nāḍu in Veṇkuṉṟak-
kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, for the merit of king Bokkaṇṇa-
uḍaiyar (i.e., Bukka I). The name of the donor is not mentioned in the
record. Probably it may be a royal gift that is referred to in this record.

l.note: The beginning of the lines are lost.
l.ta:
••• ••• •••• pokkaṇa uṭaiyar kumārar kumāra kampaṇa
uṭaiyarkkuc cellāniṉṟa

putaṉkiḻamaiyum
uttiraṭṭāti nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa cOḻamaṇṭalattu
veṇkuṉṟak koṭṭattu ciṅkapuranāṭṭup poṉṉū[rpa]ṟṟu
viṭāril uṭaiyār kaṟaikkaṇṭī

••• ••• •••• uṭaiyarukku naṉṟāka iṉṉāyanāṟku pūcaikkum
tiruppaṇikkum tiruṉantāviḷakku[kku*]m ippaṟṟil
umaiyāḷ ental tirunāmattukkāṇi yiṟaiyiliyākat
tantom ivvūr nāṟpāṟkellaikkum uṭpaṭṭa naṉcey
puṉcey māvaṭai maravaṭai kuḷavaṭai puṟavaṭai erivāy
puṟavaṭai nattam taricu kaḷar

inscription.head:
No. 79.
(A.R. No. 79 of 1908.)
VEḌĀL, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Nīlakaṇṭhēśvara temple.

This undated epigraph is engraved in characters of about the
15th Century. It records a short deed of agreement (śiṟu-muṟi) issued (by
the temple authorities) in the name of the deity Ādichaṇḍīśvara along with
a certain Kuñjaramallar to the army (śēṉai) called kaivalāṉ Taṉiy-āmai-
vallāṉ-śēṉai Kāchchukaṭṭi-Aṉṟuvarāgaṇḍaṉ regarding the grant of sites
(maṉai) (the boundaries of which are specific) as a kāṇi from the month
of Avaṇi in the above year to the temple of Kaṟaikaṇṭhīśvaram-uḍaiya-
nāyiṉār of Viḍāl. Among the boundaries specified in the record, the temple
of Sambandap-perumāḷ-nāyaṉār is situated to the south of the gift land.

l.ta:
āti caṇṭēcuraṉ tiruvāy malantaruḷiṉapaṭikku [|*] kuñcura
mallarkku ciṟumuṟi viṭāl uṭaiyārāṉa

kaṟai kaṇṭī[cu]ramuṭaiya nāyiṉār tirunāmattuk kāṇi
paritāpi varuṣām

taṅkaḷukku teruvāka campantapperumāḷ nāyaṉār
koyilukku vaṭakku kiḻciṟaku maṭamāka mukkol

kaṟai viṭṭu vaṭakkum mel ciṟaku kallukku vaṭakkum
vāṇiyan vāṉiṉattukku kiḻakkum vaṭakku

kaḻaṉikkālukku teṟkum kiḻakku tirunaṉtavaṉattukku meṟkum
uṭpaṭṭa maṉaiyum maṉaippaṭaippai-

yum kaivalāṉa taṉiyāṇmai vallāṉ kāccukaṭṭi | aṉṟu
varākaṇṭaṉ ceṉaikkum

inscription.head:
No. 80.
(A.R. No. 80 of 1908.)
VEḌĀL, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the gōpura of the Nīlakaṇṭhēśvara temple.

This Sanskrit inscription engraved in Grantha characters of about the
14th century consists of two verses in the Śārdūlavikrīḍita metre.

The first verse is in praise of the deity Nīlakaṇṭha at Vishapura,
worshipped by the large Nīlagrīva (i.e., Kaṟaikaṇṭhīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār
of Veḍāl), while the second verse speaks of seven hills in that place, named
after seven persons viz., Phaṇīpati (i.e., Ādiśēsha), etc., who are stated to
have worshipped the Īśvara here.

l.gr:
pi rāmanāmanagarē rāmēśvara stannataḥ skandaśō guha
pūjitō guha-

purē pādhāt sa vōñcaparē nīlagrīva
[mu]ni[ṣṭu]tō viṣapurē śrī nīla-

kaṇṭhaḥ kalau | [1*] ādāvēva sadāśivam phaṇipati
śrīkaṇṭhagauri-
guhā dhātā viṣṇura vīśvaraḥ viṣupurē cakram

yē kurvvanti ca tān pradakṣiṇammī mucyanta ēvaina
sā | [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 81.
(A.R. No. 81 of 1908.)
VEḌĀL, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Above the entrance into the maṇḍapa in front of a cavern on a hill.

This inscription engraved in characters of about the 16th century
and records the gift of a maṭha called Nīlakaṇṭha-nāyaṉār-maṭha to a
certain Sāmantaya-nāyakkar for the merit of one Timmā-nāyakkar. The
name of the donor is not mentioned in the record.

inscription.head:
No. 82.
(A.R. No. 82 of 1908.)
VEḌĀL, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a boulder near a natural cave known as Āṇḍār-maḍam on a hill.
This inscription may palaeographically be assigned to c. 8th Century.

It records a gift of two paṭṭis of land by Siṅgaṉ-Śattimalai to a jaina
establishment (paḷḷi) at Viḍāl. It also seems to refer to the renovation of
some structure by a certain Viḻi ••••• p-pērāyar, who is described as
the head (māmudali) of another paḷḷi at Tirunaluṅgoṇḍai.

l.note: The reading may also be ciṅkaṉ

inscription.head:
No. 83.
(A.R. No. 83 of 1908.)
VEḌĀL, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a boulder in front of the natural cave called Āṇḍār-maḍam on
the hill.

This record may be assigned to c. 8-9th century on palaeographical
grounds.

It seems to record the gift of gold for a lamp in the paḷḷi at
Nandimaṅgalam and registers an undertaking given by Sembiyan
Tamiḻavēḷar of the above paḷḷi to the son of Tiruppūr-nakkaṉ of Pulaiyyūr
to maintain the lamp by the female jaina acetic Kanaka vīrakkuṟattiyār
and her pupils from out of the income from taxes like eṇṇaik-kāṇam and
pudā-nāḻi granted by the latter. Nandimaṅgalam seems to be the other
name of the Veḍāl, called Viḍāl, the findspot of the record. This name
also figures in a record of Chōḷa Rājakēsarivarman discussed below.
(No. 84).

l.ta:
••• mātevi āna nan[ti] maṅkalattu paḷḷiyuṭ[ai*]ya
cempiyan •••

m eṇṇaiyk kāṇamum putā
nāḻiyum koṇṭu •••••

inscription.head:
No. 84.
(A.R. No. 84 of 1908.)
VEḌĀL, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a boulder in front of the natural cave called Āṇḍār-maḍam on
the hill.

This record may be assigned to c. 9th Century on grounds of
palaeography.

It records an undertaking given by the lay disciples of a Jaina
monastery at Viḍāl alias Nandimaṅgalam, in the eastern side of Śiṅgapura-
nāḍu, to protect and feed Kanakavīrakkurattiyār, the female disciple and
follower of Guṇakīrti-Bhaṭāra of Viḍāl, her pupils and the four hundred
female ascetics. The king of this record may be identified with Āditya
I.
(Published in S.I.I., Vol. III, No. 92, Ibid., Vol. XIII, No. 245)

l.ta: ṅkapura nāṭṭuk kiḻvaḻi vāṭāl mā[ḷa]viyūrānta maṅkala-

••• ṭanuṅ kiḻānummuṭaya kuṇakirtti paṭārarvaḻi
māṇākkiyār kaṉakavirakkuratti-

rā[na*]ntamaṅkala muṭaiyyārayum mavar vaḻi
māṇākkiyāraiyum •• ḷiru nūṟṟuvarayum

kanaka virakkura[t*]tiyār [i]vakaḷ[ḷātaṉa]maiyil ikkayaṟ
viḷai •• [nā] nūṟṟukkum vaḻi •• ṅ-

itu [val]laṉ na[ṉ]ṟuṭal melaṉa mātaviyūrānanta
maṅkalamuṭaiya ka[ṉa]kavirakkurattiyār

••••• vil itaṉai tiṟuttavalavar makaḷā[ta]ṉamaiyil āca
vallavar •••••

inscription.head:
No. 85.
(A.R. No. 85 of 1908.)
VEḌĀL, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a pillar of the maṇḍapa in front of the natural cave known as
Āṇḍār-maḍam on the hill.

This damaged inscription can be assigned to c. 10th Century on grounds
of palaeography.

It seems to refer to a gift by Viṇṇagar Vayiramēgaṉ to a Daśapuriyan,
a Kāḷāmukha belonging to the Harita-gōtra and Āpastamba-sūtra. The other
details are not clear as the record is incomplete.

inscription.head:
No. 86.
(A.R. No. 86 of 1908.)
NEṞKUṆAM, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a Slab set up in front of the Śilaiyammaṉ temple.

This inscription may be assigned to c. 9th Century on grounds of
palaeography.

It records a grant of land (names specified) alongwith income from
taxes as ērippaṭṭi for the maintenance of a lake at Neṟkuṉṟam on the eastern
direction of Śiṅgapura-nāḍu by Nambiyamallaṉār, son of
Nṛipatuṅgamaṅgalappēraraiyaṉ. One of the taxes referred to here is termed
āṉavāy-daṇḍam. The name Nṛipatuṅgamaṅgalappēraraiyaṉ seems to
suggest that he should have been named after the Pallava king
Nṛipatuṅgavarman.

The high regnal year of the king and the palaeography of the record
helps us to identify this Rajakēsarivarman with Chōḷa Āditya I.
(Published in S.I.I. Vol. III, No. 93; and Ibid., Vol. XIII, No. 308).

inscription.head:
No. 87.
(A.R. No. 87 of 1908.)
NEṞKUṆAM, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a Slab set up near the sluice of the lake in the village.

The details of the date viz., Śaka 1352, Sādhāraṇa, Āvaṇi 12 are not
sufficient for verification. The record may be assigned to c. 15th Century
on grounds of palaeography.

It records the permission granted by Tirumalai-nāyakkar, son of
Kanyāsūryaṉ Vaḍakku-nāyakkar and his horseman of the Kannaḍa country
for the excavation of the lake called Sukumāṟaṉ-ēri at Neṟ-kuṇam. The
village Neṟkuṇam was held as jīvita-paṟṟu by the king himself.

edition.l: cakāttam 135[2]- ṉ me

inscription.head:
No. 88.
(A.R. No. 88 of 1908.)
ŚŌḺAPURAM, SIVAGANGAI TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North, West and South bases of a ruined Gaṇēśa temple in the village.

p.b: Māṟavarman Sundarapāṇḍya I Year: Lost (acc. 1216 A.D.)

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record in three pieces has been arranged as A, B
and C. This disturbed inscription contains parts of the enlarged version
of the praśasti beginning with Pūmaruviya, etc., of the king. From the
extant portion available, it seems to register some gift to provide for food-
offerings. The name of the officer who attested the grant made is not
clear.

inscription.head:
No. 89.
(A.R. No. 89 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

The details of date of the record viz., Śaka 1421, Siddhārthi, Tai 12,
do not admit verification. The weekday and the nakshatra are not given.

The king is described as the Dharmarāya and as the son of
Narasiṁharāya.

This incomplete record refers to a dharma śāsana issued by Tipparasar
to the mūlaparusha-mahājanas of Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in
Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu, exempting the taxes like araśu-kaṭṭu, pāḍikāval,
kīḻvārappachchai, viniyōgam, echchōṟu, etc on the mānya-kshētra which
were being enjoyed by them already and for the enjoyment of the same
as sarvvamāṇya.

The present epigraph seems to furnish the last date for Tipparasa, the
agent of the king as we hear from the inscriptions from Pirāṉmalai in
Tiruppatur Taluk that some gifts were made for his merit. These two
records of Immaḍi Narasiṁha are dated Śaka 1422, Raudri, a year later
than the date of our record. Does it indicate that Tipparasa died between
the date of the record under study and that of the above two epigraphs?
(See A.R.Ep., 1903, Nos. 139 and 151).

l.ta: ṟu taṅkaḷ āḷ aṭiyār iṟai ūḻiyaṅkaḷukkuṅ kaḻittu

inscription.head:
No. 90.
(A.R. No. 90 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the central shrine in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This inscription in Sanskrit and Tamil is written in Grantha and Tamil
characters. Palaeographically, the record may be assigned to 8th-9th
Centuries A.D. The Sanskrit verses at the beginning states that Tennavan-
Pallavādhipa, who is also called Māṟaṉ-Āditya born at Pōḻiyūr in Pōḻiyūr-
dēśa made an endowment of forty Kṛishṇa-kāchā for maintainning a lamp
to the deity Śūlapāṇi in the temple at Śrīsthalī.

The Tamil portion, dated in the 4th+1st year and 593rd day of the
reign of Māṟañjaḍaiyaṉ records the gift of a perpetual lamp by Māṟaṉ-
Āchchaṉ alias Teṉṉavaṉ Pallavaraiyaṉ of Pōḻiyūr-nāḍu to be maintained
in the temple of the deity Tirukkaṟṟaḷi-bhaṭāra at Tirupputtūr, a
brahmadēya in Mīkuṇḍāru in Koḻuvūr-kūṟṟam. The amount of gift is
specified as 120 palam. The sabhai of Maṇaṟkkuḍi, a brahmadēya village
received the interest of 40 kaḻañju and agreed to supply one uri of ghee
daily measured by the śūla-nāḻi of the temple treasury to the temple of
Tirukkaṟṟaḷi-bhaṭāra. Also states that the vaṇṇār of Tirupputtūr had
received a kaḻañju as poliyūṭṭu.

The chief Māṟaṉ Āchchaṉ also figures in a record, of the same king,
dated an year earlier, from Courtallam in Tenkasi Taluk in Tirunelveli
District. (S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 4) In this record he is referred to as the araiyaṉ
of Pōḻiyūr. In our inscription he is referred to also by his title Teṉṉavaṉ
Pallavaraiyaṉ, Māṟaṉ-Achchaṉ is the Tamil variant of the Sanskrit name
Māṟaṉ-Āditya. (Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 5) Tirukkaṟṟaḷi-bhaṭāra has
been curtailed into the modern name of the temple Tiruttaḷīśvara.

It is probable that this person must have enjoyed the status of a minor
chieftain of Pōḻiyūr area. This chief is totally different from śōḻāntaka-
Pallavaraiyaṉ alias Māṟaṉ Ādichchaṉ of Poḻiyūr figuring in a record of
Śōḻantalaikoṇḍa Vīra Pāṇḍya (acc. 946 A.D.) from Paḷḷimaḍam in
Aruppukkottai Taluk of Ramanathapuram District. (See, S.I.I., Vol. XIV,
No. 79).

The village Pōḻiyūr in the division Pōḻiyūr-nāḍu must have been
included in the Mudukuḷattur or in the Paramakudi Taluk of
Ramanathapuram District. (See, S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 5).

l.gr:
śrī tēnnavan pallavādhipaḥ [||*] dīpannitya pradīptyai
sikataku ṭibhava br[ā*]hmaṇēbhyaḥ pradāya-

catvāriśatpramānān śatadhrati caraṇōtthuta varṇṇa
pradhānaḥ [|*] śrī sthalyām kraṣakācān tadudibhavi-

ṣā praddhidēyēna vipraiḥ ārddha prastēnasiddhairi
ppravihitamakarōtsusthita[ś] śūlapāṇēḥ |||—

inscription.head:
No. 91.
(A.R. No. 91 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

The details of date of the record viz., Śaka 1432, Pramōdūkā,
Uttarāyaṇa, Vasanta-ṛitu, Ṛishabha śu 12, Monday, Svāti-nakshatra regularly
correspond to 1510 A.D., May 20.

It seems to state that Śiṅgama-nāyakkar, son of Veṅgala-nāyakkar of
Sinda-ppaṭṭu ratified a grant of 6 mā of land previously made by Bhairava-
nayiṉār, son of Dakshiṇāmūrti-bhaṭṭar Ādityadēvar of Kāñchīpuram
belonging to Bhārgava-gōtra and Bōdhāyana-sūtra, for the merit of Svāmi
Chellappar alias Vīra Narasiṁharāya-nāyakkar and the same land was made
over as sarvamānya to a person who was the son of Śrīsthalīśa-bhaṭṭa,
belonging to Prathama Ātrēya-gōtra and Āgnēya vaiśya-sūtra and residing
at Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu, for the enjoyment
of his heirs. The chief who ratified this grant, is described with a string
of birudas like gaṇḍara-gaṇḍa, Vaidigajabhīma, etc., probably indicating
a high rank during the rule of this king.

l.note: The missing letter looks like u
The letter cintappaṭṭu § is engraved (as an internal caption) below the lines.

inscription.head:
No. 92.
(A.R. No. 92 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the central shrine in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

The date portion of the record is mutilated. The cyclic year
Pramōdūta during the reign period of this king corresponds to Śaka 1432
and in the Dakshiṇāyana of that year śu 12, Monday, U[ttiraṭṭādi]-nakshatra
may be equated with 1510 A.D., October 14, f.d.n. 13. This is in the month
of Tulā only.

This incomplete record semms to register the grant of 48 shares and
52 house-sites (maṉai) at Karaiyūr and Nāraṇamaṅgalam in Kēraḷaśiṅga-
vaḷanāḍu in Pāṇḍi-maṇḍalam by Bhairava-nayiṉār, son of Dakshiṇāmūrti-
bhaṭṭar Ādityadēvar of Kāñchīpuram belonging to Bhārgava-gōtra and
Bōdhāyana-sūtra, as vipra-bhāga (share for the brāhmaṇas) to Chellappa for
the study of vēdas and Purāṇas (addhyayana-vṛitti and Purāṇa-vṛitti)

The order recording the aforesaid grant, made for the merit of Vīra
Narasiṁharāya-nāyakkar alias Chellappar was issued to the aśēsha-vidvan-
mahājanas of the different gōtras and sūtras, on the date mentioned earlier.

Chellappar (Śellappar) alias Vīra Narasiṁha is known to us from the
previous record, wherein too, the gift was made for his merit. The honorific
title Svāmi mentioned for him in the record No. 91 of 1908 indicate that
he was a powerful chief or viceroy. He has been identified with
Narasiṁharāya or Narasa-nāyaka, the father of Kṛishṇadēvarāya in the
Report. (See, A.R.Ep, 1909, para 69, page. 116). V. Rangachari had suggested
that he is identical with his namesake who figures and styles as ubhaya-
pradhāni in a record from Chingleput dated Śaka 1450. (Same as, A.R.Ep.,
1909, No. 233, Ind. Ant., Vol. XLIII, pp 45-46).

l.ta:
lattu keraḷa ciṅkavaḷanāṭṭu kāraiyūrum nāraṇamaṅkalamum
ti[ruc]-

lai vela[ṅ]kuṭi ellaikkum tiruppattūr ellaikkum māṅkuṭi
ellaikkum va-

inscription.head:
No. 93.
(A.R. No. 93 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the maṇḍapa in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This epigraph belongs to the reign period of Śrīvallabha and does not
furnish the details of date except his regnal year. In the absence of praśasti,
the identification poses a problem. Palaeographically the record may be
assigned to the second half of the 12th Century.

It registers a gift of 50 sheep for maintaining a perpetual lamp to
the deity Śrītali-Paramēśvara in the temple at Tirupputtūr a brahmadēya
in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu by Rājēndraśōḻaṉ Kēraḷaṉ alias Nishādharājaṉ. The
gift was entrusted to a sheperd Naṅgaṉ Chōṟaṉ, who agreed to supply one
uḻakku of ghee measured by śūla-uḻakku for the maintenance of the lamp.
(S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 252).

l.ta:
rājan ittevaṟku
tirununtāviḷakku oṉṟu erippataṟkuc cūla uḻa-

kkāl uḻakku ney cantirātittaval aṭṭuvataṟku iṭaiyaṉ
naṅkaṉ coṟaṉ kaikkoṇṭa-

inscription.head:
No. 94.
(A.R. No. 94 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
East wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Central Shrine in the
Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This record may be assigned to the 12th century on palaeographical
groungs.

It records a gift of 300 kalam of paddy from out of the dēvadāṉa
villages Pottikkuḍi and Viḷakkamaṅgalam of the deity Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍār by
Rājēndraśōḻaṉ Kēraḷaṉ alias Nishādharāyan of Poṉṉamarāpati in Puṟamalai-
nāḍu, for the festival of pavitrārōhaṇa to be conducted in the month of
Aippigai (Aippaśi) to the god Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍār of Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya
in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu.

The king Parākrama-pāṇḍya may be identified with Māṟavarmaṉ
Parākrama, who is known to have been a contemporary of Kulōttuṅga
I as said earlier. The Vikkiramaṅgalam record of Parākrama Pāṇḍya, who
is also identified with the above Māṟavarmaṉ, refers to a gift of the 40th
year of Kulōttuṅga (See A.R.Ep., 1927, para II, para 39).

inscription.head:
No. 95.
(A.R. No. 95 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
East wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.
This record may palaeographically be assigned to the 12th century.

It states that the mūlaparishai of Tirupputtūr met in the temple of
Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār, at Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-
vaḷanāḍu and agreed to assign lands made after purchase from out of the
50 śōḻiya-naṟ-paḻaṅkāśu deposited in the temple treasury by a certain lady
Jaṉiyāṉai-Tiruvuḍaiyāḷ of Niyamam in Tēnaṟṟuppōkku and a resident of
Śrīkōyil for the purpose of maintaining a tirunilai-viḷakku (lamp) weighing
140 palam to the deity mentioned above.
