inscription.head:
No. 96.
(A.R. No. 96 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
East wall of the maṇḍapa in the front of central shrine in the
Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

p.b: In characters of C. 11th Century A.D.

inscription.p:
This record is in Grantha Characters composed in Sanskrit verse. This
is in Arya metre. This incomplete verse mentions the name Kuntaḷavati
and the daity Śristhaḷī (Tamil: Tiruttaḷī).

inscription.head:
No. 97.
(A.R. No. 97 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of central shrine in the
Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This record begins with the meykirti Tirumaḍandaiyum-Jayamaḍandaiyum,
etc., of the Pāṉdya king Śrīvallabha. It mentions the mūlaparushai of
Tiruppattūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu and registers the gift
of 11 kañañju, six mañjāḍi of gold to meet the expenses for raising the
garden (tirunandavaṉam) and for the tirumugai and tiruppaḷḷittāmam to
the dūty Śrītaḷiparamēśvarar by Tañjāvūrkiḻavaṉ Vaḍugaṉ-uyyavandāṉ, a
merchant of Aruviyūr alias Dēśi Uyyavandapaṭṭaṇam in the above nāḍu.
The sempoṉ-kāśu deposited in the treasury was weighed by the measure
called kūttāḍi. The garden raised was named Toṇḍiyūraṉ-Uyyavandāṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 98.
(A.R. No. 98 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This built in inscription which is not fully preserved may be assigned
to about the 12th century on grounds of palaeography. It commences with
the praśasti of the king Tirumagaḷ-puṇara, etc. of the king.

It states that the mūlaparushai of Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in
Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu after making an announcement (vāyiṟ-chāṟṟi) met at
the Naralōkavīraṉ-tiruv-eḍuttukkaṭṭi in the temple of Niṉṟaruḷiṉa
Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-viṇṇagar-āḻvār of Kaṟkuḷaṅgarai and transacted the sale of
lands āvaṇattu-nattam and a tank in the village Vēlaṅguḍi, a hamlet to the
west of Tirupputtūr, for the deity Śrītaḷi-Paramēśvara of the place. The
sale-price of 120 kaḻañju of gold was received by the sabhai from the
temple treasury of the above deity and made over the lands, after making
it tax free for the Naralōkavīraṉ-sandhi to be conducted in the midday
as an endowment of Uyyavandāṉ Kaṇḍadēvaṉ alias Dēvar Kāṅgēyar. A
village named Varaguṇapuram figures as one of the boundaries of the sale-
land.

inscription.head:
No. 99.
(A.R. No. 99 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

The details of the date of the record viz., Siṁha 30 in the regnal year
2+1+1 of the above king are not sufficient for verification. The record
may be assigned on grounds of palaeography to about the 12th century.

It records a gift of taxes antarāyam, nel, nāḍupāḍu and kuḍimai from
Atiśayapāṇḍyanallūr alias Kūttakkuḍi in Kīḻ-Kuṇḍāṟu, the dēvadāṉa
villages given earlier to the deity Varaguṇapperm.ḷ Kūttāḍudēvar, by
Kaṇḍaṉ Uyyavand.āṉ alias Kāṅgayaṉ of Niyamam in Tēṉāṟṟuppōkku to
meet the expenses connected with the festival conducted on the day of
his natal star Puṉarpūśam, for the food-offerings to be made on the above
day, for the persons reciting Tiruppadiyam hymns daily, to the dēvaraḍiyārs
accompanying them, the sambāṉ, mattaḷakkāṟaṉ (drummer) and to those
who sung Tamil (hymns) daily, in the temple of Tiruttaḷiyāṉda-nāyaṉār of
Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 100.
(A.R. No. 100 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This inscription may be assigned to 12th Century on grounds of
palaeography.

It records the grant of income from taxes kaḍamai and antarāyam
on lands in Nārāyaṇamaṅgalam by Tiruvālanduṟaiyuḍaiyāṉ Tirukkoḍunguṉṟam-
uḍaiyāṉ alias Nishādha-rājaṉ of Poṉṉamarāpati in Puṟamalai-nāḍu, for the
feeding of Śrīmāhēśvaras on festival days probably in the maṭha. The village
was endowed as maḍappuṟam to Tiruchchiṟṟambalam [Tiru]vāchagar-
tirumaḍam located at the western entrance in the temple of Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-
nāyaṉār of Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu.

Nishādharājan bearing the surname Tirukkoḍuṅguṉṟam-uḍaiyāṉ is
probably identical with his namesake who figures in a record of the 25th
year of the reign of Kulōttuṅga I (acc. 1070 A.D.) from Śivapuri who inturn
is identical with Nishādharājar who held the office of Śrīkāryam of the
temple at Śrīraṅgam. (See, S.I.I., Vol. XXIV, No. 83).

l.ta:
tūrt tiruttaḷiyāṇṭa nāyanār śrīkoyilil melait
tiruvā-

inscription.head:
No. 101.
(A.R. No. 101 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

The record is dated in the 4+1st year of the king which with other
details of date viz., Kaṟkaṭaka 27, Saturday, Rōhiṇi correspond to 1166 A.D,
July 23.

The record do not contain any praśasti unlike the other inscriptions
of Kulaśēkhara. It states that the Mūlaparishai-mahāsabhai of Tiruppattūr,
a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu met at the Āyiratt-eḻunūṟṟuvaṉ-
maṇḍapa in the Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār temple and declared the old
dēvadāna lands of this temple as tax-free. The sabhai received 120 śōḻiya-
naṟ-paḻaṅ-kāśu from the temple treasury as it required some money (śirudu-
dravyam) to go to Madurai to pay its respects to the king.

taḷi āṇṭa namāyaṉār śrīkoyilil āyiratteḻunūṟṟuvan-
tirumaṇṭattuk kūṭṭak kuṟaivaṟak kūṭiyiru

inscription.head:
No. 102.
(A.R. No. 102 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This inscription may be assigned to the 12-13th century on grounds
of palaeography. The details of the record viz., Ṛishabha, 20th day are
not sufficient for verification.

It records the allotment of dēvadāṉa lands of the deity by the
mūlaparishai of Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśinga-vaḷanāḍu after
receiving 50 śōḻiyanaṟ-paḻaṅ-kāśu deposited in the temple treasury by
Taḷiyilā[ḷi] Nakka[ṉ Pāṇḍiyakkō]li, a resident of Niyamam in Tēṉāṟṟuppōkku
for maintaining a perpetual lamp to the deity Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār. It
also refers to the gift of five tulā (material not known ?) for a standing
lamp, probably in the same temple.

l.ta:
m ceyaṅkoṇṭa coḻa viṇṇakarāḻvār tiruvācalil
kūṭiyi[ru]kka teṉāṟṟu pokku ni-

inscription.head:
No. 103.
(A.R. No. 103 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

The record may be assigned on palaeographical grounds to c. 12th
century. It is dated in the year 2 opposite to 1 Mithuna 5, Sunday
corresponding to 1165 A.D., May 30.

It records that the mūlaparushai-mahāsabhai of Tirupputtūr, a
brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu after purchasing the tax paying lands
of god Kailāsamuḍaiya-nāyanār, declared it as tax-free dēvadāṉa and
received in lieu of 30 śōḻiya-naṟ-paḻaṅ-kāśu which the sabhai wanted to
spend at the place where the king (perumāḷ) is staying. The above
declaration is said to take effect from the year opposite the 3rd year of
the king (i.e., the 4th year).

In a record from Kōḍaganallūr (No. 205 of 1932-33) the king is stated
to have stayed at Kuruvittuṟai in the month of November 1165 A.D. It
does not refer to the place where he was camping. Obviously, the king
must have arrived at Śōḻakulāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, the same as
Kuruvittuṟai about 6 months prior to the date of Kōḍaganallūr record. He
is later known to have moved to the city of Madurai, 30 miles to the
east of Kuruvittuṟai sometime before July 1166 A.D. (vide No. 101 discussed
above) which was visited by the mahāsabhai of Tirupputtūr for paying
respects to the said king.

l.ta:
yiṟṟu añcāntiyati peṟṟa tirunāyiṟṟukkiḻamai nāḷ

inscription.head:
No. 104.
(A.R. No. 104 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This record may be assigned on palaeographical grounds to c. 12th-
13th century. The record is dated in the year 13, Vṛiśchika 23. These details
do not admit verification.

It states that a certain Poṉṉanāgaraṉ who was guilty of having killed
a brahman (brahmahatyai-paṇṇi) was in punishment therefore, tied upto
the legs of a he-buffalo (kiḍā) and dragged along the place. To meet the
expenses to be incurred at the place where the above punishment is meted
out, a merchant named Tiruchchiṟṟambala-muḍaiyār-uttamaṉ of Neḍuvāyil
purchased and made a gift of land as maḍappuṟam to the Tiruttaṇḍattogaiyaṉ-
tirumaḍam situated at the southern entrance of the Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍār temple
at Tirupputtūr and the mūlaparushai-mahsabhai of Tirupputtūr in
Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu which met in the ambalam (hall) called Dayāpañjaram
received from Uttamaṉ a lump-sum amount of 75 śōḻiya-naṟ-paḻaṅ-kāśu
and made the land endowed by him, free from the payment of taxes.

l.ta:
karaṉaikkiṭāviṉ kāliṟkaṭṭi iḻutta vaṭattu
veṇṭum viniyokattukku

tiruttaḷiyāṇṭār śrī koyil teṟkil tiruvācalil
tiruttoṇṭat tokaiyaṉ tiru-

ṉ uttama vilaikoṇṭuviṭṭa nilam ivūr vaṭavāy
vayaṟpūti cāttavāykkāṟ kiḻ makiḻum

inscription.head:
No. 105.
(A.R. No. 105 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This fragmentary record commences with the praśasti Tirumagaḷ-pōla,
etc., of the above king. The first digit of the regnal year is lost. It may
be either 1 or 2, in which case it may correspond to 1002-03 or 1012-13
A.D.

It seems to record that the sabhai of Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in
Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu met at uṟaṅgāppuḷi on the northern bank of
Kaṟkuḷam and sold 1(1/4) śey, of land in favour of the deity Śrītaḷiparamēśvara.

There is a mutilated record adjascent to the above inscription of the
reign of Rājēndrachōḷa. It may be assigned to Rājēndra I on the basis
of a portion of the prāśasti which is available here.

tirumakaḷ pola perunilac celviyum [taṉa]ceka

inscription.head:
No. 106.
(A.R. No. 106 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This fragmentary record may be assigned on palaeographical grounds
to c. 12-13th Century. The exact date is not clear as the year opposite the
14th year is lost.

The record seems to mention the money deposited into the temple
treasury by a lady Śaṅkaranārāyaṉaṉ Tirunaṅgai for the purpose of some
offerings to the deity in the temple of Tirultaḷiyāṇḍār at Tirupputtūr, a
brahmadēya village. The donatrix mentioned here is identical with her
namesake referred to in another record of Parākrama-pāṇḍya discussed
below. (vide. No. 107 below) The details are not clear because the beginning
and the end of the lines are lost. Since the donatrix of this record also
figures in a record of the 20th regnal year of Parākrama-Pāṇḍya, the king
of the present inscription may be identified with him.

l.note: The beginning of the lines are lost.

l.ta:
var śrī paṇṭārattile nittam cūla
uḻa-

inscription.head:
No. 107.
(A.R. No. 107 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This record may be assigned to 12(13th century on palaeographical
grounds.

The record states that a certain Śaṅkaranārāyaṇaṉ Tiruvāṇḍāḷ, the wife
of Mōri Vishṇu Vishṇu, son of the kiḻār of Arugandūr, a hamlet of
Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu and her younger sister
Kuḻali deposited 56 śōḻiya-naṟ-paḻaṅ-kāśu to redeem some dēvadāna lands
from Maruṅgūr of the deity that had been probably pledged to the
mūlaparishai-mahāsabhai and to assign them in lieu of the said value for
the purpose of daily food offerings to the goddess paḷḷi-aṟaināchchiyār of
the temple of Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍār. It is mentioned that the rice supplied for
the food offerings is measured by śūla-nāḻi.

The Parākrama Pāṇḍya of this record may be identified with
Māṟavarmaṉ Parākrama-Pāṇḍya of accession 1143 A.D. with the praśasti
Tirumagaḷ Puṇara, etc., whose 12th year record is found at Tirupputtūr
(No. 131 of 1908).

l.ta:
kuḻaliya miviruvom tiruttaliyāṇṭār tiruppaḷḷi
aṟainācciyāṟku nittañ cūlanāḻiyā lirunāḻi tiruvaḻutarici-

inscription.head:
No. 108.
(A.R. No. 108 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This record may be assigned to c. 12th century on palaeographical
grounds.

It records the gift of 25 cows and a bull for maintaining a perpetual
lamp to the deity Śrītaḷi-Paramēśvara of Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in
Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu by Tirukkāḍikuḍidēvan, a veḷḷāḷa of Kūṟṟalūr in
the same nāḍu. The cowherd Vēndan-bhaṭṭan alias Tiruvīdikkōṉ took the
above gift and undertook to measure out a uḻakku ghee by śūla-uḻakku
to maintain a perpetual lamp in the above temple. The king of this record
may be identified with Jaṭāº. Śrīvallabha with Tirumaḍanḍaiyum praśasti
whose accession has been placed in 1101 A.D. He was a predecessor of
Māṟavarmaṉ Parākrama Pāṇḍya of accession 1143 A.D.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 246.

l.ta:
kku ney cantirātittaval aṭṭuvatāka ittevar iṭaiyuḷ
ventaṉ paṭṭarāṉa tiruvitit koṉkai-

k koṇṭa cāvāmuvāp perum pacu 25- m eṟute 1- m
[|*] itu śrī māhēśvara rakṣai [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 109.
(A.R. No. 109 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This record is dated in the 20th year of the reign of Śrīvallabha. It
may be assigned to c. 12th century on palaeographical grounds.

It records the gift of 25 cows and a bull by Amudiṟpiṟandāḷ, wife
of Mahāmātra Periyāḻvāṉ of Būdanūr in Marugal-nāḍu in Gēya-māṇikka-
vaḷanāḍu in Śōḻa-maṇḍalam for maintaining a perpetual lamp to the deity
Śrītaḷi-Paramēśvara of Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu.
Śūlanāḻikkāl is referred as the temple measure by which the cowherd
Chēramāṉ Jayaṅgoṇḍāṉ had agreed to measure out one uḻakku of ghee
to maintain the above endowment.

The king of this record can be identified with Jaṭāvarmaṉ Śrīvallabha
of accession 1101 A.D.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 247.

l.ta:
māttiraṉ periyāḻvāṉ
akamuṭaiyāḷ amutiṟ piṟantanāḷ vaitta tirununtāvi-

ḷakku oṉṟukku cūlanāḻikkāl uḻakku n[e]y cantirātittiyaval

inscription.head:
No. 110.
(A.R. No. 110 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This fragmentary bilingual record engraved in two scripts refers to
the king as the last son of Rājēndra of the Chōḷa dynasty.

It states that while the king was seated on his throne Abhimānarāmaṉ
in the western hall called Rājādhirājaṉ at Gaṅgaikoṇḍachōḷapuram, he
made a gift of the tax income from the villages Pottikkuḍi and Viḷakka-
maṅgalam, both in Chōḷa-Pāṇḍya-nāḍu in Rājēndrachōḷa-vaḷanāḍu in
Rājarājappāṇḍināḍu to the deity Tiruttaḷi-mahādēva of Tirupputtūr. The
gift was made on the advise of his mother when he saw the god in his
dream.

The throne of this Chōḷa king is in all probability identical with its
namesake at Tiruvāṉaikkāval which has figured in the record from
Uyyakkoṇḍān Tirumalai (No. 462 of 1908). The hall Rājādhirājaṉ seems
to be otherwise unknown.

inscription.head:
No. 111.
(A.R. No. 111 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in the Tirutaḷīśvara temple.

This epigraph dated in the tenth year and 205th day of the king
registers remission of taxes amounting to 43. māḍai on two hamlets for
providing worship to the god Tirutaḷi-Mahādēva at Tiruppattūr a
brahmadēya in Kēraḷasiṅga-vaḷanāḍu. At the request of Kāliṅgarāyaṉ the
king is stated to have issued this order while he was seated in the Paḷḷikkaṭṭil
called Pāṇḍiyaṉ in the hall Aḻagiyapāṇḍiyaṉ in the inner precincts of the
palace at Madurai.

An officer named Parākramapāṇḍiya-Uttaramantri figures in the
record and so a certain Parākrama-Pāṇḍya must have been a predecessor
or atleast a contemporary of this Śrīvallabhadēva.

l.ta: ṇai parantu ceṅkoluṭaṉ vaḷara maṉṉiya vira

ya pāṇṭiyaṉ kūṭattiṟ paḷḷikkaṭṭil pāṇṭiyaṉi
leḻunta-

inscription.head:
No. 112.
(A.R. No. 112 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the Agastyēśvara shrine in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

The details of the date of the record viz., Śaka 1452, Vikṛiti,
Dakshiṇāyana śu 8, Monday, Viśākha regularly correspond to 1530 A.D.,
August 1.

It records the sale of one mā of land made in the presence of the
mūlaparushai-mahāsabhai of Tiruppattūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-
vaḷanāḍu by a certain Bhāskara-śarman, son of Śrīsthalīśa-śarman, after
receiving the land value of 20 paṇam from Tiruvaraṅgapperumāḷ-
āvuḍaiyār, the kiḻār of Viḷaṅguḍi in Kulaśēkharapaṭṭaṇam, the city of
Aruviyūr in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu. The person who sold these lands is
stated to have received the same as gift from a certain Veṅgaḷa-nāyaka
on the occasion of a solar eclipse.

The kiḻār is stated to have invested the money on the purchase of
the lands for providing two sandhi lamps to god Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār
and goddess Śivakāmasundari and also for providing food-offerings to god
Vijayavanda-piḷḷaiyār of the temple. The purchaser is said to have set up
these two lamps for his own merit.

The donor Veṅgaḷa-nāyaka figuring in this record is probably identical
with his namesake who is mentioned as the father of Śiṅgama-nāyaka in
a record of Kṛishṇarāya from the same place (vide No. 91 of 1908).

inscription.head:
No. 113.
(A.R. No. 113 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the Agastyēśvara shrine in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

The details of the date of the record viz., Śaka 1457, Jaya, Dakshiṇāyana,
Kaṟkaṭaka, Amāvāsya, Saturday, Pushya-nakshatra, Vajra-yōga, Nāgana-
karaṇa regularly correspond to 1534 A.D., Jully 11.

It records the grant of the wet and dry lands in the village Varaguṇap-
puttūr, a dēvadāṉa village belonging to the deity Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyanār
of Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu, as kuḍinīṅgā-
dēvadāna and kaḍamai-kāṇi, to Pāṇḍipperumāḷ Kāliṅgarāyar, son of
Śaravaṇaip-perumāḷ, the accountant of the temple at Tirupputtūr. The village
Varaguṇapputtūr was originally granted as a dēvadāṉa to the deity
Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyanār by Gaṅgā-nāyakkar. Pāppukumma-nāyakkar, the
agent (kāryattuk-kaḍavar) of Viśvanātha-nāyakkar, son of Nāgama-
nāyakkar, the ūḻiyam of the ruling king, for the merit of Viśvanātha-
nāyakkar. The shepherd of the place Pōttukuḍi by the name Aḻagukōṉ
Pirayān had to collect the taxes from the settlements of the village which
is to be maintained as a dēvadāṉa-kuḍi of the deity Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyanār
and carry out the orders of the tānattār of the temple. The measure called
kūttāḍi is mentioned to measure out the 15 kalam of paddy for one mā
of land as per the Tirupputtūr measure for the wet lands in the aforesaid
dēvadāṉa village.

The boundaries of the gift village Varaguṇapputtūr Vēlaṅguḍi-kuḷam
and Vāṭpālaiṅgāḍu of the old dēvadāṉa village of the deity in the east,
Śēṉāvaraiyaṉ canal in the south and Vēḷārpaḷḷam in the east.

inscription.head:
No. 114.
(A.R. No. 114 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the kitchen in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This inscription may be assigned on palaeographical grounds to c. 13th
Century.

It records the gift of 2 nāḻi of rice daily to the deity Aḻaguḍaipiḷḷaiyār
in the temple of Kayilāsmuḍaiya-ṉāyaṉār of Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya
in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu by a certain Veṇṇivēlāṇ of Mutikaṭṭinallūr alias
Prathimādēvinallūr in Madurōdaya-vaḷanāḍu. The donor is stated to have
deposited 30 śōḻiya-naṟ-paḻaṅ-kāśu in the treasury of the temple of
Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār to meet the above endowment and the expenses
connected with the repairs of the prākāra (tirunaḍai-māḷigai) in the temple.

The king has been identified with his namesake who ascended the
throne in 1401 A.D. But the palaeography will miltilate against this
identification. Of the three Vikrama Pāṇḍyas who had the accession dates
1249, 1269 and 1283, the first date on which Vikrama Pāṇḍya I with the
praśasti Tirumalarmādu, etc., may be the king to whose 13th regnal year
our record can be assigned.

l.ta:
[i]kkācu muppatum tirunaṭaimāḷikai tiruppaṇikku •••••
••••• i śrī pa[ṇ]ṭāratte celvatāka ••• ••• ••••

inscription.head:
No. 115.
(A.R. No. 115 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the kitchen in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

The record may be assigned to c. 12th Century on palaeographical
grounds.

It states that the kitchen (tirumaḍaippaḷḷi), on the wall of which the
present inscription has been engraved was caused to be constructed in the
temple of Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍār at Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-
vaḷanāḍu by the queen Ulagamuḻuduḍaiyār, who is called muḍikkīḻ-
nambirāṭṭiyār of the said king.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 248.

l.ta:
ḷi ceyvittār ittevar muṭikkīḻ nampirāṭṭiyār
ulakamu[ḻu]tuṭaiyār [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 116.
(A.R. No. 116 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the kitchen in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This epigraph may be assigned to c. 13th Century on palaeographical
grounds.

It records an order (ōlai) issued by the mūlaparushan-mahāsabhai
exempting the maḍappuṟam lands of the maṭha called Tiruttuṇḍaittugaiyaṉ-
tirumaḍam from taxes (i.e., as iṟaiyili) like antarāyam, etc and for the
utilisation of this income as sabhāviniōgam for the reconstruction of the
same maṭha. It is said that the maṭha raised by Uttamaṉ, grandson of
Paḷḷikoṇḍāṉ Tirumuṇḍamaḻagiyār of Neḍuvāyil in Aḷagāpuram alias
Sōḻiya-Nārāyaṇapuram was caused to be reconstructed by the latter. Also
it is mentioned that the mahāsabhai purchased the timber and tiles removed
from the maṭha during its stone construction for the price of 20 paḻan-
kāśu fixed for the materials for the purpose of repairing the hall called
Dayāpañjaraṉ. A certain Neḍuvāyiluḍaiyāṉ Paḷḷikoṇḍāṉ.
Tirumuṇḍamaḻagiyāṉ of Aḷagāpuram alias Śōḻiya-Nārāyaṇapuram is
referred to have arranged for the removal of the wood in the above maṭha
situated in the southern wing of the southern street in the tirumaḍaiviḷāgam
of the temple of Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār of Tirupputtūr. For the maṭha
referred to in this inscription, a merchant named Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-
uḍaiyāṉ-uttamaṉ of Neḍuvāyil is stated to have endowed some lands as
maḍappuṟam in the 13th year record of Kulaśēkahara. These lands given
as maḍappuṟam in the record of Kulaśēkhara seem to have been declared
as iṟaiyili once again in the 17th year of the reign of Vīra Pāṇḍya. This
gives us a clue to suggest that the taxes seem to have been collected before
the 17th year of the reign of Vīra Pāṇḍya, for the reasons not known.
The sequence thus quoted helps us to fix this record latter to that of
Kulaśēkhara. From the palaeographical grounds and the mention of old
maḍappuṟam lands in the present inscription, the king may be identified
with Jaṭāvarmaṉ Vīra Pāṇḍya of accession 1281 A.D., whose records are
found in Tiruppattūr region. (Vide: A.R.Ep., 1936, para 56).

inscription.head:
No. 117.
(A.R. No. 117 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the kitchen in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

p.b: Tribhuvanachakravartin Vikrama Pāṇḍya (Māṟavarmaṉ Vikrama-Pāṇḍya) Year 12: 1281-82 A.D.

inscription.p:
This epigraph may be assigned on palaeographical grounds to c. 13th
Century.

It records the gift of money (dravyam) deposited into the treasury
of the temple of Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār by Villiuḍaiyanāchchi alias
Māṉatilatavalli, a dēvaraḍiyāḷ of Rājēndram for the daily food-offerings
to the deity Tirunāvukkaraśudēvar set up by her in the temple of
Kayilāsamuḍaiya-nāyanār at Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-
vaḷanāḍu.

The śrībhaṇḍāram has to supply 2 nāḻi of rice daily for the said
purpose. This was caused to be engraved on stone by the Śrīmahēśvara-
sabhai.

The place Rājēndram is mentioned elsewhere in a record of the 7th
year of the reign of Māṟavarmaṉ Vikrama-pāṇḍya with the praśasti,
Tirumagaḷ Jayamagaḷ, etc. and of accession date 1269-70 A.D. This record
refers to him as seated on his throne called Maḻalaiyadaraiyaṉ in his palace
at Rājēndram, east of Rāśiṅgaṉkuḷam. Also it is stated to be the place
of Kaṇḍiyadēvar. (vide A.R.Ep., 1924, part II, p. 109). On account of the
palaeography and the mention of the palace. Rājēndram in our record, the
king may be identified with Māṟavarmaṉ Vikramapāṇḍya of acc. 1269 A.D.

l.ta:
tevaraṭiyāḷ villi uṭaiya nācciyāṉa [mā]ṉa tilativalli
eḻuntaruḷuvit[ta]

yāṇṭa nāyanār śrīpaṇṭārattile oṭikkiṉamaiyil i
śrīpaṇṭāratte ||—

inscription.head:
No. 118.
(A.R. No. 118 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the kitchen in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

The record may be assigned on palaeographical grounds to c. 12th
Century. The details of the date of the record viz., year 3, Mithuna 12,
Sunday may correspond to 1165 A.D., June 6.

It records the gift of 32 paḻaṅ-kāśu deposited into the treasury of
the temple for maintaining a five-eighth (araiyē-araikāl) of a perpetual lamp
to the deity Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār by a lady Uyyakkoṇḍāḷ, the wife of
a brāhmaṇa named Mōśi-Gaṇapati Vishṇu, son of the kiḻār of Arugandūr
in Kulaśēkhara-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Śōḻapāṇḍiya-vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 119.
(A.R. No. 119 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the first prākāra in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This record may be assigned to c. 14th century on grounds of
palaeography. It is dated in the regnal year 46, Kaṟkaṭaka 14. śu 5, Monday,
Uttiram (Uttara-Phalguni) which details may correspond to 1342 A.D., July
8. However, the calculated date was actually the 11th solar Kaṟkaṭaka and
not the 14th as given in the record.

This epigraph records the declaration made by the mūlaparushai-
mahāsabhai of Tirupputtūr in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu, agreeing to give
annually a specified quantity of corn on each mā of the land and to confer
certain privileges in the temple of Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār to a certain
Periyanāyaṉār alias Vīrālayadēva, a member by the village assembly (avai)
of Śūraikkuḍi in Tēṉāṟṟuppōkku in Adaḷaiyūr-nāḍu in order to show their
gratitude to him. This Viśālayadēvar is stated to have reconsecrated the
deity of the temple and saved the people at the time when the temple
was occupied by the encamped Muhammadans (tuḷukkar) ‘whose time it
was’ and the village and the temple have been ruined. In consequance
of this, the inhabitants of Tirupputtūr become unsettled.

mavāramum peṟṟa uttirattu

aiyom atalaiyūr nāṭṭut teṉāṟṟuppokku cūraikkuṭi

ceytoṟum kuruppatāka ca

inscription.head:
No. 120.
(A.R. No. 120 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the first prākāra in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

The details of the date of the inscription viz., year 46, Mithuna 21,
śu 12, Sunday, Anurādhā regularly correspond to 1342 A.D., June 16.

This inscription records the declaration made by the administrative
officials of the temple of Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār of Tirupputtūr, a
brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu, to the effect that Periyanāyaṉ alias
Viśālayadēva, the avaiyaṉ of Śūraikkuḍi in Tēnāṟṟuppōkku in Adaḷaiyūr-
nāḍu would be entitled to receive certain amount of corn for each mā
of the temple land as tiruvāśal-pāḍikāval and the privileges. This was given
in order to show their gratitude to the recipient for his act of renovation
of the temple and the reconsecration of the deity consequent upon the
disturbance made by the Muhammadans during the time of their occupation.
The temple officials seem to have decided to confer the parivaṭṭam on
Viśālayadēva but latter preferred to give it to the common people of the
village which was accepted too.

śrī virapāṇṭiya tevarkku yāṇṭu 46- vatu midhuna

inscription.head:
No. 121.
(A.R. No. 121 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the first prākāra in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

The record is dated in Śaka 1460, Viḷambi, Āshāḍha śu 12, Tuesday,
Mūla-nakshatra, Māhēndra-yōga, Bāla-Karaṇa, Chātūrmāsya-vrata-
mahāpuṇyakāla day corresponding to 1538 A.D., July 9.

It records the grant of wet and dry lands at Kīḻainilai alias
Bhanavanallūr, part of Mañjālaippaṟṟu, in Poṉṉamarāpati-nāḍu in
Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu as dēvadāṉa for the worship and services to the deity
Bhairava in the temple of Āṇḍapiḷḷaiy-nayiṉār of Tirupputtūr, a
brahmadēya in the same vaḷanāḍu, by Periyarāmappa-nāyakkar, son of
Golla-Basava-nāyakkar of Aṇṇaṉ-ēndal and who is described as
svāmidrōharagaṇḍan, for the merit of the king. It is stipulated that in two
instalments (kandāyam) 40 poṉ per year as nattak-guttagai and the taxes
like kāṇikkai, pariśaṭṭam, nall-erudu and naṟ-paśu should be collected from
out of the gift lands. The boundaries of the gift lands are specified. Among
these mention may be made of the field called Māṉābharaṇaṉ, a dēvadāṉa
of the deity Kailāya-nayiṉār, Nelliśśai Tuvaramaṅgala-maṇṇāṇi-vāḻaikkuḍi,
paraiyar-ppuravu, etc.

The donor Periya Rāmappa-nāyakkar may be identified with his
namesake who figures in a record of Śaka 1462 (= 1540 A.D.), which also
refers to the division Poṉṉamarāpati-nāḍu to which the grant village
Vēlaṅguḍi-puravu is included. It may be said that the chief Rāmappa-
nāyakkar of the present record is different from his namesake who figures
in three other inscriptions dated Kollam 725 (A.R.Ep., 1916, No. 119), Kollam
727 or Śaka 1473 (Ibid., No. 428) and Śaka 1457 (Ibid., 1905, No. 129).

inscription.head:
No. 122.
(A.R. No. 122 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the first prākāra in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This record may be assigned on palaeographical grounds to c. 14th
century. The details of the date of the record viz., year 44, Dhanus 5,
śu 1, Mūla-nakshatra regularly correspond to 1339 A.D., December 2.

The inscription records the sale of dēvadāna, tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam,
kuḍippaṟṟu lands at Tirupputtūr executed, as pāḍikāval by the
mūlaparushai-mahāsabhai of Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-
vaḷanāḍu to a certain Avayaṉ Avayaṉ alias Māḷavachchakravarthi of
Śūraikkuḍi in Tēnāṟṟup-pōkku in Adaḷaiyūr-nāḍu, after receiving the sale
price of the right of 250 vāḷāl-vaḻi-tiṟandāṉ-paṇam from the latter. The
purchaser has to give ten marakkāl of paddy for one mā of land measured
by four foot span as pāḍikāval privilege. Āyirattu-eḻunūṟṟu-mūvēndiravēḷḍṉ
figures as a signatory of this transaction.

This record has to be placed prior to the date of the record No. 120
belonging to the reign period of the same king. Besides the earlier date
assigned to the present record, the sequence also supports it. In the other
record (No. 120) referred to above, the pāḍikāval rights were given away
to a certain Viśālayadēva out of gratitude for his meritorious deed. The
mahāsabhai would have resorted to his act because Māḷavachchakravarti
was in all probability not able to defend the onslaught of the Muhammadans.

inscription.head:
No. 123.
(A.R. No. 123 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the first prākāra in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This record begins with the praśasti Pūvinkiḻatti-mēvi-vīṟṟiruppa, etc.,
of the king.

It records the sale-deed executed in the name of deity Chaṇḍēśvara
by Tiruchiṟṟambalam-uḍaiyāṉ Poṟpadikkunāyagaṉ for the sale of kārāḻmai
(cultivators right) of the lands and the income from kaḍamai, antarāyam
etc., at Teṉvāyiḍaikkuḍi assigned to the deities Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nā[ya*]ṉār
and Viṉāyagappiḷḷai enjoyed from the days of his father to a certain Dēvar-
kaṇḍiyadēvar after receiving the sale value (viḷaipporuḷ), who assigned the
same for the purpose of food-offerings to the deity Tirukkāmakōṭṭam-
uḍaiya-nāchchiyār set up by him in the temple of Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār
at Tiruppattūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu. The inscription ends
with the phrase Śrīmāhēśvara-rakshai.

inscription.head:
No. 124.
(A.R. No. 124 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the first prākāra in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

p.b: Jaṭāvarmaṉ Vikrama Pāṇḍya Year 8+14: Śaka 1344 = 1422 A.D.

inscription.p:
The details of the date of the record viz., year 8+14, Śaka 1344,
Śubhakrit, Dhanus 19, śu 3, Wednesday, Tiruvōṇam regularly correspond
to 1422 A.D., December 16.

It records the sale of uḻavu-tavaṇai (right to cultivate lands for lease)
pertaining to the lands at Muḷaikkāḍu below the Muḷaikkāḍuchannel, a
dēvadāṉa of the deity Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār, north of Tirupputtūr and
the settlements in the west, that was lying below and not fetching the lease
rent due from Aḻagiya-nambiyār Pallavarāyar, to some persons including
Śeṇbagarāyar Parākrama-pāṇḍya Viśaiyālayadēvar Ariyanāyar of Śūraikkuḍi
in Tēṉāṟṟuppōkku in Adaḷaiyūr-nāḍu, for reclamation cultivation and
payment of the lease rent regularly, after receiving the price-value of 200
Āḍūr-paṇam from them by the Śrīrudra-śrīmāhēśvaras, śrīkāriyam-śeyvār,
māḍāpattiyam, Abhimuttīsvaram-uḍaiyāṉ and dēvarkaṉmi of the temple
of Tiruttaḷiyāṉda-nāyaṉār at Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-
vaḷanāḍu. A tax called kīḻvārapachchai which was collected for the above
lands previously was not collected later, probably after this transaction. The
transaction pertaining to the lease of the temple lands was attested by the
Vaḷḷalār, Aḻagapperumāḷ-nambi, dēvarkaṉmi Poṉṉa-baṭṭar and Abhimuttīśvaram-
uḍaiyāṉ.

The temple accountant Perumāṇḍāṉ Mayilēṟuvāṉ alias Brahmapriyaṉ
is stated to have drafted the sale-deed. The inscription gives in detail the
situation of the land and its boundaries.

inscription.head:
No. 125.
(A.R. No. 125 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the first prākāra in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

The details of the date of the record viz., year 23, Tulā 17 are not
sufficient for verification.

This epigraph records the sale of lands, ēmbal and kuṟichchi executed
in the name of the deity Ādichaṇḍēśvara (Ādichaṇḍēśvaravilai) to the
temple of Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār by the mūlaparushaṉ-mahāsabhai of
Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu. The above sabhai
is stated to have met in the temple and taken the measures to defalcate
the amount due from a certain Villiyāṇḍāṉ-Aḻagapperumāḷ and his younger
brothers of the place who did sin to the brāhmaṇas, who had engaged a
widow from outside for the family, who utilised the food-offerings, the
jaggery for the purpose of food-offerings to the deity Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-
nāyaṉār, who met the bhaṇḍāra-mudal out of the wages accrued from the
dēvadāṉa lands, who had taken away the trees felled in the dēvadāṉa lands,
who had redrafted the document by assigning to their own persons of the
lands that have been supervised in the village all along, etc. The remaining
portion of the record is lost. The lands owned by the above individual
and his brothers seem to have been confiscated on account of the atrocities
committed by them and sold by the sabhai as stated above. (Also see No.
130 B of 1908).

l.ta:
yom uṭaiyār tiruttaḷiyāṇṭa nāyaṉār

inscription.head:
No. 126.
(A.R. No. 126 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the first prākāra in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

The inscription is dated the Śaka 1309, Prabhava, Mēsha 2 śu 9,
Thursday, Pūsam (Pushya) corresponding to 1387 A.D., March 28.

It refers to the sale of the kāṇiyāṭchi of the office of the accountant
of the temple by the Śrī-rudra-śrī-māhēśvaras, Śrī Kāriyañ-cheyvārgaḷ,
māḍāpattiyam-śeyvāṉ, the śivabrāhmaṇas of thirty vaṭṭam and the tāṉattār
of the temple of Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyiṉār at Tiruppattūr, a brahmadēya in
Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu to the three persons viz., the temple accountant
Brahmapriyaṉ, Chokkanārāyaṇaṉ Kariyamāṇikkāḻvāṉ of Maṭṭiyūr alias
Nṛipaśēkhara-chaturvēdimaṅgalam of the above nāḍu and Vēṇḍavaḷandāṉ
Āyappiḷḷai of Śiṟuvayal in Tēnāṟṟuppōkku in Adaḷaiyūr-nāḍu. It states that
a certain Ambalatāḍuvāṉ alias Āyiratteḻunūṟṟu-mūvēndavēḷāṉ committed
a śivadrōha by managing to engage a smith named Āyiratteḻunūṟṟutaṭṭāṉ
and a stone worker (kaṟtachchṉ) named Alaivāy-ugandāṉ to forge the stone
records that would bestow upon him a house-site (maṉai) and certain other
privileges that were not there for him in the village. After committing
this crime he is mentioned to have ran away. The moment that the
śivadrōha committed by him was detected, both the smith and the stone-
mason were taken to custody before the kaṇmāḷar, who punished them
suitably and accordingly the prāyaśchitta was done to the temple.

It is further recorded that out of kāṇiyāṭchi right of the office of
the accountant, exempting half of the share of the right of Brahmapriyaṉ,
for the other half share, the price value was fixed for 300 Āḍūr-paṇam.
The above right and all other privileges were sold by the sabhai after
receiving the value of 300 Āḍūr-paṇam. This includes the 200 Āḍūr-paṇam
given to a certain Viśaiyālayadēvar as kaṟpūra-mudal and 100 Āḍūr-paṇam
given to the śrībhaṇḍāra of the temple.

It may be stated that the Viśaiyālayadēvar of the present record is
different from his namesake who figures in a record of 1342 A.D. from
the same temple. (See No. 120 of 1908).

inscription.head:
No. 127.
(A.R. No. 127 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the first prākāra in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

The details of the date of the record viz., Śaka 1309, Prabhava, Mēsha
2, śu 9, Thursday, Pūsam (Pushya) correspond to 1387 A.D., March 28.

It records the sale of the kāṇiyāṭchi right of the office of accountant
by the Mūlaparushai-mahāsabhai of Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in
Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu to the three individuals viz., Madhyasthan Brahmapriyaṉ,
the accountant of the village Tirupputtūr Madhyasthan Chokkaṅārāyaṇaṉ
Kariyamāṇikkāḻvāṉ alias Mahājana-priyaṉ of Maṭṭiyūr alias Nṛipaśēkhara-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in the above nāḍu and Vēṇḍavaḷandāṉ
Āyappiḷḷai of Śiṟuvayal in Tēṉāṟṟuppōkku in Adaḷaiyūr-nāḍu. It states that
the accountant Kulālaṉ Ambalattāḍuvāṉ alias Āyiratteḻunūṟṟu-mūvēndavēḷāṉ
got the records forged to the effect that he got it from the sabhai the
house-site and certain privileges in his village. He got the forged record
engraved on the walls of the temple by the stone-mason Alaivāyugandāṉ
of Tirukkōṭṭiyūr through the smith named Āyiratteḻunūṟṟuttaṭṭāṉ of
Tirupputtūr.

The villagers came to know of this grāmadrōha which he had
committed and thus he was handed over to the kaṇmāḷar from whom he
reaped the punishment. On account of this act, the Ambalattāḍuvāṉ-s share
of the kāṇiyāṭchi of the office of the accountant was brought to sale for
a value of 300 Āḍūr-paṇam. The kāṇiyāṭchi land sold as sabhāviniyōgam
yielded a value of 100 paṇam while the amount of 200 paṇam as
kaṟpūramudal was given to Viśaiyālayadēva. Further, the mahāsabhai has
bestowed on the purchaser the right of enjoying a house-site adjacent to
the site of Brahmapriyaṉ. The document was attested by the accountant
of the sabhai Madhyasthan Perumāṇḍāṉ Mayiteṟuvāṉ Brahmapriyaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 128.
(A.R. No. 128 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the first prākāra in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

The details of the date of the inscription viz., year 22, Ṛishabha 4,
śu 2, Rōhiṇi-nakshatra may be equated with 1275 A.D., April 29, Monday.
But the nakshatra ended on the previous day. The other equivalent is
Sunday, the 3rd May, 1318 A.D., taking the 4th Solar day as 8th day. (Ep.
Ind., Vol. XI, pp. 137-38)

It records the sale of uḻavu-tavaṇai (right of cultivating the lands) to
Poliñjuniṉṟapirāṉ Aḻagiyanambiyar alias Pallavarāyar of Muttūr alias
Uyyakkoṇḍaśōḻanallūr in Muttūṟṟuk-kūṟṟam by the śrīkāriyañ-cheyvārgaḷ,
the śivabrāhmaṇas who had the right of worship for a cycle of thirty days
(muppadu-vaṭṭam), the tānattār of the sthāna and Abhimuttīśvaram-
uḍaiyāṉ of the temple of Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār of Tirupputtūr, a
brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu.

The lands along with its income at Muḷaikkāḍu, a dēvadāṉa of
Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār located below Muḷaikkāḍu-channel and to the
north of Tirupputtūr were sold as uḻavu-tavaṇai, after receiving its value
of 1000 rāśi-paṇam which was taken to Vilaiy-āvaṇa-kaḷam. The purchaser
has to tell and give the lease amount. The tax kīḻvārappachchai which
was collected before, need not be collected again for these lease lands.

The sale document thus executed was attested by Perumāḷ-pādaṉ,
Sēṉaiperumāḷ-nambi, Avimuttīśvaram-uḍaiyāṉ, Nityasundara-bhaṭṭaṉ, Atarvēdi-
bhaṭṭaṉ, Āṇḍapiḷḷaiyār, Tillaiyāḷi-bhaṭṭaṉ, Maḻavarāya-bhaṭṭaṉ, Tirunāgīśvaram-
uḍaiyāṉ Āṇḍapiḷḷaiyār, Āṇḍapiḷḷai-nambiyār, Sundara-Pāṇḍya-bhaṭṭaṉ,
Tribhuvanasundara-bhaṭṭaṉ, Śivaṉ-bhaṭṭaṉ, Śaivarāśa-bhaṭṭaṉ, Māṟgaviśai-
Tirundapiḷḷai, Śaḍaikoṇḍa-vayirāgi, Tiruvīdippiḷḷai, and Ālāla-sundara-
vayirāgi. This was drafted by the temple accountant Āṇḍapiḷḷai.

Since the signatories viz., Perumāḷ-pādaṉ and Nityasundara-bhaṭṭaṉ in
this record and in a record of Māṟavarmaṉ Kulaśēkhara dated 1304 A.D.,
(Vide No. 133 of 1908) seem to be identical, it may be surmised that
Māṟavarmaṉ Kulaśēkhara and Jaṭāvarmaṉ Vīra Pāṇḍya of the above two
inscriptions are not far removed from each other in point of time. On
account of the proximity of dates and the mention of signatories, the king
of our record may be identified with Jaṭāvarmaṉ Vīra Pāṇḍya of accession
1297 A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 129.
(A.R. No. 129 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the first prākāra in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

p.b: Māṟavarmaṉ Sundara-pāṇḍya I ‘who gave Śōṇāḍu’ Year 20+1: 1236 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in the year 20+1, Kaṉṉi ba 6, Thursday; Pushya
which details may correspond to 1236 A.D., September 25.

The king in this record is described by the title Śōṇāḍu-vaḻaṅgi-aruḷiya.
It records an order of the king pertainig to the sale of kāṇi-paṟṟu lands
excluding the share of Kāṅgaiyar-kūṟu in the village Chakravarti-nallūr in
Paṉaiṅkuḍipaṟṟu of Tiruttiyūr-vaṭṭam as iṟaiyili-kārāṇ-kiḻamai and as
maḍappuṟa-iṟaiyili to Śrīkaṇṭha-śiva, an āṇḍār of Tirujñānasambandaṉ-
maḍam to the east of Śrītaḷi in the tirumaḍaiviḷḍgam of the temple of
Tiruttaḷiyāṉḍa-nāyaṉār at Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-
vaḷanāḍu by Māḷavarmāṇikkam Tirukkāṉaippēruḍaiyāṉ alias
Māḷavachchakravatti of Senpoṉmāri in Tāḻaiyūr-nāḍu. Śrīkaṇṭhaśiva is said
to have belonged to the mudalīs of Āchāramaḻagiyāṉ-maḍam, a santāṉa
of Teṉkuḷaṅgaraikkīḻai-maḍam at Selvat-Tiruvārūr. The sale of lands
included the income from several taxes obtained as kaḷḷar-kāṇi from Kaḷḷar.
Taxes like kaḍamai, kaṟpūra-vilai, achchu, and other kind of taxes of the
nāḍu, poṉvari, antarāyam, veṭṭi-muṭṭāvāḷ, kāṇi-ārāṭchi, ūrāṭchi, pāḍikāval,
ūrāṭchippēṟu, etc. are referred to.

Further it records the sale of land, made taxfree, to Śrīkaṇṭha-śiva
of the above mentioned maṭha, after receiving the price-value of 10 āṉai-
achchu, which was taken to viḷaiyāvaṇakaḷam by Māḷavachchakravatti. This
is stated to be the sale-deed pertaining to the above transaction and not
any other price-deed (poruḷmāvaṟudi) and it should not have error of the
order (ōlai), draft, oral, descriptive etc. Māḷavamāṇikkam Tirukkāṉaippēruḍaiyāṉ
Māḷavachchakravatti, who appears as the signatory was one of the persons
who executed the sale of lands to Śrīkaṇṭha-śiva. A number of other
individuals are mentioned as signatories. Among the boundaries of the sale
land, the record mentions the villages Mummuḍi-Chōḷaṉ and Kāḷaiyar-
kālanallūr. The office of tiruvāykkēḻvi is mentioned at the beginning of
the record.

inscription.head:
No. 130.
(A.R. No. 130 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the first prākāra of the Agastyēśvara shrine in the
Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This record is dated in the 2nd regnal year, Dhanus 11, śu 10, Wednesday,
Aśvati-nakshatra which details are not sufficient to final out the equivalant.

It records an order of the king at the instance of tiruvāykkēḻvi
regarding the sale of lands in the old dēvadāṉa village Kāḍavadarāyaṉ-
kuḍikkāḍu alias Māḷavakaṟpaganallūr in Kaṅgāṇip-paṟṟu in Kalavāyi-
nāḍu to a certain Aḻagaṉ Vāṉōrpirāṉ alias Vāṉavaṉ Brahmādarāyar, one
of the Āriya-brāhmaṇas, probably hailing from Śōnāḍu by Kaṇḍaṉ-
Udayañcheydāṉ alias Kāṅgēyaṉ. It states that the lands thus sold should
be brought under cultivation by the measuring span padiṉeṇ-sāṇ-kōl and
pay the taxes kaḍamai and antarāyam collected from the two crops
perumpū and kuṟuvai raised in the aforesaid lands to the deity Āṇḍapiḷḷaiyār
in the temple of Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār at Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in
Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu. The kārāṇmai made over to the Brahmādarāyar was
exempted from the previous taxes like achchu, veṭṭippāṭṭam pañjupili,
sanduvikkiragappēṟu and others. The ratio of the kaḍamai and antarāyam
to be paid for each crop are specified in detail.

Udaiyadivākara-mūvēndavēḷāṉ and Sundarapāṇḍyak-kāṅgēyaṉ are
referred to as the signatories of the order stated above.

inscription.head:
No. 130A.
(A.R. No. 130A of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the first prākāra of the Agastyēśvara shrine in the
Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

The details of the date of the record viz., year 2, Dhanus 11, śu 10,
Wednesday may be equated with 1218 A.D., November 3, f.d.t. ·14, in which
case it was Dhanus 3 and not 11 as recorded in the epigraph or with 1239
A.D., December 7. In either case, the king may be identified with one
of the first two Māṟavaṟman Sundara-pāṇḍya's.

It states that Poṉpaṟṟiyuḍaiyāṉ Moṇṇaippirāṉ Aḻagapperumāḷ alias
Viḻuppādarāyar of Pullūr, a naḍuviṟ-kūṟu of Miḻalaik-kūṟṟam purchased the
lands including nattam, garden, tank, etc., at Iḷaiyāttikkuḍippaṟṟu in
Kalavāyi-nāḍu as tirunandavaṉappuṟa-iṟaiyili from Kaṇḍan Udaiyañ-
Cheydāṉ alias Kāṅgēyaṉ, for the maintenance of the persons rearing the
flower garden called Maṉamperiyāṉ-tirunandavaṉam. The person who
purchased the above lands is stated to have maintained the garden from
out of the income from taxes such as kaḍamai, antarāyam, kaṟpūra-vilai,
kāriyavārāṭchi, veṭṭipāṭṭam, pañjupili, sandivikkiragappēṟu, marakkalam,
taḍippadakku besides the produce of different crops (as specified) raised
in the various seasons in the lands measured by 24 foot-span named
kuḍitāṅgi-kōl. The document was attested by the chief Kaṇḍaṉ Udayañ-
cheydāṉ alias Kāṅgēyaṉ and Eḻukaḻikāma-mūvēndavēlāṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 131.
(A.R. No. 131 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the second prākāra of the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

The record furnishes the date viz., Regnal year 12, Mithuna, Monday
which are insufficient for verification. However, it may be assigned to
about the 12th century A.D. on palaeographical grounds.

It commences with the king-s praśasti, Tirumagaḷ-puṇarap-pugaḻ-
magaḷ-viḷaṅga, etc. It states that the mūlaparushai which met at the temple
of Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍār at Tirupputtūr in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu gave an
undertaking to Uyyavandāṉ Kaṇḍadēvaṉ alias Dēvar Kāṅgēyar for the
endowments made in the śrīkōyil by the latter. It records the gift of
dēvadāṉa lands, the enjoyments pertaining to the śivabrāhmaṇas and the
vṛittis of the temple servants made as the capital (mudal) by him for the
conduct of services and like the gōpura outside, Naralōkavīraṉ-tirumāḷigai
in the centre, another gōpura, the tiruvaṇukkaṉ-tirumāḷigai and the
tirumañjaṉamuṟṟu in the circumambulatory passage (uḷḷālai); for the
coating to be made to the deity, for the food-offerings to be made on
the day of the Tiruppūśam festival and for the burning of lamp to the
deity Piḷḷaiyār (the image of which is kept inside), when it is taken out
in procession; and for the food-offerings, lamp, coating to be made to
the image Kshētrapāladēva on the day of Pūśam every month. A document
was executed by the mūlaparushai to the effect that the daily services would
be carried out from out of the gifts detailed above.

inscription.head:
No. 132.
(A.R. No. 132 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the second prākāra of the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

p.b:
Māṟavarmaṉ Kulaśēkhara Who assumed the title ‘emmaṇḍalamum-koṇḍaruḷiya’
Year 3[3] or [36]: 1301 or 1304 A.D.

inscription.p:
This record furnishes the details of date viz., Vaikāśi 10 which are
however, not sufficient for verification. It may correspond to 1304 A.D.,
May 4, if the regnal year is taken as 36. The record may be assigned
on palaeographical grounds to c. 14th Century.

It records a deed executed by the śrīkāryam of the deity
Ādichaṇḍēśvaradēvar in the temple of Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār, the
māḍāpattiyam Avimuttīśuram-uḍaiyāṉ, the śivabrāhmaṇas who had the
right of worship for 30 days cycle and the tāṉattār to the
Periyaṉāḻvāṇḍapiḷḷai, Kūttāṇḍap-periyanambi, etc., for the enjoyment of
the rights, since there is a lapse in the thirtieth year of this king when
the enjoyment of rights was withdrawn.

The above deed was attested by śrīkāryam-cheyvārgaḷ, māḍāpattiyam
Avimuttīśvaramuḍaiyāṉ and a number of other individuals whose names
are specified.

inscription.head:
No. 133.
(A.R. No. 133 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the second prākāra of the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

p.b:
Māṟavarmaṉ Kulaśēkhara who took the title ‘emmaṇḍalamum-koṇḍaruḷiya’
Year 36: 1304 A.D.

inscription.p:
The record is dated in the year 36, Vaikāśi 1, which details though
do not admit verification, seem to correspond to 1304 A.D., April 25. On
palaeographical grounds, the inscription may be assigned to c. 14th century.

It records a deed executed by the śrīkāryam of the deity
Ādichaṇḍēśvaradēvar in the temple of Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār, the
māḍāpattiyam Avimuttīchchuram-uḍaiyāṉ, the śivabrāhmaṇas who had the
right of worship for a cycle of 30 days and the tāṉattār of the tāṉam
(sthāna) to Akkanambi Aṭkoṇḍapiḷḷai alias Perumbaṟṟappuliyūr-nambi of
Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu. It is stated that earlier
in the 34th regnal year of the king, some of the authorities (probably of
the temple), had taken the jewels and the kāṇi lands, ēmbal and kuṟichchi
that was enjoyed by the Perumbaṟṟap-puliyūr-nambi illegally by putting this
person and the sabhaiyār inside the temple and cancelled as a sort of a
bond by mutilation (kāda(ra)ṟichcheydu) in order to convert the lands as
a mānya and thus the said jewels and lands were given to a Vaḷḷuvanāḍāḻvāṉ.
Then the dispute was taken up by Uḍaiyār Muṉaiyadarayar who got the
nambi and the sabhaiyār released, brought them before Perumāḷ with a
request to settle the issue. On enquiry, it was found that the above nambi
had not committed any crime and therefore (he) ordered for the land
compensation (pratikshētram) to be paid to Perumbaṟṟappuliyūr-nambi.
The lands given to him consist of 20 mā of lands inclusive of ēmbal and
kuṟichchi and the lands belong to the temple of Kayilāśāmuḍaiya-nayiṉār.
A number of persons including the śrīkāryam Perumāḷpādar ••• ••• ••••
māḍāpattiyam Avimuttīśvaram-uḍaiyāṉ, the inviduals for their respective
shares and those of the tāṉattār figure as signatories to the above document.

This Vaḷḷuvanāḍāḻvāṉ may be identified with his namesake who figures
in a record of Jaṭāvarmaṉ Sundara-pāṇḍya. (cf: A.R.Ep., 1909, No. 315).
This king seems to be a third king of that name.

inscription.head:
No. 134.
(A.R. No. 134 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the store room in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This incomplete record is dated in the 10th regnal year, Mithuna 7,
Magha-nakshatra. These details are not sufficient for verification.

It records a gift made by the mūlaparushai-mahāsabhai of Tirupputtūr,
a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu to the deity Sūryadēva of the temple
of Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉar. A certain Achcha-Sirikaraṇi is referred to as
a member of the Kaṉṉaḍiya cavalry group. Probably this person must
have come from the Karnāṭaka country.

l.ta:
yom uṭaiyār tiruttaḷiyāṇṭa nāyaṉār śrī
koyilil

inscription.head:
No. 135.
(A.R. No. 135 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the store room in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

It records an undertaking given by the mūlaparushai-mahāsabhai to
one Avanimuḻuduḍaiyār, wife of Dēvar Kaṇḍaṉ who endowed the amount
received from Sundaṉammaiyāḻvi as mūlaparushai-kaṇkāṇi dravyam for the
supply of four water pots for the sacred bath during the midday sandhi
to be conducted to god Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār of Tirupputtūr in
Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu. The Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple undertook to carry
out the daily supply of water-pots from out of the gift amount received.

cci ••• ••• •••• ṟuṭaiya nilam citevi mutalum muṉṟattoṉṟu
••• ••• •••• kiṇaṟu ••• ••• •••• ṅ

inscription.head:
No. 136.
(A.R. No. 136 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab in the first prākāra of the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This record of Māṟañjaḍaiyaṉ is dated in the 4th year opposite to the
6th year which must be taken as the 6th year opposite to the 4th year.

It commences with a Sanskrit ślōka which records the gift of ten dināra
for a lamp to god Śrīsthalīśa by Paṭṭā, the wife of Śaṅkara and the daughter
of Māra. The Tamil portion following the Sanskrit verse also records the
same in detail. It records the gift of kāśu for maintaining a perpetual
lamp to god Tiruttaḷipperumānaḍigaḷ of Tirupputtūr by Paṭṭa, daughter of
Māṟa, the wife of (brāhmaṇi) Mōśi Kaṇḍaṉ Śaṅkaraṉ, the son of the kiḻār
of Arukandūr. The gift was entrusted under the protection of a body called
Āyiratteḻunūṟṟuvar.
(Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 9)

inscription.head:
No. 137.
(A.R. No. 137 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab in the prākāra of the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

This inscription is dated in the 4th year and 4635th day of his reign.
This record carries the puḷḷi over the consonants.

It begins with the Sanskrit verse which records the gift of 15 kāśu
by Kāḍambavēḷāṉ for the temple of Śiva at Nūtanagrāma. It is stated
that a lamp was to be maintained in the above temple from out of the
interest accrued out of the gift amount.

The Tamil portion following the Sanskrit verse records the same fact
but in greater detail. It states that Maṟavaṉ Aṇukkap-pēraraiyaṉ alias
Kaḍambaṉ-vēḷāṉ of Perumāttūr in Muttūṟṟuk-kūṟṟam made a gift of 15
paḻaṅkāśu for a perpetual lamp to be maintained to the deity Tiruttaḷi-
bhaṭārar of Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Koḻuvūrk-kūṟṟam. It is also
mentioned that from out of the interest accrued out of 15 kāśu besides
a perpetual lamp to the deity, a nilaiviḷakku was also to be maintained.
This donor is also stated to have endowed 15 paḻaṅkāśu for the supply
of garlands to this Mahādēva. The sabhai of Tirupputtūr undertook to
supply 15 vaḍam daily, each vaḍam measuring a length of two muḻam, to
the deity from out of the interest accrued from the gift amount. The
garland is of the width of two fingers and carries two fresh flowers
(naṟumpū) at each point.

A body called Āyiratteḻu-nūṟṟuvar is mentioned. The word
Tirupputtūr is a Tamil form of the Sanskrit expression Nūtanagrāma. The
word is split into Pudu+ūr meaning ‘nūtana’ and ‘grāma’.
(Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 15)

inscription.head:
No. 138.
(A.R. No. 138 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the Aṅgāḷammaṉ temple.

This record is dated in a year which seems to be ‘four’ in numerals
in the reign period of the above king.

It registers a gift of 150 sheep for the maintenance of a perpetual
lamp to the deity Jalaśayaṉattu-bhaṭārar of Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in
Koḻuvūrk-kūṟṟam by Maṉōmayaṉ-Māṟaṉ, a kāvidi of Iḷaveṇbaikkalattirukkai
in Veṇbaikkuḍi-nāḍu. It was required to supply one uri of ghee daily for
the lamp. A body called Āyiratteḻunūṟṟuvar is mentioned as the protector
of the endowment. The word kāvidi is an official title which is being
applied to the officials both in the Pāṇḍya and in the Chōḷa Country.
Maṉōmayaṉ is probably a variant of the expression ‘Manōnmaṇi’, for e.g.,
Maṉōmayanīchchurattu used as the name of a deity in a record of the
2nd year of Māṟañjaḍaiyaṉ (See S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 17).

inscription.head:
No. 139.
(A.R. No. 139 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This damaged inscription is dated Śaka 1476, [Ānanda], [dvādaśi], which
details are not sufficient for verification.

It seems to record some gift to provide for the food-offerings to the
goddess Aṅgāḷamma by a nāyaka, whose name is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 140.
(A.R. No. 140 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the Niṉṟa Nārāyaṇa-perumāḷ temple.

This record may be assigned to the 13th Century on palaeographical
grounds.

It records an order (ōlai) of Kāṅgēyaṉ granting 6 mā of lands along
with income from taxes such as kaḍamai, antarāyam, achchuvari,
Veṭṭimuṭṭāvāḷ, echchōṟu, kūṟṟariśi and other iṟai-kuḍimai for the various
services including tiruppaḍimāṟṟu, for conducting a sandhi called
Vēṇḍaviṉiyāṉ-sandi after the donor's name and for the tiruppōṉagam to
the deity Kōlavarāha-viṇṇagar-emberumāṉ, at the instance of Teṉṉavaṉ .
•••

The grant land of 6 mā was converted as kuḍitāṅgip-paḍi from the
12 mā of lands measured by padiṉeṇśāṇ-kōl purchased by Veḍiyāṟṟūrār from
Pālpoṉṉaṅgainallūr of Paṭṭamaṅgalam in Tirunandiyūr-muṭṭam. A certain
Teṉṉavaṉ insisted that tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam lands should be given for the worship
of the above deity.

A number of signatories including Kaṇḍaṉ Udayañcheydāṉ alias
Kāṅgēyaṉ are mentioned to have attested the document.

The chief who is stated to have issued orders is known from a number
of records to have served under Māṟavaṟmaṉ Sundara-pāṇḍya I. (acc. 1216
A.D.)

inscription.head:
No. 141.
(A.R. No. 141 of 1908.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the central shrine in the Sundararāja-perumāḷ temple.

This fragmentary inscription commences with the praśasti, Pūmalaṟ-
tiruvum,* etc., of the king. The record may palaeographically be assigned
to 13th century.

It records an order of the king issued to the sabhai of Rājachūḍāmani-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Aḷa-nāḍu, to collect the taxes once
levied from the village, at the instance of Tiruvuḍaiyār, a māmatti of the
king from the 2nd year of his rule.

The document is stated to have been attested by Poduvakkuḍaiyāṉ
Viḻupparaiyaṉ of Poduvakkuḍi in the eastern division of Miḻalaik-kūṟṟam,
Āyaṉ Nilamaiyaḻagiyaperumāṉ alias Uttiramantri of Perumaṇaḷūr in Aṇḍa-
nāḍu and Araiyaṉ Viradamuḍittāṉ alias Pallavarāyaṉ of Chakkarapāṇinallūr
in Sevvirukkai-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 142.
(A.R. No. 142 of 1908.)
KUḶḶAPUṞAM, PERIYAKULAM TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the central shrine in the Sundararājaperumāḷ temple.

This fragmentary record begins with the praśasti, Pūmaruviya-
tirumaḍandaiyum, etc., of the king. The rest of the portion of the record
is lost. The inscription may be assigned to about the 13th Century on grounds
of palaeography.
The extant portion mentions his queen Ulagamuḻuduḍaiyāḷ.

inscription.head:
No. 142A.
(A.R. No. 142A of 1908.)
KUḶḶAPUṞAM, PERIYAKULAM TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the central shrine in the Sundararājaperumāḷ temple.

On palaeographical grounds, this fragmentary record may be assigned
to 13-14th Century A.D.

It records an order of the king issued to the sabhai of
Rājachūḍāmaṇich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam granting 4 mā of lands measured
by kaḍamai-iṟukkuṅ-kōl at Kaṇṇappaḷḷam in the above village as
nandavaṉappuṟa-iṟaiyili, for the flower-garden raised by Tiruvuḍaiyāḷ, one
of the queens (nam peṇḍugaḷ) of the king (whose name is lost), to god
Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷapperumāḷ. It is said that a Śrīvaishṇava of this temple
Niṉṟa-Nambi alias Tirumāliruñjōlai-nambi of Karāmbiṟchēṭṭu and his
descendants were to rear the flower-garden from the land grant received.

The name of the queen and the signatory Tirumalaiyuḍaiyāṉ alias
Chēdirājaṉ, who is referred to as the mantri (minister) of Perumaṇalūr in
Aṇḍa-nāḍu are identical with their namesake figuring in the 5th year
record of Māṟavarmaṉ Sundara-pāṇḍya ‘who took the Chōḷa Country’.
(cf: A.R.Ep., 1908, No. 148). Hence, our record may be assigned to the
same Māṟavarmaṉ Sundar-pāṇḍya i.e., Māṟaº Sundara-pāṇḍya I.

The record No. 142 A, refers to Tiruvuḍaiyāḷ as nam-peṇḍugaḷ. This
Tiruvuḍaiyāḷ has also figured in a record of the 5th year of Māṟavarman
Sundara-pāṇḍya, who took the Chōḷa country from the same place i.e.,
Kuḷḷappuṟam. (No. 148 of 1908). She is referred to therein as nam-peṇḍugaḷ
only. But in a record of the second year of a king (whose name is lost),
she is designated as māmatti. In the absence of any confirmatory evidence
except the praśasti, Pūmalar-tiruvum, etc., it is not possible to say whether
she is ‘the wife of maternal uncle’ of the king, and if so, it is not clear
why she should figure in a record of Māṟavarmaṉ Sundarapāṇḍya (I) as
nam-peṇḍugaḷ, unless it is interpreted as meaning ‘wife’ of the king in
question. In which case, it indicates that Māṟavarmaṉ Sundara-pāṇḍya I
was the maternal uncle of Māṟavarmaṉ Sundara-pāṇḍya II, which is hitherto
unknown. From this record, it may be inferred that Tiruvuḍaiyār was the
wife (queen) of Māṟavarmaṉ Sundara-pāṇḍya I. Her name continued to
occur in the reign of his successor Māṟaº Sundara-pāṇḍya II. The praśasti
Pūmalar-tiruvum, etc., found in the inscriptions from Aḻagarkōyil in Melur
Taluk of Madurai District (Nos. 9, 17 and 18 of 1931-32) and
Tirumālugandāṉkōṭṭai in Mudukulattur Taluk of Ramanathapuram
District (Nos. 36, 38 and 54 of 1931-32) has been attributed to Māṟavarmaṉ
Sundara-pāṇḍya II (See A.R.Ep., 1931-32, part II, para 22).

edition.l:
The beginnings and ends of the lines are lost. —ḷic ceyal [|*] irājacūṭāmaṇic catu[ rvvēti maṅka] lattu
sabhaiyāṟku [|*] aḻakiya maṇavāḷap perumāḷukku nam
peṇṭukaḷil tiruvuṭaiyāḷ ceyvikkiṟa tiruna[n*]ta
vaṉattukkut taṅkaḷurk kuṟuvai peru—

—[y]okam uṭpaṭa nantavaṉappuṟa i[ṟaiyi]liyāka iṭappeṟa
veṇumeṉṟu ivaḷ namakkuc coṉṉamaiyil taṅkaḷurk
kaṇṇaṉppaḷḷattile kaṭamaiyiṟukkuṅkolāl nālu
mānilam—

—viṉiyokaṅ koṇṭu ikkoyiṟ tiruppati śrī vaiṣṇavaril
karāmpiṟ ceṭṭut tirumāliruñcolai nampi makaṉ niṉṟa
nampiyāṉa tirumāḷikai ceytāṉ

—paṭi cantirātittavaṟ celvatāka kalliluñ cempilu[m*]
veṭṭuvittuk kuṭukka [|*] ivai aṇṭa nāṭṭup
perumaṇalūr mantiri tirumalaiyuṭai[yā]ṉāṉa cetirājaṉ
eḻuttu ivai kiḻ cempināṭṭu pālaṅ—
l.note: The rest of the record is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 143.
(A.R. No. 143 of 1908.)
KUḶḶAPUṞAM, PERIYAKULAM TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the central shrine in the Sundararājaperumāḷ temple.

This inscription purports to be a copy of the order (tirumugam)
referring to an iṟaiyili issued by the king (dēvar) in the month of Tai
of the above year.

It registers the grant of 10 mā of land, with 5 mā each for the two
flower-gardens Adigamaḻagiyaṉ-tirunandavaṉam and Añjādaperumāḷ-
tirunandavaṉam, as kuḍinīṅgā-tirunandavaṉappuṟam, to god
Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷapperumāḷ. The lands of 10 mā measured by āṇmai-
aḻagiyāṉkōl and the income from taxes like kaḍamai, iṟaivari, antarāyam,
paḷḷichchandam, pāḍikāval, vaṇṇakkak-kūli, peruvari, etc., as śēsha (balance)
were endowed for the rearing of the flower-gardens. The gift
tirunandavaṉappuṟam was made over to the Śrīvaishṇavas of the temple
of Aḻagiyamaṇavālapperumāḷ viz., Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷanambi of Iḍavai and
Iḷaiyāḻvāṉ Tiruviḷakku Nārāyaṇa-nambi of Vaṅgippuṟam. The lands thus
given, are located 5 mā below the tank of Kūḍalūr alias Rājachūḍāmaṇi-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Aḷa-nāḍu and the other 5 mā at Kaṇṇaṉ-kuḷam.
Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷa-nambi and Tiruviḷakku-Nārāyaṇaṉ are stated to have been
looking after the two gardens respectively mentioned above.

The document is mentioned to have been attested by Aḻagiyaperumāḷ-
[Chokka]ṉ alias Āṇmai-aḻagiyāṉ Duvārapativēḷāṉ, Nāyaṉ-Viṉāyaka-bhaṭṭaṉ
and Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-uḍaiyāṉ alias Duvārapati-brahmādirājaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 144.
(A.R. No. 144 of 1908.)
KUḶḶAPUṞAM, PERIYAKULAM TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the central shrine in the Sundararājaperumāḷ temple.

This damaged inscription purports to ba a copy of the king's order
(tirumugam) issued in the month of Tai in the above regnal year.

It seems to record the grant of lands including the tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam
lands of the god and the income from taxes for the persons serving in
the temple of Maṅgaladēva-viṇṇagar-emberumāṉ, the temple in the west
of the village [Rāja*]chūḍāmaṇi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Aḷa-nāḍu. It is
further stated that the gift was made as iṟaiyili for conducting the festival
named Āṇmaiyaḻagiyāṉ-tirunāḷ named after Āṇmaiyaḻagiyān, and for the
food-offerings made on this day instituted for the welfare of the king.

The document was attested by a number of signatories. Some of these
officials are referred to in a record of the same date from this place
discussed above (A.R.Ep., 1908, No. 143).

l.ta:

kālilum muṭiko

iṟukkavu[m] ••••• kaḷ ukavai oṉṟu pāti •••••

antarāyam ••• ••• •••• kāval vaṇṇa ••••• ūr

liyāka nirvāṟtut tantom au ••••• ṭikaik kolāl

ṇmai yaḻakiyāṉ tirunāḷāka eḻu ••••• kka[vum] ippaṭi
eḻuntaruḷun tirunā ••••• viñcaṉaṅkaḷ uṭpaṭa

uṭpaṭa ••••• eppeṟpaṭṭa pala viñcaṉa-

edition.l: yaka bhaṭṭaṉ tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyāṉāṉa tuvārapati Incomplete.-

inscription.head:
No. 145.
(A.R. No. 145 of 1908.)
KUḶḶAPUṞAM, PERIYAKULAM TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Sundararāja-perumāḷ temple.

This fragmentary inscription may be assigned to about the 14th century
on grounds of palaeography.

It seems to record a grant as poliūṭṭu to the deity of the Maṅgaladēva-
viṇṇagar temple probably at Kaḍūr Malai maṇḍalam. The details are lost.

l.note: The beginnings and the ends of the lines are built in.
The rest of the record is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 146.
(A.R. No. 146 of 1908.)
KUḶḶAPUṞAM, PERIYAKULAM TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Nīlakaṇṭhēśvara temple.

This inscription in characters of 13th Century refers to an order of
the king's official Aḻagapperumāḷ Śokkaṉ alias Āṇmai-Aḻagiyāṉ issued to
the tāṉattār and of dēvakaṉmis of Tirumarunduṟai-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār temple
and Rājarāja-Īśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār temple at Kūḍalūr alias Rājachūḍāmaṇi-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Aḷa-nāḍu in regard to the grant of income from
taxes on lands made as iṟaiyili. The taxes collected on the 31 mā, kāṇi
dēvadāṉa lands of the temples measured by Āṇmai-Aḻagiyāṉ-kōl include
ugavai, and peruvari inclusive of kuḍirnai-iṟai, pariykārāṇmai, puḷḷichchōṟu,
pāḍikāval, Vaṇṇagakkūli, etc., were granted as iṟaiyili and the paddy of
690 kalam was provided for the daily food-offerings as well as on festival
days, to the god, and for the viṉjanam to the nimandakkāṟar. The balance
of the produce was to be provided for the tiruppaṇi in the temples. The
share was to be made in the taxes collected thus.

The order was engraved by Appaṉ Kāḍaṉ alias Kodukula-āchāri, who
was holding the tachchāchāriyam in the temple. Of the taxes mentioned
in the record, a few are very interesting to note, for e.g., puḷḷichchōṟu
meaning ‘tax for the estimation of products’, vaṇṇagakkūli meaning ‘tax
for testing coins’. The term vaṇṇaga refers to an official tester of coins.
(See Tolkāppiyam, Cholladikāram 16, Sēṉa-)

l.ta:
§ aruḷicceyal [||*] aḷanāṭṭuk kūṭalūrāṉa irāja
cūṭāmaṇic catu

inscription.head:
No. 147.
(A.R. No. 147 of 1908.)
KUḶḶAPUṞAM, PERIYAKULAM TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Nīlakaṇṭhēśvara temple.
This record may palaeographically be assigned to the 13th Century.

It records an order of confirmation, evidently issued by the king in
the above year. It is stated that Duvārāpati-vēḷār gave a piḍipāḍu to the
dēvakaṉmis and the śivabrāhmaṇas of the temples of Tirumarudantuṟai-
uḍaiyār and Irāja[rāja*]-Īśvaram-uḍaiyār at Rājachūḍāmaṇich-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam granting the dēvadāṉa lands of 1(1/2) mā-kāṇi, measured
by Āṇmai-Aḻagiyāṉ-kōl, along with the income from several taxes (specified)
as iṟaiyili to the god for the services including tiruppaḍimāṟṟu and for
conducting a tīrtham festival in the month of Paṅguni on the day of the
(king's) natal star Uttiraṭṭādi, to the deity Rājarāja-Īśvaram-uḍaiyār.

The document is stated to have been attested by Sēvagattēvaṉ
Aṉavaratadāṉaṉ alias Māṉasēṉa-mūvēndavēḷāṉ of Īśaṉūr in Miḻalaik-
kūṟṟam and others.

The taxes specified are kaḍamai, antarāyam, puḷḷichchōṟu, pāḍikkāval,
vaṇṇagakkūli and others known to the ūr.

l.ta:
muṭaiyārum koyilt tevakaṉmikkum civappirāmaṇaṟkum
iṉṉāyaṉmāṟku tiruppaṭimāṟṟuḷḷiṭṭu vēṇṭum ni-

māṉaceṉamuventa veḷāṉeḻuttu ivai karuṅ[ku*]ṭi nā-

inscription.head:
No. 148.
(A.R. No. 148 of 1908.)
KUḶḶAPUṞAM, PERIYAKULAM TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the viṇāyaka shrine in the Nīlakaṇṭhīśvara temple.

This damaged inscription records the oral order of the king issued at
the instance of Tiruvuḍaiyār (his queen) to the dēvakanmis and the
śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple of Rājarājayīśvaram-uḍaiyār of Rājachūḍāmaṇi-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Aḷa-nāḍu exempting the taxes on Kārāṇmai lands
of 5 mā measured by Āṇmai-Aḻagiyāṉ-kōl in the village Muḍigoṇḍaśōḻanallūr
in the above nāḍu along with its income from kaḍamai, antarāyam,
veṭṭipāṭṭam, pañjupili, sandivigrahappēṟu, etc., for the various services
including tiruppaḍimāṟṟu, to the deity Rājarāja-Īśvaram-uḍaiyār.

The order granting the lands as dēvadāṇa-iṟaiyili was to take effect
from the 5th year of this king. The same was attested by several officers
including the mantri Tirumalaiyuḍaiyāṉ of Perumaṇalūr in Aṇḍa-nāḍu.
It was engraved by the tachchāchāriyaṉ of the temple named Ammai
Appaṉ alias Kodukula-āchāriyaṉ.

ṇṭaruḷiya śrīcuntarapāṇṭiya tevaṟku yāṇṭu añcāvatu

inscription.head:
No. 149.
(A.R. No. 149 of 1908.)
KUḶḶAPUṞAM, PERIYAKULAM TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up on the bank of a tank.

This inscription can be assigned to about the 13th Century A.D. on
grounds of palaeography.

It records an order issued by Añjādaperumāḷ Porkaiyaṉ Chēdirāyaṉ
for desilting the tank from out of the income from tax called pāśippāṭṭam
collected from Periyakuḷam in Rājachūḍāmaṇich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.

edition.l: svastistu Read svastyastu[||*]

inscription.head:
No. 150.
(A.R. No. 150 of 1908.)
MĀṈŪR, PALANI TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the Chōḷēśvara temple.

This inscription stops abruptly after the mention of the king's name.
Palaeographically, the record may be assigned to 13-14th Century. In which
case, the king may be identified with the third king of that name.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] vīrava(ā)llāḷatevar virācciyampaṇṇi
aruḷā tiruṉalliyāṇ
unfinished•

inscription.head:
No. 151.
(A.R. No. 151 of 1908.)
MĀṈŪR, PALANI TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the Chōḷēśvara temple.

This inscription may be assigned to about the 14th Century on grounds
of palaeography.

It records an order of the king granting 5 mā of lands along with
its income from taxes in the village Vīrarājēndraśōḻanallūr as iṟaiyili, for
providing food-offerings to the deity of the temple of Vīrarājēndrachōḷīśvaram-
uḍaiya-nāyaṉār in the above village included in Vaigāvūr-nāḍu.

The tāṉattār of the temple was to raise crops in the above lands and
conduct the offerings to the deity with effect from the 14th+1st year.

The taxes specified are oṭṭuachchu, ugavai, ārāṭchi-kaḍamai, kāṇikkai,
vāśalviniyōgam, etc.

l.ta:
vatti koṉeriṉmai koṇṭāṉ vaikāvūr
nāṭṭu ••• ••• •••• ntira coḻanallūr u[ṭaiyā]r
virarājentira coḷi

yār koyil tāṉattār innāyaṉāṟkku amutupaṭi
uḷḷiṭṭu veṇṭum ve[ñcaṉaṅ]kaḷukku ivvūril-
patiṉālā[vatu] varaiyum kaṇakkil paṭṭa nilam niṅkala-

iṟukkum oṭṭu accu ārāṭci kaṭa-

eppeyarpaṭṭa varikaḷum uṭpaṭa innāḷ mutal
iṟaiyiliyāka tantom ippaṭikku

celvatākak
kalliluñ cempilum veṭṭik koṇṭu aṉupavikka [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 152.
(A.R. No. 152 of 1908.)
MĀṈŪR, PALANI TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the Chōḷēśvara temple.

This record may palaeographically be assigned to about the 14th
Century.

It registers a grant of lands along with the income from taxes as iṟaiyili
to provide for the food-offerings to the deity Vīrarājēndrachōḷīśvaram-
uḍaiya-nāyaṉār. It is stated that three kāṇi of land to this god, kāṇi-araik-
kāṇi of lands to god Viṉāyakappiḷḷai and kāṇi-araikkāṇi of lands to the
goddess Piḍāri was provided from the 2nd year of his reign in the
Kuḷappaṟṟu at Vīrarājēndrachōḷanallūr in Vaigāvūr-nāḍu.

The royal order effecting the grant was issued to the tāṉattār of the
temple of Vīrarājēndrachōḷīśvaram of the reign of a king, whose name
is not given in the record.

l.ta:
vaṉac cakkaravatti koṉerimai koṇṭāṉ
vaikāvūr nāṭṭu māṉūrāṉa virarācentiracoḻa nallūr

ivvolai piṭiyāka koṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 153.
(A.R. No. 153 of 1908.)
MĀṈŪR, PALANI TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the Chōḷēśvara temple.

This incomplete inscription may be assigned to the 14th century on
palaeographical grounds.

It records an order of the king issued to the dēvakaṉmis of the temple
of Vīrarājēndrachōḷīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār at Māṉūr alias
Vīrarājēndrachōḷanallūr in Vaigāvūr-nāḍu granting 10 mā of land at Māṉūr
along with income form taxes (specified) as iṟaiyili, for the food-offerings
to the god Vīrarājēndra-Chōḷīśvaram-uḍaiyār. The boundaries of the gift
land are to the west of western entrance of the Piḷḷaiyār temple in the
east, to the north of vaḻitiṭṭal in the south, to the east of the central Taḻaikuḻi
in the west and to south of old dēvadāṉa in the north. Of the taxes
mentioned the tax called Kāliṅgarāyar-viniyōgam is interesting to note. This
was named after the chief Kāliṅgarāyar.

l.ta:
vaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ koṉerimel
koṇṭāṉ cavantāpāṇṭiya tevaṟku yāṇṭu

rāṉa virarājentira coḻaṉ nallūril uṭaiyār
virarājentira coḷi

ramuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyil teva
kaṉmikaḷukku nam o-

ram uṭaiyāṟku amutupaṭi cāttupaṭi palapaṭi
nimantattukkum mutala-

ṭaṅka iṟaiyiliyāka viṭṭa māṉūril viḻi iṭṭalil nām
kuṭutta nilam

melai v[ā*]calil piḷḷaiyār koyilukku meṟkum (te)
teṉpāṟkellai vaḻi iṭṭalukku vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai
naṭuvil taḻai kuḻikku [kiḻakku*]m vaṭapāṟkellai paḻan-

tevatāṉattukku teṟkum iṉṉāṉkellaikkuḷpaṭṭa nilam
pattumāvukku ekavai ukavai tirumuṉkāṭci iṭṭac-
cu[ṅka(ka)] vari kāliṅkarāyar viniyokam

kūṟṟilakkai tiruvācal viṉiyokam kiṟṟuvari mukam
p[ār*]vai antarāyam vaṭṭam k[ār*]ttikaipaccai kiṇokkiya
kiṇaṟu meṉokkiya maram eppe[r*]paṭṭa

inscription.head:
No. 154.
(A.R. No. 154 of 1908.)
PORUḶŪR, PALANI TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A broken slab lying in the prākāra of the Perumāḷ temple.

This inscription may palaeographically be assigned to about the
13th century A.D.

It records the setting up of an image of Kaṟpagappiḷḷai at the western
entrance of the village Pulariyūr in Aṇḍa-nāḍu and the endowment of one
achchu made for maintaining a sandhyādīpa to this deity by a person,
where name is lost. But he is said to be a native of a place included in
the division Pāṇḍi-maṇḍalam. A Śivabrāhmaṇa of this village and belonging
to Kauśika-gōtra is stated to have received the gift of an achchu and
maintained the lamp to the god.

inscription.head:
No. 155.
(A.R. No. 155 of 1908.)
DĒVATTŪR, PALANI TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a rock to the west of the village.

This damaged epigraph may be assigned to the 13th Century on grounds
of palaeography.

It records an order of the king probably issued to the people of
Dēviyartuṟai in Aṇḍa-nāḍu for the purpose of some constructions.
One Vikramachōḷa figures as a signatory of the above order.

inscription.head:
No. 156.
(A.R. No. 156 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This inscription in the absence of the praśasti, may be assigned to
the 10th Century A.D. on grounds of palaeography.

It records the gift of a silver plate for the purpose of food-offerings,
a sliver pot (kalaśap-pāṉai) and a chouri with gold handle to the deity
of the temple of Siddhēśvaram-uḍaiya-mahādēvar at Tirunaṟaiyūr, a
dēvadāṉa in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu by Uḍaiyapirāṭṭiyār Śembiyaṉ Mahādēviyār
alias Pirāntakaṉ-Mādēvaḍigaḷār.

This record which mentions the queen Sembiyaṉ Mādēvaḍigaḷ same
as Sembiyaṉ-Mahādēviyār is known to have lived long enough till the time
of Rājarāja I. Hence, the record under study may be assigned to the reign-
period of Rājarāja I. (acc-985 A.D.)
(Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 10).

l.ta:
varkku uṭaiyapirāṭṭiyār cempiyaṉ māteviyārāṉa
pirāntakaṉ m[ā]-

inscription.head:
No. 157.
(A.R. No. 157 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This incomplete inscription commences with the Sanskrit verse Ētad =
viśva-nṛipa-śrēṇi, etc., followed by the king's historical introduction
Tirumagaḷ-ōla, etc.

It records an oral order (tiruvāymoḻind-aruḷa) of the king issued to
the Śrīkāryam officer and to those who are looking after the various
services in the temple who inturn got the orders engraved for the grant
of 52 vēli of land made in a village (name not clear) in Arumoḻidēva-
vaḷanāḍu, a dēvadāṉa of the god of the temple of Arumoḻidēva-Īśvaram-
nāyanār in Kshatriyaśikhāmaṇi-vaḷanāḍu, to provide for the food-offerings
to the deities Maṇaliṅgadēvar and Anavadyadēvar, probably of the above
temple. The details of food-offerings made and the apportionment of the
rice for each services in the temple are given.
