The rest of the record is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 158.
(A.R. No. 158 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This fragmentary inscription commences with the praśasti, Pugaḻmādu-
viḷaṅga, etc., of the king.

The year of the record is lost. It gives the details of date viz., Regnal
year, 40, Kaṉṉi śu 10, Monday, which may correspond to 1109 A.D.,
September 6, the tithi, however ended at 98 on the previous day.

It refers to the vyavasthai made by the sabhai of Tirunaṟaiyūr alias
••••• dēvich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu in Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-
vaḷanāḍu which met once under the pipal tree called Rājanārāyaṇan and
granted lands as iṟaiyili to the diety Siddhēśvara.

The nāḍu is said to have been administered by Rājēndraśōḻa-
Brahmādhirājan. It refers to an announcement made by this officer. Other
details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 159.
(A.R. No. 159 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This built in inscription refers to the king Uttamachōḷa, as being born
to Pirāntakaṉ Mādēvaḍigaḷār alias Sembiyaṉ-Mādēviyār and registers the
grant of 6 vēli of lands for the food-offerings daily as well as on Saṅkrānti
days and for the sacred cloths (pariśaṭṭam) to the deity Siddhēśvaram-
uḍaiya-mahādēva at Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu. Also it records the grant of a tax-
free dēvadāṉa land in the village Mēlaḍavāy in the same nāḍu. It is
stipulated that the brāhmaṇa who performs worship in the above temple
should receive ten nāḻi of paddy, probably for his maintenance. The place
is referred to in the hymns of Tēvāram.
(Published in S.I.I., Vol. III, No. 149.)

inscription.head:
No. 160.
(A.R. No. 160 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the central shrine in the Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This damaged inscription may palaeographically be assigned to 10th
Century.

It records the grant of lands of arecanut gardens as iṟaiyili by a certain
Naṭṭaṉ Peṟṟāḷ, a lady, whose native is not known, for maintaining a perpetual
lamp and for the food-offerings to be made on the day of Aṉusha every
month, to the deity Siddhēśvaram-uḍaiya-mahādēva of Tirunaṟaiyūr, a
dēvadāṉa in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 161.
(A.R. No. 161 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the central shrine in the Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This inscription commences with the praśasti, Tirumagaḷ-pōla, etc., of
the king. Rājarājakēsari, who has been identified with Rājarāja I.

It records an undertaking given by the Śivabrāhmaṇas (names specified)
of the temple of Sidhēśvaram of Tirunaṟaiyūr, a brahmadēya in
Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu in Kshatriyaśikhāmaṇi-vaḷanāḍu, to supply 60 kalam of
paddy for the different daily fod-offerings to the deity Kaṅkāḷadēva set
up in the temple of Siddhēśvara, from the month of Vriśchika in the above
year. The paddy thus provided was the interest (paliśai) collected on 30
kāśu, endowed by Nāraṇan Ambalam-uḍaiyān who belonged to Ātrēya-
gōtra and a resident of Amudamaṅgalam in the said nāḍu, a māṇi (dwarf)
of the temple of Rājarājadēvar.

inscription.head:
No. 162.
(A.R. No. 162 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the central shrine in the Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This epigraph in the absence of a praśasti of the king may be assigned
to Rājēndra I of accession 1012 A.D. on the basis of palaeography.
Palaeographically, the record may be assigned to about the first quarter
of the eleventh century A.D.

It records a gift of [12] sheep for a sandhi lamp to the deity
Kaṅkāḷadēva in the temple of Siddhēśvara by a lady named Ādaṉ-aḍiyāḷ,
wife of Nallāṉaḍigaḷ alias Tanichchēvagappornāyaṉ, a vellāḷa of
Tirunaṟaiyūr a brahmadēya in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu in Kshatriyaśikhāmaṇi-
vaḷanāḍu. A shepherd (maṉṟāḍi) Sāttaṉ-bhaṭṭaṉ undertook the gift and he
was to supply annually 12 nāḻi of ghee measured by Siddaṉ at the rate
of one nāḻi per month and araippiḍi daily to the temple.

l.ta:
••••• ivvāṭu pa ••• ṇṭu koṇṭu ivvūr irukkum
maṉṟāṭi cāttaṉ paṭṭa-
ṉ nittam araippiṭi neyāka tiṅkaḷ ney nāḻi
lu ••••• ṭṭak kaṭava ney paṉniru nāḻi •••••

l.note: The ends of the lines are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 163.
(A.R. No. 163 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This inscription commences with the praśasti Pūmādu-puṇara, etc., of
the king. The details of the date of the record viz., regnal year 3, Mithuṉa,
śu 12, Thursday and Aṉiḻam (Anusha) regularly correspond to 1120 A.D.
June 10.

It registers a sale of iraṇḍēmukkāṇik-kīḻ-mukkāle-mūṉṟu-mākkāṇi of
land for 10 kāśu which was deposited into the temple treasury by Pichchan
Vannāyagan Paḻandivarājar of Karuppūr in Maṇṇi-nāḍu in Virudarāya-
bhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu, to the Ādichaṇḍēśvaradēvar, the mūlabhṛityas of the
temple of Siddēśvaram-uḍaiyār of Pañchavan-mahādēvi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam,
a brahmadēya in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu in Kōlōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu by the
mahāsabhai of Mummuḍiśōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in
Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu in Bhūpālakulavalli-vaḷanāḍu. The mahāsabhai is stated
to have given the land as iṟaiyili, for 122 kāśu received from the
Ādichaṇḍēśvaradēvar and undertook to pay the taxes like peruvari, śilvari,
tiruvāśaliṟpōnda-kuḍimai etc., on the lands. Since the 6th year of
Kulōttuṅgachōḷa, to the day (till the date of the record), the uḍaiyār brought
the lands under cultivation but failed to pay the taxes. In the event of
this the sabhai raised crops, collected the taxes, provided for the feeding
of the apūrvi-śrīmāhēśvaras, who came to worship the deity Siddhēśvaram-
uḍaiyār and granted it as maḍappuṟam to them.

The above transaction was made by the mahāsabhai of Mummudiśoḻa-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam which met in the temple of Mādari-viṇṇagar-Āḻvār.

It is interesting to note that Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu was included both in
Kōlōttuṅgaśoḻa-vaḷanāḍu and Bhūpālakulavalli-vaḷanāḍu. We know that,
after whom the division was named, was perhaps only the queens of
Vikramachōḷa. The earliest name of the division which should have been
coined during the time of Kulōttuṅga I continued to be called in the days
of his successor Vikramachōḷa also.

inscription.head:
No. 164.
(A.R. No. 164 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This inscription commences with the praśasti Pūmādu-puṇara, etc., of
the king. The details of the date of the record viz., year 3, Mithuṉa śu
6, Friday and Pūram regularly correspond to 4th June, 1120 A.D.

It registers the grant of lands as maḍappura-iṟaiyili to the
Ādichaṇḍēśvaradēvar, the mūlabhṛityas of the deity by the mahāsabhai of
Tirunaṟaiyūr alias Pañchavaṉmādēvich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Tirunaṟaiyūr-
nāḍu in Kulōttuṅgaśoḻa-vaḷanāḍu which met in the temple of Siddhēśvaram-
uḍaiyār. The lands of araiyē-oru-mā-mukkāṇiy-araikkāṇi-mundirigai
purchased from Bhaṭṭaṉ Dakshiṇāmūrti and his younger brothers of Mogili
(ie., Maudgalya-gōtra) by the mahāsabhai for a value of 40 kāśu, deposited
into the treasury by Pichchan Vannāyagaṉ alias Paḻandivarājar of Karuppūr
in Maṇṇi-nāḍu in Virudarājabhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu were endowed as
maḍappuṟa-iṟaiyili for the purpose of feeding the apūrvva-śrīmāhēśvaras
who came to worship the deity Siddhēśvaram-uḍaiyār. The taxes such as
peruvari, śilvari, tiruvāśalikoṇḍa-kuḍimai, were to be collected by the sabhai
itself. The Śrīmāhēśvaras of the temple agreed to carry out the endowment
by undertaking the lands given as iṟaiyili. A number of signatories are stated
to have attested the iṟaiyili document.

It is evident from the present and the previous records both dated
in the 3rd year that this year started earliest then by 4th June 1120 A.D.
Therefore the accession of the king must have been on or before 4th June
1118 A.D. and not on or about the 29th June 1118 A.D. as stated by Kielhorn
(see Ep. Ind., Vol. VII, pp 4-5)

inscription.head:
No. 165.
(A.R. No. 165 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This inscription commences with the praśasti, Puyalvāyttu-vaḷam-
peruga, etc., of the king.

The details of the date of the record viz., year 5, Vṛiśchika, śu 3,
Saturday, Uttirāḍam may be equated with 24th November 1150 A.D.
However, the weekday was Friday.

It records the gift of 200 kāśu for maintaining a perpetual lamp to
the deity Siddhēśvaram-uḍaiyār at Tirunaṟaiyūr in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu in
Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanādu by Viḻupparayaṟ Madurāntakan of Maṇṇaikkudi
in Maṇṇaikkuḍi-nāḍu. The poliyūṭṭu amount specified above was received
by the Śivabrāhmaṉas, who had the right of worship for a cycle of 30
days in this temple, undertook to supply one uḻakku of oil daily to maintain
the gift. The names of the Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple are mentioned
in the record.

inscription.head:
No. 166.
(A.R. No. 166 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This inscription gives the details of the date viz., year 2, Kuṁbha 21,
ba 1, Wednesday, Pūram which regularly corresponds to 1150 A.D., February
13.

It records the gift of 100 kāśu for maintaining a perpetual lamp to
god Siddhīśvaram-uḍaiyār of [Tirunaṟaiyūr] in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu in
Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu by Ekavāchagaṉ Kulōttuṅgaśōḻaṉār alias
Vāṇakōvaraiyar of a place (name lost) included in Tuṉḍa-nāḍu in
Muḍigoṇḍaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. The Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple undertook the
poliyūṭṭu amount and agreed to maintain the lamp.

inscription.head:
No. 167.
(A.R. No. 167 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This inscription commences with the praśasti, Pūmādu-puṇara-
puvimādu-vaḷara, etc., of the king. The details of the date of the record
viz., year 12, Ṛishabha śu 6, Thursday, Magha-nakshatra regularly correspond
to 1130 A.D., May 15, f.d.n. 26.

It records the assignment of 6 mā of land including the dēvadāṉa
lands to meet the expenses of the different worship and food-offerings to
the deity Kūttāḍumdēvar by the śrīmāhēśvara-kaṇkāṇi-cheyvārgaḷ,
dēvakaṉmis, śrīkāryam-cheyvārgaḷ śrībhaṇḍāra-kaṇkāṇi-cheyvārgaḷ and the
paṉmāhēśvaras of the temple of Siddhīśvaram-uḍaiyār of Pañchavanmādēvich-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu in Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, for
7 kāśu and the arrears of interest (paliśai) amount of 10 kāśu on this
obtained from Kōdaiy Irāmaṉ of Veṇpula-nāḍu. In exchange (parivarttaṉai)
for the bits of iṟaiyili lands of the village from Koḍumalaiyāḷaṉ Kōdaiy
Irāmaṉ, the six mā of lands lying to the east of Pavitramāṇikkaval and
to the north of Rājēndraṉ-vāykkāl was given.

The order (niyōgam) relating to this parivarttaṉai was drafted by the
temple accountant uḍaiyaṉ Maṇḍaiyaṉ, who is a karaṇattāṉ and native of
Pērāvūr and Mādaṉamaṅgalam. It was attested by śivavēdiyār-kaṇkāṇi
Parasamaiya Gowrinambi, dēvakanmi Nārpatteṇṇāyira-bhaṭṭan and the
śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple who figure as signatories.

inscription.head:
No. 168.
(A.R. No. 168 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This record begins with the king's praśasti, Puyal-vāyttu and the same
is not continued further as in the records of Kulōttuṅga III.

The details of the date viz., 2nd year, Mithuṉa śu 9, Monday, Hasta
are irregular. It may be assigned to 12th Century on palaeographical grounds.

It records a deed of agreement pertaining to the sale of lands (nilavilaip-
pramāṇa-iśaivutīṭṭu) given by Kaḻukaṇdāl-nāyakattēvaṉ alias Ariyāṉ, his
younger brother Andiyum Pagaḻumāḍuvāṉ, Kāḍaṉ and their sister
Śivaṉamuḻudum-uḍaiyāḷ, her younger sister Selvāṇḍāḷ and their mother
Maṉṟēṟi-ōlaganār to the Ādichaṇḍēśvara-dēvakaṉmis of the temple of
Akkāḷīśvaram-uḍaiyār of Tirunaṟaiyūr alias Pañchavaṉmādēvi-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu in Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu and
registers the sale of oru-mā araikkānik-kīḻ-mukkāliṉ-kīḻ mūṉṟu-mā lands
made over to the deity as tirunāmattukkāṇi, for the price-value of 6000
aṉṟāḍu-naṟkāśu and against a surety (māttu) given by Nigaḻum-vēḷāṉ of
Kāñchivāyil. The lands thus sold were once given as iṟaiyili to the deity
by the sabhai of Tirunaṟaiyūr alias Pañchavaṉmādēvich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.
Among the boundaries of the lands are mentioned Pavuttiramāṇikkavadi
and Rājēndraśōḻaṉ-vāykkāl. Besides, the lands at Kulōttuṅganallūr,
Rājarājamaṅgalam, Rājarāja Āṇḍanalūr and the tirunāmattukkāṇi lands of
the deity Siddhīśvaram-uḍaiyār are said to have been included in the sale
ones. Two others named Sōmaṉātha-paṇḍitaṉ and Māḷigai Kaḻakaṇḍār
Śōḻaṉāyagam Irājēndraśōḻa Viḻupparāyaṉ are stated to have signed in the
absence of the person who acted as surety for the deed. The price value
of 6000 kāśu was entrusted to Tāḷattāl-āḍuvaraṅga-nāyagadēvaṉ. The record
was engraved by Nāḍaṟipugaḻ-āchāri.

l.ta:
ra coḻatevaṟkku yāṇṭu iraṇṭāvatu
mituṉanāyaṟṟu pū

peṟṟa attattu nā[ḷ] kulottuṅkacoḻavaḷa nā[ṭ*]ṭu

inscription.head:
No. 169.
(A.R. No. 169 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This inscription may be assigned to about the 12th century A.D., on
grounds of palaeography.

The record registers the gift of one kāśu deposited into the temple
treasury for providing a sandhi lamp to the deity by Sūriyadēvan of
Panaṅguḍi in Paṉaiyūr-nāḍu in Kōlōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. The lamp was to
be maintained from the vṛiddhi accured from out of one kāśu endowed.

l.ta: ka coḻavaḷanāṭṭu paṉaiyūr nāṭṭu panaṅkuṭaiyān cūriya
ṭārattu oṭukkiṉa kācu onṟu ikkācāl vanta

inscription.head:
No. 170.
(A.R. No. 170 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This inscription commences with the praśasti, Pūmādu-puṇara, etc., of
the king.

The details of date of the record viz., 2nd year, Mithuṉa śu 12, Monday,
Viśākha are irregular as the tithi and nakshatra in the given month fall
on Thursday, 10th June, 1120 A.D. or, if the regnal year 2 is taken as a
mistake for 3, then the details would correspond to 1120 A.D., May 30.

The record states that the mahāsabhai of Tirunaṟaiyūr alias
Pañchavanmādēvich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu in
Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu met under (the tree) Rājanārāyaṇan-araiśu and
made a sale of land as iṟaiyili to the Ādichaṇḍēśvaradēva of the temple
of Siddhīśvaram-uḍaiya-mahādēva of the above village.

During the 2nd year of the king, the expenditure of the sabhai was
spent in excess of the ūrmāviyanda-kāśu (i.e., a tax amount payable to the
king) and as further taxes could not be imposed on the village for the deficit
amount, the sabhai sold the lands (specified) as iṟaiyili to the
Ādichaṇḍēśvaradēva, in lieu of 34 kāśu got from the temple treasury. They
have also agreed to remit the taxes on these lands. A number of signatories
are referred to in this record.

inscription.head:
No. 171.
(A.R. No. 171 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This epigraph is engraved in continuation of the previous inscription
(No. 170 of 1908). The details of the date of the record viz., year 3, Mithuṉa
8, Thursday and Uttiram reqularly correspond to 1121 A.D., May 26. In the
absence of the regular praśasti, this record may be assigned to about the
12th century A.D., on grounds of palaeography.

It records the sale of 37 vēli of lands as tirunāmattukkāṇi, for 700 kāśu
received from the temple. The mahāsabhai of Tirunaṟaiyūr alias
Pañchavanmādēvich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu in
Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu is stated to have met at Eḻunūṟṟuvaṉ and made
over the lands as iṟaiyili, transacted in favour of Ādichaṇḍēśvaradēvar of
the temple of Aḻagiya Siddhīśvaram-uḍaiyūr of the village, for they spent
in excess of the maravinda-kāśu (a tax payable to the king) and could not
impose additional tax on the village, for the deficit of amount incurred
by them. Among the boundaries of the lands thus sold for this reason, are
mentioned the area to the east of the vadi through which the car (temple
car) proceeds and to the north of Rājēndran-vāykkāl. The document was
attested by Kēśava-bhaṭṭan, Kēśavan and Mādhavan.

A similar transaction is mentioned in the previous record. (A.R.Ep., 1908,
No. 170).

inscription.head:
No. 172.
(A.R. No. 172 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This inscription which is partly built in, commences with the praśasti,
Kaḍal-śūḻnda-pār-ēḻun, etc., of the king. The details of date viz., year 6, Mīṉa
śu 6, Thursday, Rōhiṇī correspond to 6th March, 1169 A.D.

It seems to record an undertaking given to one Kavi ••••• Arayaṉ
Periyāṉ, a Śivabrāhmaṇa of the temple of Siddhīśvaram-uḍiyār at
Tirunaṟaiyūr in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu in Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu for the gift
received from him, for maintaining a sandhi lamp in this temple.

inscription.head:
No. 173.
(A.R. No. 173 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This incomplete inscription commences with the praśasti, Pūmaruviya-
tirumādum, etc., of the king.

It records the grant of taxable lands at Tirunaṟaiyūr alias
Pañchavaṉmādēvi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam and at Ediriliśōḻamaṅgalam for the
worship and offerings to the deity Siddhīśvaram-uḍaiyār of Tirunaṟaiyūr
in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu in Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. It was probably entrusted
to the dēvakaṉmi and the śrīmāhēśvara-kaṇkāṇi-cheyvār of the temple. One
of the lands thus given belong to the kāṇi of Vikramaśōḻa-brahmārāyaṉ.

etirili coḻamaṅkalattu tiṟappu nilattu ittevar ūrkkiḻ
iṟai-

pottakapaṭi nilam araiye nālumāvaraiyiṉ kiḻ

uyyakkoṇṭār vaḷanāṭṭu
l.note: Rest of the record is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 174.
(A.R. No. 174 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This incomplete epigraph commences with the praśasti, Pūmādu-
puṇara, etc., of the king.

It records the grant of iṟaiyili lands by Sembiyaṉ Mādēvi-pirāṭṭiyār
for the mid-day worship of the deity Vaḍavāyil Mātta-piḍāriyār of
Pañchavaṉmādēvich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu in
Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. The karaṇi-gaṇattār, evidently of the village,
undertook to supply 7 nāḻi of paddy and to maintain the endowment. The
lands endowed as iṟaiyili was the capital for the said offering and the
karaṇattāṉ of the village Mādanamaṅgalam-uḍaiyān Dēvaṉ Ādimūrtthi was
empowered to manage the gift as a representative (magaṉ) of karaṇi-gaṇattār.
He has to pay the taravu for the antarāyam collected annually on these
lands and that after him, his descendants and those who held the office
should pay the same.

l.ta:
pūmātu puṇara puvimātu vaḷara nāmātu
viḷaṅka jayamātu nilava tantiruppatu malar mannavar cūṭa
manniya urimaiyāl maṇimuṭi cūṭi ceṅkOl ceṉṟu
ticaitoṟum vaḷaṟppa veṅkali niṅki meyyaṟan taḻaippak
kaliṅka miriya kaṭalmalai naṭātti valaṅkoḷāḻi varaiyāḻi
naṭātti iricuṭaraḷavum oru kuṭainiḻaṟṟa vira

tiripuvaṉa muḻutuṭaiyāḷoṭum viṟṟiruntaruḷiya koppara
kecaripanmarāna tiripuvanaca[k*]karavattikaḷ śrīvikkiramacoḻa
tevaṟkku yāṇṭu

cantirātittavaṟ celvatāka ucci

rukiṟapaṭikku meleṟṟi amutupaṭikku arici irunāḻikkum
kaṟiyamutu neyyamutu tayiramutu uppamutu miḷakamutu
aṭaikkāyamutu ilaiyamutu tiruviḷakku uḷpaṭa

oṉṟukku nellu eḻunāḻiyāka ēṟṟi karaṇikaṇattāre
iṉnima[n]tañ celuttak kaṭavārkaḷākavum innimantattukku
uṭalāka ivvūr karaṇattāṉ mātāṉa maṅkalamuṭaiyān
tevaṉ ātimurtti ikkaraṇikaṇattārkku makanāy
ippiṭāriyarkku iṟaiyiliyāka cempiyaṉ mātevip
pirā[ṭ]-

ṉ vāykkālukku vaṭakku mutaṟkaṇṇāṟṟu
mutaṟ catirattu nilaṅkāle mukkāṇic ciṉnattukkum oṭṭina

rāyam āṭṭāṇṭu toṟum ivan taṉ kaṇak[ku]
jivitattile kuṇṭikaiyilevaittu taravukoḷḷak
kaṭavutākavum ivaṉukku piṉpu ivaṉ vaṟkattārum maṟṟum
iyarvācil

inscription.head:
No. 175.
(A.R. No. 175 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

The details of the date of the record viz., year 2, Miṉa, śu 1, Thursday
and Uttiraṭṭādi correspond to 1180 A.D., February 28.

It records the gift of gold by Nārāyaṇan Sūryadēva-bhaṭṭan of Kāśyapa-
gōtra and a resident of Tirunaṟaiyūr, for maintaining a perpetual lamp to
the deity Siddhīśvaram-uḍaiyār of Tirunaṟaiyūr in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu in
Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. The Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple who had the
right of worship for a cycle of 30 days undertook the gift and agreed to
maintain the lamp from out of the interest of 120 kāśu on the gold.
The names of the Śivabrāhmaṇas are mentioned in the record.

l.ta:
vatu miṉanāyaṟṟu pūrvapakṣattu
piratamaiyum viyāḻakkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa uttiraṭṭāti nāḷ
kulottuṅka coḻavaḷanāṭṭu tirunaṟaiyūrnāṭṭu
tirunaṟaiyūr uṭaiyār citticuramuṭaiyāṟku ivvūr
kāciyappaṉ nārāyaṇan cūriyatevapaṭṭaṉ cantirātittavaṟ
cellavaikkiṟa tirunantāviḷakku oṉṟukku upaiyamāka
tiruviḷakkerikka ikkoyil muppatu vaṭṭamuṅ
kāṇiyu[mu]ṭaiya civappirāmaṇar kāciyapaṉ paramicura
muṭaiyāṉ āṭkoṇṭa nāyakapaṭṭaṉum pāratāyan
maturāntakatevaṉ āṭaviṭaṅkapaṭṭaṉu-

m āttiraiyaṉ peruntiruvāḷan irācapaṭṭaṉum

uruṭṭiyacevaṭi nāṟpatteṇṇāyira paṭṭaṉum āttiraiyaṉ

mātevaṉ eḻuṉūṟṟuvapaṭṭaṉum āttiraiyaṉ

opātittu ippon koṇṭu poliyūṭṭāka

inscription.head:
No. 176.
(A.R. No. 176 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This inscription commences with the praśasti, Pūmaruviya-tiśai-
mugattōn, etc., of the king.

The details of date of the record viz., 5th year, Kuṁbha ba 9, Friday,
Mūla corresponds to 18th February, 1183 A.D.

It states that a kūṟṟ-ōlai was executed by the ūravar to one Māttuḻāṉ
Chāmuṇḍadēvaṉ Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-uḍaiyāṉ of Māttūr in Kumiḻi-nāḍu in
Āmūr-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam and to his marumagaḷ śrī
Pugaḷūrdēvaṉ Peṟṟāḻvi for the lands of araiyē-nālu-mā enjoyed by them.
Of these 14 mā of lands, after deducting the 7 mā of lands belonging to
śrī Pugaḷūrdēvaṉ Peṟṟāḻvi, the remaining 7 mā of lands were got exchanged
(parivattaṉai) from Vimiśiram-uḍaiyāṉ Rājarājadēvaṉ. In this after
excluding the lands of kāṇi-araikkāṇi-mundirigai given earlier to the temple
of Tāmattīśuram-uḍaiyār, the balance of āru-mā-arai-mundirigai were
endowed to provide for the food-offerings to the deity Āḷuḍaiya-piḷḷaiyār
in the temple of Śirāḷīśuram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār, the gift being entrusted to the
Śrīmāhēśvaras; to the goddess Āḷuḍaiya-Nāchchiyār in the tiruppaḷḷiyaṟai
of the temple of Śīrāḷīśuram-uḍaiyār, to the god Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-
uḍaiyār and his Nāchchiyār and Vināyakap-piḷḷaiyār (all the three) being
worshipped by the donor's father, to the god Adirpaḍa-Āḍavallār in the
temple of Siddhīśuram at Tirunaṟaiyūr in the nāḍu (of the same name)
for the coating, processional ceremony and food-offerings to be made on
the day of Vaikāśi festival and to the deity Tirumāḷigaip-piḷḷaiyār in the
temple of Tāmattīśuram-uḍaiyār for the purpose of food-offerings. Of the
ēḻu-mā-kāṇi-araikkāṇi kīḻ-mukkālē mūṉṟu-māk-kāṇi obtained in exchange
from Tiruvēgambam-uḍaiyāṉ Ponmēnda-perumāḷ, the provision of food
offerings was made to the deity Tirumāḷigai-piḷḷaiyār by raising the crops
after reclamation to the extent of kāṇi-araikkāṇi-kiḻ-mukkālē-mūnṟu-māk-kāṇi
lands.

The royal order (tirumugam) refers to the gift of these lands as
neyttōṟpaṭṭi-iṟaiyili for the donor's father. A portion of the iṟaiyili gift should
be enjoyed as capital by the person who has received the donor share
(ōpādik-kāṇi). It is stipulated that he should pay the taxes annually for
the share of land which had been declared as nettōṟpaṭṭi (i.e., the lands
endowed to the heirs of the persons who lost their lives in fighting), failing
which the donor's ōpādi-kāṇi should be distributed.

This inscription (No. 176) of Vīrarājēndra, identified with Kulōttuṅga III
is interesting in more than respect. It makes a particular reference to the
gift lands made by Māttuḻān Chāmuṇḍadēvan Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-uḍaiyāṉ
for his father (pitā) as neyttōṟ-paṭṭi-iṟaiyili. It is also referred to as nettōṟpaṭṭi.
The lands which were in enjoyment of the donor were given. It is a tax-
free gift of the lands given to the heirs of persons who lost their lives
in fighting or in the course of services rendered to the king or to his
representative.

The term neyttōṟpaṭṭi is the same as its variant udirappaṭṭi occurring
in a number of Tamil records. The expression ney in Tamil means ‘blood’.
The Kannaḍa nettar and the Telugu netturu or netra or rakta are all similar
expressions which should have been derived from a common Dravidian root.
The word neyttōṟ should have been corrupted into nettōṟ meaning ‘blood’
and the gift given as neyttōṟpaṭṭi can be understood as the grant made
to the descendants of the deceased who must have shed their blood in the
wars.

The synonymous expressions netturuviddha and nettravaṭṭi figures, in
the Telugu records of 12th century A.D. (S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 172; S.I.I., Vol.
V. No. 1014).

inscription.head:
No. 177.
(A.R. No. 177 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This inscription commences with the praśasti, Pūmādu-puṇara, etc., of
the king. The ends of the lines are built in. The extant portion of the record
gives the details of the date viz., 5th year, Saturday, Pūram which may
correspond to 5th May, 1123 A.D. The month may be Ṛishabha and the
tithi being śu 9.

It seems to record a deed of agreement executed by the sabhai (sabhā-
vyavasthai) of Tirunaṟaiyur alias Pañchavanmādēvi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in
Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu in Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu which met in the temple of
the deity Siddhīśvaram-uḍaiyār pertaining to the sale of lands made over
to this deity and the grant of 2 mā of lands as tirunundāviḷakkuppuṟam
and oru mā-mukkāṇi-kiḻ-ēḻu-mā of lands as amāvāsippuṟam for the feeding
of the śivayōgis on the day of Amāvāsi. The sabhai is stated to have executed
this transaction of lands after receiving the price-value (śela-aṟa) from
Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-uḍaiya Mauṉadēvaṉ of Neṟkuṉṟam.

Among the boundaries of the lands thus sold, mention may be made
of the Pavitramāṇikka-vadi and Rājēndraśōḻa-vāykkāl. The sabhai consist
of a number of members including Kavuṇiyan Paramēśvaran Iḷaiya
Tiruvēṅgaḍattāṉ and several others. The document is said to have been
attested by Kaṇakkaṉ Mādānamaṅgalam-uḍaiyāṉ.

l.ta:
pūmātu puṇara puvimātu vaḷara nāmātu
viḷaṅka jayamātu nilavattanniruppatu malar manṉavar cūṭa
manniya urimai
l.note: The ends of the lines are built in.

l.ta:
m ticaitoṟum valarppa veṅkali niṅki meyyaṟantaḻaippak
kaliṅkamiriyakkaṭa[l] malai naṭātti valaṅkoḷāḻi varaiyāḻi
naṭātti irucuṭaraḷavum oru-

m canikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa pūrattināḷ kulottuṅka
coḻavaḷanāṭṭu tirunaṟaiyūr nāṭṭu tirunaṟaiyūrākiya

inscription.head:
No. 178.
(A.R. No. 178 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This inscription commences with the praśasti, Puyal-vāyttu-vaḷam-
peruga, etc., of the king.

The details of the date of the record viz., 6th year, Siṁha ba 9, Saturday,
Mṛigaśīrsha correspond to 13th August, 1183 A.D.

It records an undertaking given by the Śivabrāhmaṇas who had the
right of worship in the temple of the deity Siddhīśvaram-uḍaiyār of
Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu in Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu to Ēkavāśagaṉ Ulagukaṇvidai
pe ••••• Vāṇakōvaraiyar, a chief of Tuṇḍa-nāḍu in Muḍigoṇḍaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu
and a certain Kāppukka Malāḍuḍaiyāṉ Muḍigoṇḍaśōḻaṇ Iruḷnīkkiyāḻvār, for
a sum of 200 kāśu received from the latter, to provide for a perpetual
lamp to this deity. For this gift, two nilaittarāk-kutti-viḷakku each weighing
165 (palam ?) were provided.

A certain Vāṇakōvaraiyar who had the name Ēkavāśagaṉ Kulōttuṅgaśōḻanār
figuring as a chief of Kulōttuṅga III, though not identical with the chief
of the present record, may be said to be related with each other.

l.ta:
puyal vāyttu vaḷam perukap poyyāta
nāṉmaṟaiyiṉ ceyalvāyppat tirumakaḷuñ ceyamakaḷum

inscription.head:
No. 179.
(A.R. No. 179 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This inscription is dated in the 11th+1st year of the king. It does
not give any praśasti of the king. The record may palaeographically be
assigned to c. 12th century.

It records the gift of a kāśu deposited into the treasury of the temple
by a certain Tiruvāṉaikkāvaṉ Udayañjeydāṉ, a chief of Neḍumbulam in
Puṟaṅkarambai-nāḍu in Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu for maintaining a sandhi
lamp to the deity Siddhīśvaram-uḍaiyār of Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu. The
Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple have agreed to maintain the gift from out
of the amount deposited in the treasury by the donor.

l.ta:
viḷakku oṉṟukku ittevar śrī[paṇ]ṭārattu
oṭukkiṉa kācu

inscription.head:
No. 180.
(A.R. No. 180 of 1908.)
TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This inscription in the absence of the praśasti of the king may be
assigned on palaeographical grounds to c. 12th century.

It records the gift of one kāśu deposited into the temple treasury by
Nakkaṉ Aṇḍa[m*]mai, wife of Amudaṅkōvan, a merchant of
Avaṉinārāyaṇapuram for maintaining a sandhi lamp to the deity from out
of the interest (virutti) on the above amount.
The name of the deity is probably Siddhīśvaram-uḍaiyār.

l.ta: varkku vaitta canti viḷakku onṟukku itte-
var paṇṭārattu oṭukkiṉa kācu onṟu ikkā-

inscription.head:
No. 181.
(A.R. No. 181 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
East wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.

Kulōttuṅga III Year 25: 1202-03 A.D.
(‘who was pleased to take Madurai,
Iḻam and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya’)

This incomplete inscription records the sale of a servant (āḷ-āvaṇam)
by a certain Uḍaiyāṉ Namban Prasannadēvan of Śelgalūr in Iḍaikkā-nāḍu
in Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu executed in favour of the deity Ādichaṇḍēśvara
of the temple of god Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār.

It is a rare and interesting record which refers to the sale of human
beings.

l.note: The rest of the record is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 182.
(A.R. No. 182 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
East wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This inscription does not furnish any praśasti of the king. The details
of date viz., Kaṉṉi śu 5, Monday and Anusha correspond to the 1st September
1242 A.D., f.d.n. 22. On grounds of palaeography the king may be identified
with Rājarāja III.

It records the order of royal official or chief Araiyaṉ Nāḍuḍaiyāṉ
Viḻuppādirāyaṉ, the headman of Śiṟṟārkāḍu in Pāṇḍikulāśani-vaḷanāḍu issued
to the officers of the temple of Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār relating to
the grant of one vēli of land at Sundaraśōḻapuram as tirunāmattukkāṇi and
as ūrkkīḻ-iṟaiyili for the food-offerings to the diety during the midday
worship. The gift land is stated to be the kāṇi (hereditary right) of the
donor (i.e., the chief).

inscription.head:
No. 183.
(A.R. No. 183 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
East wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This epigraph commences with the praśasti, Puyal-vāyttu-maṇvaḷara,
etc., of the king. It may palaeographically be assigned to c. 13th Century.

It records the grant of lands at Tiruchchennel[puṟ]am to the deity
Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār of Tiruuśāttāṉam alias Kēraḷakulāśaṉich-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Puṟaṅkaṟambai-nāḍu in Rājēndraśōḻa-
vaḷanāḍu. The purpose of the grant and the details of the lands endowed
are not known as the record after the 8th line is built in.

cakkaramum ceyal vāyppu paṉnūlum ceṅkolun
ticainaṭappak karuṅkaliyum veṇmatiyum kālkalaṅki

inscription.head:
No. 184.
(A.R. No. 184 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This inscription may be assigned on palaeographical grounds to c. 13th
Century A.D.

It records a gift of 500 kāśu for maintaining a sandhi lamp by a certain
Ammaiyāḻvān Vīdiviṭaṅkap-perumāḷ alias Urāyarāman, a native of
Puduppaṭṭaṇam. He is one among the persons who is referred to as Umbar-
śānattār.

inscription.head:
No. 185.
(A.R. No. 185 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This inscription may be assigned to about the 13th Century on grounds
of palaeography. It does not furnish either the praśasti or the title of the
king.

The king is stated to have raised the family of Manu (Manukula) (from
its low position) and to have worn the heareditary crown. This indicates
the attempts made by the king to regain the territories and to consolidate
the kingdom.

It records an undertaking given by the tillers of the dēvadāna lands
of the deity Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiyār in Uśākkāṉam alias Kēraḷakulāśaṉi-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Puṟaṅkarambai-nāḍu in Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, who
agreed to pay one kuṟuṇi of paddy per 1/8th of a chēy as a capital for
the maintenance of Tirumāḷigaippiḷḷai, a representative of Irāvaḷa
Sōmanādēva-mudaliyār alias Padineṇbhūmi Kārāḷa Kaṟpagappiḷḷaiyār of
Seventy-nine nāḍu, who was conducting the services in the temple.

The king may be identified with Rājēndra III on the basis of the
inference drawn of his having raised the Manukula and got the fillet of
crown.

l.ta: ṭi kiḻumāy niṟkiṟa viḷakkoḷi maṅkala muṭai-

edition.p: 1. Rest of the record is built in.

l.ta: ippaṭikaḷ ceyyum miṭattu iv irāvaḷa mu-

toru[m*] vā(c)cal aṉucarikka pukuntatākkum itil
māḷikai piḷḷai ••• tiṉṟu collic cey-

yak kaṭavārākavum ippaṭikku ivai perumarutūruṭaiyār
comaṉāveḷḷāṉ eḻuttu

ippaṭikku ivai vikkirama coḻaveḷāṉ eḻuttu •••••
nṟum ippaṭikku ivai vaṅkaṉ ••• kā uṭaiyāṉ
ñāṉacampantaveḷāṉ eḻuttu e[ṉ]ṟum

ippaṭikku ivai vikkiramacoḻa nallūr uṭaiyār •••

inscription.head:
No. 186.
(A.R. No. 186 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This inscription may be assigned on grounds of palaeography to c. 13th
Century.

It records the grant of lands (maḍakku-nilam) of the twelfth grade
as the balance of the maḍam (maḍaśēsham) to a certain Āṇḍār
Prasannadēvar who executed the repairs to the temple in the above year
to the temple of Tiruvuśāttānam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār by the mahāsabhai
including the kaṇakku (accountant) Śākkāṉam-uḍaiyāṉ Danmappiriyaṉ of
Uśāttāṉam alias Kēraḷakulāśaṉich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam. Among the boundaries
of the land given to Prasannadēvar, is mentioned the field to the west of
the lands of a certain Vikkiramaśōḻa-mūvēndavēḷār of Rājēndraśōḻapuram
and the reference is made to the revenue survey that is done in the 38th
year of the king.

Such a classified and surveyed land is stated to have been made over
as iṟaiyili free from taxes such as antarāyap-pāṭṭam, etc., for the food-
offerings to be made to the deities of the Śaiva saints Piḷḷaiyār-Nāyaṉār
(Tirujñānasambandar), Tirunāvukkaraśu-nāyaṉār (Appar) and Sundara-
nāyaṉār consecrated in the circumambulatory path (tirunaḍai-māḷigai) in
the temple of Tiruvuśāttānam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār.

Here, the name of the king in whose 38th year the survey had been
conducted is not mentioned.

vai uttama paṭṭaṉ ṉeḻuttu | ivai kaḷattūr kirā-

inscription.head:
No. 187.
(A.R. No. 187 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.

The record furnishes the details of date viz., year 4, Siṁha ba 3,
Thursday, Pūraṭṭādi-nakshatara which regularly correspond to July 29, 1249
A.D.

It records a deed of agreement (iśaivu-tīṭṭu) issued by the dēvaraḍiyār
of the temple to the Ādichaṇḍēśvaradēvar of the temple of Tiruvuśāttāṉam-
uḍaiyār at Uśāttāṉam alias Kēraḷakulāśanich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in
Puṟaṅkarambai-nāḍu in Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu pertaining to an arrangement
entered into among the dancing girls for maintaining lamps during different
sandhis in front of the goddess Buvaṉāpati-nāchchiyār consecrated by
Kaḷarikkuḍaiyāṉ Poṉṉambalakūttaṉ alias Irumuḍiśōḻa-Pallavarayaṉ in the
western side circumambulatory path in the temple of Tiruvuśāttāṉam-
uḍaiyār.

inscription.head:
No. 188.
(A.R. No. 188 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This record may be assigned on palaeographical grounds to c. 13th
Century. It records the grant of maḍakku lands excluding the tirunāmattu-
iṟuppu of the deity such as kaḍamai, kuḍimai, niṉaippuṭṭ-taṇḍum, etc.,
enjoyed as tirunāmattukkāṇi by Paṭṭāṇḍār, wife of Tillaivāṉuṟaivāṉ, the
kāṇiyāḷar of Rājēndrapuram, by Rājarāja-Chēdirāyar for the worship and
services (tiruppaṇi) to the goddess Buvaṉādipati-nāchchiyār which came to
be installed and worshipped in the western side tirunaḍaimāḷigai in the
temple of Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār at Uśāttāṉam alias Kēraḷakulāśaṉich-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam. An order (ōlai) recording the iṟaiyili grant of lands
is stated to have been issued by the nagarattār of Mummuḍiśōḻapuram
in [Puṟaṅ]karambai-nāḍu in Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. This was ordered to
be engraved on the wall of this temple and attested by several officers.
Among the signatories mention may be made of Vikkiramaśōḻa-
mūvēndavēḷāṉ and others.

inscription.head:
No. 189.
(A.R. No. 189 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This epigraph may be assigned on palaeographical grounds to c. 13th
Century. The record furnishes the details of date viz., year [10], Mīṉa śu
10, Tuesday, Punarpūśa-nakshatra corresponding to 1256 A.D., March 7.

It records a deed of agreement (iśaivu-tīṭṭu) given by the tāṉattār of
the temple of Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiyār at Śāttāṉam alias Kēraḷakulāśaṉich-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Puṟaṅkarambai-nāḍu in Rājēndraśōḻa-
vaḷanāḍu to Araiyaṉ Poṉṉambalakkūtaṉ alias Irumuḍiśōḻa-Pallavaraiyar
of Kaḷarikkuḍi in Kūṭṭuk-kūṟṟam in Pāṇḍikulāśaṉi-vaḷanāḍu and to the
cultivating tenants of the dēvadāṉa lands of the temple for the gift amount
of 3000 kāśu and the paddy measuring a tūṇi for one vēli of land for the
food-offerings, maintenance of lamps and worship to the goddess
Buvaṉādipati-nāchchiyār installed by the above araiyaṉ in the western side
prākāra (circumambulatory path) (also called tirunaḍaimāḷigai) in this
temple. It is also mentioned that the wages for the sculptors named
Tirukkoḍuṅguṉṟa-vayirāgi and Kōvāṇḍāṉ who came to carryout the
repairs of the northern side tirunaḍaimāḷigai was to be met with from out
of the money endowed above. The deed was recorded on stone by Viṭaṅkaṉ
alias Vaḷavatoṅga-āchāriyaṉ, who had the right of Śippāchāriya-kāṇi in the
temple.

l.note: The word ‘vatu’ is expressed by symbol.

mika-

[pallavaraiyar] ••• ••• •••• tanta paricāvatu [|*] iṉṉāyaṉār

uṭaiyār tantakācu

inscription.head:
No. 190.
(A.R. No. 190 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in
the Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This record may be assigned on palaeographical grounds to c. 13th
century.

It gives the boundaries of the dēvadāna lands of the temple of Tiru-
Usāttāṉam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār. The boundaries of the dēvadāna lands are
specified in detail.

innilaṅkālukkum kiḻpāṟkellai muṟcollappaṭṭa
vāykkālukku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai tirupputtūr
paṭṭālakaṉ uyyavantāṉpaṭṭaṉ uḷḷiṭṭār nilattukku
vaṭa(ṭa)kkum melpāṟkellai nārācavarampukku kiḻakkum

inscription.head:
No. 191.
(A.R. No. 191 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in
the Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This record is not dated in the reign of any particular king but it
refers to the regnal year 38. The king is perhaps identical with a Chōḷa
king who had the highest regnal year i.e., Kulōttuṅgachōḷa III.

The record gives the details of the boundaries of the dēvadāna lands
of the deity Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiyār, excluding the tiruviḷakkuppuṟam and
other lands. The details of the land boundaries are given.

kiḻaraiye irumāvaraiyum itaṉ vaṭakku innāya Incomplete.-

inscription.head:
No. 192.
(A.R. No. 192 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in
the Mantrapurīśvara temple.

The record may be assigned on palaeographical grounds to c. 13th
century A.D.

It refers to an order (ōlai) recording the gift of land for the food
offerings to the deity of the temple of Uḍaiyār Tiruuśāttānam-uḍaiyār
in Puṟaṅkarambai-nāḍu. The order was issued to the tānattār of the temple
by a certain Kāliṅgarāyan. The lands donated as iṟaiyili are from Uppūr
alias Vikramaśōḻapaṭṭaṇam, Uśāttānam alias Kēraḷakulāsani-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, etc.

It also mentions Śāttānam alias Kēraḷakulāśaṉi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam
and Kalyāṇapuraṅgoṇḍaśōḻapuram among the boundaries.
The signatories mentioned are Kāliṅgarāyan and Muṉaiyadarayan.

l.ta: tukku pokiṟa peruvaḻikku teṟkku nilam ira-

inscription.head:
No. 193.
(A.R. No. 193 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in
the Mantarapurīśvara temple.

This record is dated in the 29th+1st regnal year and other details
of date are Kaṟkaṭaka, trayōdaśi, Punarpūśam, Monday ••

This record refers to the gift of land as iṟaiyili for the food offerings
to the deity Subrahmaṇya in the temple of Tiru-Uśāttānam-uḍaiyār enshrined
to the west of prākāra (tirunaḍaimāḷigai) in the assembly of
Peruvāḻvutandaperumal-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, an agara in Puṟaṅkarambai-
nāḍu, a sub-division of Rājēndraśōḷa-vaḷanāḍu. Among the boundaries of the
land Ulōgamādēvi-vadi is also mentioned.

l.ta: nāṅkaḷ nirvāttu kuṭutta nilamāvatu piṭākai ko-
kkaṭaiya rūpāy nikki itaṉ tekku rūpāy iṉ-
m kuṭimai koyiṟṟampa peṟu uḷḷiṭṭanavum ū-

l.note: The beginning and the end of this and the following lines are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 194.
(A.R. No. 194 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in
the Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This record states that at the request of the chief Iḷaṅg[ō]yuḍaiyāṉ alias
Maṉaiyadaraiyaṉ, the mahāsabhai made a grant of land as [tirunāmattukkāṇi]-
iṟaiyili in Śākkāṉam alias Kēraḷakulāśaṉi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya
of Puṟaṅkarambai-nāḍu in Rājēndrachōḷa-vaḷanāḍu. The name Śākkāṉam is
probably a mistake for Śāttāṉam (i.e., Uśāttāṉam). Among the boundaries
of the land, mention may be made of tachchar and kollar settlements
(kuḍiyiruppu).

inscription.head:
No. 195.
(A.R. No. 195 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in
the Mantarapurīśvara temple.

This record is dated in the 20th regnal year, which with other details
of date are Makara, śu 7, Monday, Aśvini-nakshatra •••

This inscription records the gift of land by the great assembly of
Peruvāḻvutanda-perumāḷ-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Puṟaṅkarambai-nāḍu, in
Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, for the food offerings to be made to the deity
Tirukkāmakkōṭṭam-uḍaiya-periya-nāchchiyar, set up by the prince
Purōśaikkuḍaiyār. The gift was made for the welfare of the king and for
the victory of the Prince Iruṅgōḷar.

l.ta: ta nilamāvatu [|*] piṭākai koṟṟa nallūrāṉa te-
ṟkkaṭaiya nilam kiḻakku mukkālmā vum iraṇṭāñcatirattu
ṅkaḷāl tarappaṭi va[rika]ḷil uḷḷa maṭakku kaḻittu oḻuki-
le eṟaṭṭu iṟukka kaṭavatākavum ippaṭi canti-

inscription.head:
No. 196.
(A.R. No. 196 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in
the Mantarapurīśvara temple.

This record refers to the gift of land to the tānattar of the temple
of Śāttāṉam alias Kēraḷakulāśaṉi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam by those who were
in-charge of the village duties (grāma-kārya), for the prosperity of the king
Ulaguḍaiya-perumāḷ and for the victory of the prince Iruṅgōḷar. Also refers
to the provision made for the food-offerings to the goddess Tirukāmakkōṭṭam-
uḍaiya-periyanāchchiyār, set up by prince Purōśaikkuḍaiyār of
Kulōttuṅgaśōḻanallūr. This was attested by a number of signatories.
Boundaries of the gift land are specified at the end.

l.ta: m piṭākai kulottuṅka coḻa nallūr tiruvaraṅ-
kavatikku meṟku irā[ca*] rā[ca*] vāykkālukkut teṟku ai-

edition.l: ṇiyum iṟaiyiliyum ākak kaikkoṇṭu pa Incomplete.-

inscription.head:
No. 197.
(A.R. No. 197 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.
This epigraph may palaeographically be assigned to 13th century.

It records the assignment of income from taxes such as antarāyam,
kōyiṟṟampēṟu, kuḍimai, etc., collected on the iṟaiyili lands of the temple
of the deity Tiru Uśāttāṉam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār, upto the tenth year, to meet
the services (tiruppaṇi) of the god. The village accountant (ūr-kaṇakku)
Śākkāṉam-uḍaiyāṉ Uttamappiriyaṉ undertook to provide the income for the
temple. The decision to this effect was taken by the assembly of Uśāttāṉam
alias Kēraḷakulāśanich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam and the same was agreed to by
several signatories (names are mentioned). Mahājanapriyaṉ (Makāchanappiriyan),
the ūr-kaṇakku are referred to at the end as one of the signatories.

l.ta: yūr muṭkoṭṭi nārāyaṇa paṭṭaṉ makaṉ citara-
vanta paṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai u-

nta locaṉapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai irāyūr
muṭkkoṭṭipaṟma
ṉāpa paṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai cu-

inscription.head:
No. 198.
(A.R. No. 198 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This record furnishes the details of date viz., Regnal year 31, Ṛishabha
śu 7, Sunday, Makha-nakshatra corresponding to 1247 A.D., May 12, Sunday,
f.d.t. º09.

It records the grant of iru-mā-arai lands called Kommaḍich-chey as
tirunāmattukkāṇi and iṟaiyili to provide for the food-offerings to the deity
Sūryadēva enshrined in the northern wing of the east prākāra (tirunaḍai-
māḷigai) in the temple of Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiyār at Uśāttāṉam alias
Kēraḷakulāśaṉich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Puraṅkarambai-nāḍu by the
nagarattār of Vikramābharaṇapuram to the west of Umbar-nāḍu. The ūr
has to meet the payment of taxes such as antarāyam, uṟaivēli,
kōyiṟṟamapēṟu, etc., on these lands. Among the boundaries of the gift land
are mentioned periyakōyil-nambi-nilam in the east, puṟṟiḍaṅkoṇḍa-ppēraiyaṉ
lands in the south, Śāliyappēraiya[ṉ*] lands in the west and kollai tax-free
lands in the north.

This was caused to be recorded in stone in the tirumāḷigai by the
nagarattār. The decision of the nagarattār was attested to by several
signatories including the ūr-kaṇakku (village accountant) Tumbūr-uḍaiyāṉ.
Three other signatories in this document are Dharamapālaṉ of
Pirāntakapuram, Vayirapālaṉ and Udayapālaṉ.

l.ta:
miyum
nāyaṟṟuk kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa makattunāḷ umpar nāṭṭu
melaivakaipāyiṭṭa nakaram vikkiramāparaṇapurattu nakarattom

ucāttāṉamāṉa keraḷakulācaṉic catu

inscription.head:
No. 199.
(A.R. No. 199 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.

The details of date given in the record are Regnal year 31, Mēsha,
Pañchami, Monday and Anusha-nakshatra.

This epigraph records the grant of two mā of lands called Śaḍaiyappaṉ-
nilam at Parāntakapuram by the nagarattār of this place situated in the
western division (mēlai-vagipāyiṭṭa) of Umbar-nāḍu for the food-offerings
to the deity Sūriyadēva (Sun-god) east prākāra (kīḻait-tirunaḍai-māḷigai) in
the temple of Tiru Vuśāttāṉam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār at Uśāttāṉam alias
Kēraḷakulāśaṉich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Puraṅkarambai-nāḍu. It also refers
to the remission of taxes and grant of the same made over as iṟaiyili. The
ūr has to meet the taxes specified here. The document was caused to be
recorded on stone in the tirumāḷigai. One of the signatories in this document
was Vayirapālaṉ, two others being Udaiyapālaṉ and Daṉmapālaṉ.
Tumbūruḍaiyāṉ figures as the ūr-kaṇakku just as in the other record dated
in the same year (A.R.Ep., 1908, No. 198).

It may be suggested that since some of the signatories of this and the
other record discussed above are the same in view of the fact that both
the places are located in the western division of Umbar-nāḍu.
Vikramābharaṇapuram and Parāntakapuram are the places close by.

inscription.head:
No. 200.
(A.R. No. 200 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in
the Mantrapurīśvara temple.

The record may be assigned to 12th-13th century on palaeographical
grounds.

This incomplete inscription records the boundaries of dēvadāna-iṟaiyili
lands belonging to the temple of Uḍaiyār Śāttāṉam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār. It is
interesting to note the name of Periyadēvar Vikramachōḷadēva figuring in
this record.

Mention has been made about the river Kaḍukali near this place as
one of the boundaries of the donated lands.

l.ta:
uṭaiyār tiruvucāttānamuṭaiya nāyanāraip periya
tevar vikkirama coḻa tevar

inscription.head:
No. 201.
(A.R. No. 201 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the gōmukha (water chute) in the south side of the maṇḍapa in
front of the central shrine in the Mantrapurīśvara temple.
The record may palaeographically be assigned to 13th century A.D.

It registers the gift, probably of the water chute, where the record
is engraved, by a certain Tirukkāppunīr-aḻagiyāṉ alias Vīraśiṅgarāyaṉ of
Ilattaippuliyūr, who use to offer food to the deity Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiyār.

inscription.head:
No. 202.
(A.R. No. 202 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south base of the verandah of the first prākāra in the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.

The inscription may be assigned to century on palaeographical
grounds.

It records an order (ōlai) of Paiyyuḻāṉ Periyāṉ-Aḻagiyaperumāḷ alias
Śōḻagaṅgaṉ granting the dēvadāṉa lands of 10 mā at Chaṇḍaṉ-karambai,
one of the villages declared as paḍaippaṟṟu (village given as military tenure)
and the land of araikkāṇi-mundirigai given as house-site for the settlement,
as tirumāmattukkāṇi-iṟaiyili for the worship and services (tiruppaṇi) to the
deity Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār. The boundaries of the gift lands and
the signatories who attested the document are mentioned.

l.ta:
§ paiyyuḻāṉ periyāṉaḻakiya perumāḷāṉa coḻakaṅkaṉ

inscription.head:
No. 203.
(A.R. No. 203 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south base of the verandah of the first prākāra in the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.

The record may be assigned to c. 13th century on palaeographical
grounds.

It refers to an order (ōlai) of Chokkaṉāyaṉ Rājagambīra Śōḻiya-varaiyaṉ
and records the grant of 2 mā of lands at Nambaṉkuṟichchi, a hamlet
of Uśāttāṉam alias Kēraḷakulāśaṉich-charuppēdimaṅgalam for
tirukkaikoṭṭippuṟam as iṟaiyili to the reciters (ōduvar) of Tiruvuśāttāṉam-
uḍaiya-nāyaṉār temple. The document was attested by Villavadarayan and
other signatories.

inscription.head:
No. 204.
(A.R. No. 204 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south base of the verandah of the first prākāra in the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.

The epigraph may be assigned to c. 13th century on palaeographical
grounds.

It records an order of the prince (piḷḷai) to his officer Kuṟukkai-uḍaiyāṉ
through the nagarattār of Mummuḍiśōḻapuram for the levy (kaḍamai) to
be imposed on each load of paddy or rice carried in the route called
Seṉanādaṉ (Jaṉanāthaṉ)-pādai for the tiruppaḍimāṟṟu to the deity
Tiruvusāttāṉam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār. The sub-division Teṉkarai-nāḍu is mentioned
at the end.

l.ta:
kuṟukkai uṭaiyāṉukkuc cellumpaṭi tiripuvaṉac
cakkaravattikaḷ śrīrācentira coḻa-

tevaṟkku yāṇṭu pattāvatiṉ netirāmāṇṭu mummuṭi
coḻapurattu nakarattom ōlai [|*] nammūril ce-

ṉanātaṉa pātaittuṟaiyil nel arici matikiṟa potiyil
kaṭamaikkumiḻakiṟa ariciyile oru poti matippaṭi

uṭaiyār tiruvucāttāṉamuṭaiya nāyaṉārkku tiruppaṭi
māṟṟukku viṭuvateṉṟu piḷḷaiyolai eḻutiṉamaiyi-

l ippoti oṉṟil nammuṭaiya nilakkūliyāy peṟum

inscription.head:
No. 205.
(A.R. No. 205 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south base of the verandah of the first prākāra in the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This inscription may be assigned to c. 13th century on palaeographical
grounds.

It records an order (ōlai) issued by an officer of the king Paiyyuḻāṉ
Periyāṉ Aḻagiyaperumāḷ alias Śōḻagaṅgaṉ to the tāṉattār of the temple
of Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār granting the lands from his kāṇi-paṟṟu
at Nambaṉkuṟuchchi, a hamlet of Śāttāṉam alias Kēraḷakulāśanich-
charuruppēdimaṅgalam as iṟaiyili, for the purpose of food-offerings,
tirumēṟppūchchu, tiruchchāndu, tiruppaḷitāmam and tiruviḷakkeṇṇai to the
deity Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār on the days of festivals such as
tirukkāppunāḷ, tiruveḻuchchi and tiruaraippuṟam instituted by him in the
month of Vaikāśi. A number of signatories like mudal-kaṇakk-uḍaiyāṉ
Tirujñānasambanda-vēḷāṉ, etc., have attested this document.

paiyyūḻāṉ periyāṉ aḻakiya perumāḷāṉa

ttāṉamuṭaiya nāyaṉārkoyil tāṉattār kaṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 206.
(A.R. No. 206 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south base of the verandah of the first prākāra in the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This inscription may be assigned to c. 13th century on palaeographical
grounds.

It records the grant of 32 kalam for a vēli for 4 mā of lands purchased
as kuḍinīṅgāk-kāṇi from the tirunāmattukkāṇi lands of the deity
Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār by Ādittadēvaṉ, a vēḷāṉ of Veḷḷūr in
Paiyyūr-kōṭṭam in Perun-Toṇḍaimaṇḍalam. The balance of paddy received
as iṟaimigudi was to be given to the Śrīmāhēśvaras of the temple. The
grant was entrusted to the Ādichaṇḍēśvara-dēvakaṉmigaḷ of the above
temple.

l.ta:
tu peruntoṉṭai maṇṭalattu paiyyūr koṭṭattu

ṉ uṭaiyār tiruvucāttāṉamuṭaiya nāyaṉār tirunāmattuk
kāṇiyil nāṉkuṭi niṅkāk kāṇiyāka koṇ-

inscription.head:
No. 207.
(A.R. No. 207 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south base of the verandah of the first prākāra in the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This incomplete epigraph can be assigned to c. 13th century on
palaeographical grounds.

It records an iśaivu-tīṭṭu issued by the settlements of the dēvadāna
village of the deity Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiyār of Uśāttāṉam alias Kēraḷakulāśaṉich-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Puṟaṅkarambai-nāḍu in Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu and
the āṇḍārgaḷ who raised crops in the dēvadāṉa of the deity to Vēḷuḻān
Ādichchadēvar, a resident of Vēḷūr in Paiyyūr-kōṭṭam in Toṇḍaimaṇḍalam
agreeing to raise crops in the lands called Iśaiyēndraperumāḷ-vayakkal given
as tirumunnoḍukku-puṟam from the dēvadāṉa of the deity. This agreement
deed was attested by a member of signatories whose names are mentioned.

ucāttāṉamāṉa keraḷakulācaṉiccaruppeti maṅkala-

ttu uṭaiyār tiruvucāttāṉamuṭaiyār tevatāṉattu
ūr kuṭimakkaḷum iṉṉāyaṉār tevatāṉattu veḷāḻmaic

inscription.head:
No. 208.
(A.R. No. 208 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South base of the verandah of the first prākāra in the Mantrapurīśvara
temple.

This record furnishes the details of date viz., Regnal year 10+1, Kaṉṉi,
ba 6, Monday and Rōhiṇi-nakshatra corresponding to 1256 A.D., September
11.

It refers to an attested document registering the agreement in the sale
of land (nila-vilaip-piramāṇa-iśaivu-tīṭṭu) executed by the great sabhai of
Uśāttāṉam alias Kēraḷakulāśaṉich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Puṟaṅkarambai-
nāḍu in Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu to the Ādichaṇḍēśvara-dēvakaṉmis and
temple of Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiyār. The land of oru mā arai-araikkāṇi-
kīḻaraiyē-iraṇḍu-mā surveyed in the 38th year of Periyadēvar
Tribhuvanadēvar was endowed as tirunāmattukkāṇi to the deity, after
purchase, for a price-value of 1200 aṉṟāḍu-naṟkkāśu. It mentions the breach
of a channel named Akaḷaṅkappērāṟu and the labour employed for the
restoration of the maḍai (sluice) of the Chekkār on the east bank of the
channel. The ūr-kaṇakkar viz., Śāttāṉam-uḍaiyāṉ Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷappiriyaṉ
and Muḷagoṭṭi Iḷaiya Nārāyaṇa-bhaṭṭaṉ of Irāyūr are mentioned as the
members of the assembly. The sale-deed was attested by a number of
signatories such as Gōvinda-bhattaṉ and Śīrāma-bhaṭṭaṉ of Kaḷattūr,
Tiruvēṇkaḍa-bhaṭṭaṉ of Irāmavalūr, etc.

tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīirācentira

inscription.head:
No. 209.
(A.R. No. 209 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
West base of the verandah in the first prākāra in the Mantrapurīśvara
temple.

This incomplete record may be assigned to c. 13th century on grounds
of palaeography.

It records an order (ōlai) of Pallavaraiyaṉ alias Rājagambhīraśōḻa-
Pallavaraiyaṉ granting the lands of mā-kāṇi-mundirigaik-kīḻarai including
the residue (migudi-uḷḷaḍaṅga) at Nambaṉkuṟuchchi as iṟaiyili to provide
for the food-offerings on the occasion of a sandhi named nāḍuḍai-ṉāyakaṉ-
sandhi to the deity Tiruvuś-ttāṉam-uḍaiya-ṉāyaṉār at Uśāttāṉam alias
Kēraḷakulāśaṉich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Puṟaṅkarambai-nāḍu in Rājēndraśōḻa-
vaḷanāḍu. Among the boundaries of his kāṇi lands in the above place,
mention may be made of Vīrakkōṉmaṅgalam-uḍaiyāṉ lands in the village
paṟṟu forming its eastern boundary. It is stated as maḍakku-nilam. The
other details are not given as the beginnings of lines 11-19 are lost.

l.ta:
pallavarāyaṉāṉa irāca kempirac coḻa
pallavaraiyaṉ Olai | irācen-

tira coḻa vaḷaṉāṭṭuppuṟaṅkarampai ṉāṭṭu ucāttāṉamāṉa
keraḷa kulācanic-

caruppeti maṅ[ka*]lattu uṭaiyār tiruvucāttāṉamuṭaiya
ṉāyaṉār koyil tā-

kku amutu paṭikkum viññaṉattukkum uṭalāka
iṭakkaṭavatāṉa nilattu-
kku muṉpu iṭṭaṉilam nikki iṟaiyiliyāka tanta

ṇiyāṉa ṉilattu iṭṭa muṉṟām vāykkālk kiḻ
kaṇṭattuṉilattukkuk kiḻpā-

ṉilattukku me-

ṟkkum teṉpāṟkkellai iṉṉāyaṉār tevatāṉattukku
vaṭakkum melpā-

ṉoṭaikku meṟkuṉ kī[ḻ*]

peruṉāṉkellaiyuḷ ṉaṭuvupaṭṭa

miku[ti]

muṉpu iṟukkiṟa paṭiye i Incomplete and left unengraved.-

inscription.head:
No. 210.
(A.R. No. 210 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
West base of the verandah in the first prākāra in the Mantrapurīśvara
temple.

This inscription may be assigned to c. 13th century on grounds of
palaeography.

It records an order for the tax to be collected from the dēvadāna and
tirunāmattukkāṇi lands from the month of Āḍi in the above year. The
boundaries of such lands were demarked and the document concerning this
was attested by mudal-kaṇakku Kiḻakkuḍaiyān Tirujñānasamba[nda*]r-
uḍaiyān, Tribhuvanavīravēḷan and others.

l.ta: ya varaiya neḻuttu eṉṟum ippaṭikku ivai •••
vai mutal kaṇakku kiḻakkuṭaiyān tiruñāṉa campa •••

inscription.head:
No. 210A.
(A.R. No. 210A of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
West base of the verandah in the first prākāra in the Mantrapurīśvara
temple.

It refers to the order (ōlai) of Uḍaiyār Pallavarāyaṉ Vāṇarāyaṉ
Śoḻiyavaraiya[ṉ*] and records the grant of his kāṇi lands at Namban-
kuṟuchchi as dēvadāna and tirunāmattukkāṇi-iṟaiyili for the food-offerings
to the deity Pallavamādēvīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār enshrined in the western
side of the circumambulatory path (tirunaḍai-māḷigai) in the temple of
Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiya-ṉāyaṉār at Uśāttāṉam alias Kēraḷakulāśaṉich-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Puṟaṅkarambai-nāḍu in Rājēndra-śōḻa-vaḷanāḍu.
Among the boundaries of the lands endowed is mentioned a brāhmaṇa-
paṟṟu named after an officer Rājagambhīra Śōḻiyavaraiyaṉ. This officer
is identical with his namesake who is called Śokkanāyaṉ whose order making
a gift of land to the ōduvar (reciters or eulogists) in the Tirukkai-kkōṭṭai
of the temple. The record ends abgruptly and hence the details of the
signatories of the order are not available.

l.ta:
uṭaiyyār pallavarāyaṉ vāṇarāyaṉāṉa coḻiya
varaiya[ṉ*] olai [|*] irācentiracoḻavaḷanāṭṭu

inscription.head:
No. 210B.
(A.R. No. 210B of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
West base of the verandah in the first prākāra in the Mantrapurīśvara
temple.

This fragmentary record may be assigned to the 13th century on
palaeographical considerations. The writing on the stone in the middle of
this record and the record of the 12th year of Rājēndradēva (A.R.Ep 1908,
No. 210), has been erased by chiselling. Therefore, the details of the record
are not known. However, from the extant portion of the record, it may
be said that it refers to some lands granted by an officer for the purpose
of services (tiruppaṇi) to some deity. One Eyiṉaṅjuḍaiyān is referred to
as mudal-kaṇakku and also as one of the signatories.

edition.p:
1. This inscription is written in the stone after No. 210/1908 with a space in
between, probably intanded for chiselling a record.

inscription.head:
No. 211.
(A.R. No. 211 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
West base of the verandah in the first prākāra in the Mantrapurīśvara
temple.

This inscription may be assigned to the 13th century on palaeographical
considerations.

Except the solar month Āḍi of the above regnal year, other details
of date are not furnished in the record.

This is an order (ōlai) of Paiyyuḻān Pallavarāyan Vāṇarāyaṉ alias
Śōḻiyavaraiyaṉ. It records the grant of oru-mā-mukkāṇi-araikkāṇi-kīḻaraiyē-
iraṇḍu-mā lands at Nambankuṟuchchi, a hamlet (piḍāgai) of Śākkāṉam alias
Kēraḷakulāśaṉich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam as maḍappuṟa-iṟaiyili to a maṭha
called Vāṇarāyaṉ-maṭha, located in the street to the north of the tirumaḍai-
viḷḍgam of the temple of Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār. It is stated that
the above lands were to be enjoyed as iṟaiyili along with Makkuḻan-kāṇi
and the income from taxes (kaḍamai) accrued out of the above. The
document was attested by Ādikāram Vāṉavan Pallavaraiyan and by others
called mudal-kaṇakku (names of four of these persons are furnished).

inscription.head:
No. 212.
(A.R. No. 212 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North base of the verandah in the first prākāra in the Mantrapurīśvara
temple.
This epigraph can palaeographically be assigned to the 13th century.

It records the grant of mā-kāṇi-araikkāṇi lands at Śiṅgaḷāntanallūr by
four individuals as tirunāmattukkāṇi to the deity Tiru-Uśāttānam-uḍaiya-
nāyaṉār. The above lands are stated to have been made over as iṟaiyili
to the deity for the purpose of providing food-offerings, at the request of
mudaliyār Poṉṉaya[m*]ba-nambi and tāṉattār of the temple. The decision
was taken by the assembly of the temple of Irājarāja-īśvaram (Irārā-īśvaram)
of Śiṅgaḷāntanallūr, a hamlet (piḍāgai) of Suttavallich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam
in Puṟaṅkarambai-nāḍu in Rājēndraṟśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. The lands granted as
tirunāmattukkāṇi are half mā each by Tiruvaiyāṟaḍigaḷ-bhaṭṭaṉ of
Poṉṉampuṟam and Tillaināyaka-bhaṭṭaṉ of Kuṇḍūr, thus totally one mā of
cultivable land in the south-east of the village and kāṇi-araikkāṇi of garden
lands called Śīrāttōṭṭam-nilam in the south-east of the lands on the eastern
bank of Muḷḷivāy-āṟu (inclusive of araikkāṇi-mundirigai lands given by Śrī
Mādēva-bhaṭṭaṉ and araikkāṇi-mundirigai lands given by Piḷḷainambi-
bhaṭṭaṉ). Among the boundaries of the garden lands endowed are mentioned
Śōḻakulasundara-vadi in the west and Muḍigoṇḍaśōḻa-vadi in the north. The
paddy in maḍakku from the classified lands was made over as iṟaiyili to
the temple.

The place Suttavallich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam referred to here is different
from the village Suttavalli alias Vīravinōdach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in
Mēlvemba-nāḍu referred to in the records from Tirunelveli temple (cf. S.I.I.,
Vol. V, No. 421, line 25)

inscription.head:
No. 213.
(A.R. No. 213 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North base of the verandah in the first prākāra in the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This incomplete record furnishes the details of date viz., Regnal year
17, Ṛishabha śu 3, Sunday and Pūsam (Pushya)-nakshatra corresponding to
1263 A.D., May 13, Sunday. But the tithi was pañchami on that day.

It records the grant of maḍakku lands, made after the purchase from
several individuals by the peruṅguṟi-mahāsabhai of Uśāttāṉam alias
Kēraḷaṉkulāśaṉich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Puṟaṅkarambai-nāḍu in Rājēndra-
śōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, as iṟaiyili to provide for the food-offerings to the deity on
the occasion of Irā[śarāśa*]dēvaṉ-sandhi instituted after Periya-uḍaiyar
Rā[ja*]rā[ja*]dēvaṉ alias Pallavarāyar who is described as the son (piḷḷai)
of the deity. The yield of 59 kalam, 2 tūṇi-kuruṇi-4 nāḻi of paddy resulted
from out of the tirunāmattukkāṇi lands of the god in the east of the village
Uśāttāṉam was endowed and entrusted to the Ādichaṇḍēśvara-dēvakaṉmigaḷ
of the temple to meet the food-offerings. Among the boundaries of the
gift lands mention may be made of the highway (peruvaḻi) leading to
Kalliyāṇapuraṅkoṇḍaśōḻapuram, the Muṉṉūṟṟuvaṉ-peruvaḻi, Vikramaśōḻanallūr,
Pirāntakapuram, Aiyyaṉkiḍaṅguḍaiyār-kōyil, etc.

The decision of the mahāsabhai was attested by the village accountants
(ūrk-kaṇakku) Tiruvaraṅgappiriyaṉ and Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷappiriyaṉ and several
others (names mentioned).

inscription.head:
No. 214.
(A.R. No. 214 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North base of the verandah in the first prākāra of the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This inscription furnishes the details of date viz., year 14, Siṁha śu
3, Wednesday, Hasta-nakshatra which may be equated with 1263 A.D., August
8, f.d.n. ·34.

This incomplete record stops with the mention of the ancient
geographical divisions to which [Kēraḷa*]kulāśaṉich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam
belonged. It is stated to have been included in Puṟaṅkarambai-nāḍu in
Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 215.
(A.R. No. 215 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
East, north and west walls of the Chaṇḍēśvara shrine in the
Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This inscription is dated in the regnal year 7+1, Kumbha śu 3, Monday,
Sōdi (Svāti)-nakshatra. The details of the date are irregular.

It records the grant of 4 mā kīḻaraiyē-mūṉṟu-mā-mukkāṇi, made after
the purchase, as dēvadāṉa to provide for the food offerings to the deity
Pallavaṉīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār installed as āśraya liṅga in the temple of
the deity Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiya-ṉāyaṉār of Uśāttāṉam alias Kēraḷakulāśaṉich-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu by Pichchaṉ
Pallavarāyaṉ alias Śōḻiyavaraiyaṉ of Paiyyūr in Paiyyūr-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 216.
(A.R. No. 216 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the first prākāra of the Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This epigraph furnishes the details of date viz., Regnal year 2[8],
Kumbha ba 6, Monday and Svāti-nakshatra corresponding to 1274 A.D.,
January 29, f.d.n. ·13.

It records the grant of 7(1/2) mā of lands, made after the purchase, from
several persons, by the peruṅguṟi-mahāsabhai of Kēraḷakulāśaṉich-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam as iṟaiyili, to provide for the food-offerings and to meet
the expenses of a festival (tirunāḷ-aḻivu) to the deity Tirujñāṉampeṟṟa-
piḷḷaiyār set up by Sembaṅkuḍaiyāṉ Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-uḍaiyaṉ
Kūttāḍunāyaṉār, a merchant of Mummuḍiśōḻapuram. The image is stated
to have been set up in the temple of Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiya-ṉāyaṉār of
Uśāttāṉam alias Kēraḷakulāśaṉich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in
Puṟaṅkarambai-nāḍu in Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. Among the boundaries of
the lands granted as iṟaiyili after purchase, the land of mā-kāṇi-mundirigaik-
kīḻaraiyē-iraṇḍu-mā-araikkāṇi is stated to have been surveyed as per the
village measure in the 38th year of Periyadēvar Tribhuvanavīradēva.

Tribhuvanavīradēva is known to be the name of Kulōttuṅga III. Here
he is attributed with the title Periyadēvar. Just as Rājarāja III stood in
relation to Kulōttuṅga III, Rājēndra III too is related. The survey made
in several places as referred to in the present record indicates not only the
prosperous condition that prevailed but also the maintenance of the lands
within his empire.

inscription.head:
No. 217.
(A.R. No. 217 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the first prākāra of the Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This inscription furnishes the details of date viz., Regnal year 12, Kaṉṉi
śu 3, Monday and Hasta-nakshatra.

It records a deed of agreement issued by the peruṅguṟi-mahāsabhai
to the Ādichaṇḍēśvaradēvakaṉmigaḷ of the temple of Tiruvuśāttāṉam-
uḍaiya-ṉāyaṉār of Uśāttāṉam alias Kēraḷakulāśaṉich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam,
a brahmadēya in Puṟaṅkarambai-nāḍu in Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu for the
lands granted as iṟaiyili, to provide for the food-offerings to the deity Nambi-
viṉāyakap-piḷḷaiyār set up in the tirumaḍai-viḷāgam in the temple. Among
the boundaries of the lands endowed as tax-free, mention may be made
of the highway of Vikramaśōḻanallūr, lands called Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-
brahmārāyaṉ, Sembiyaṉ-brahmārāyaṉ, etc. The deed was signed by
Kōmaḷattu Nīlagiriyaṉ, Eṭṭukkūr Sūriyadēva-baṭṭaṉ and several others.

inscription.head:
No. 218.
(A.R. No. 218 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the first prākāra of the Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This inscription may be assigned to c. 14th century on palaeographical
considerations.

It records an arrangement (vyavasthai) made by the nāṭṭavar of
Puṟaṅkarambai-nāḍu to provide 25 kalam of paddy, by raising crops in the
maḍak-kāṇi of fifteen mā of lands obtained from the sabhaiyār, to the
Paripūrṇṇaśivāchāryar who presided over the Kūttāḍunāyaṉār-maḍam in the
western street of the tirumaḍaiviḷāgam of the temple of the deity
Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiya-ṉāyaṉār of Uśāttāṉam alias Kēraḷakulāśaṉich-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Puṟaṅkarambai-nāḍu, in Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. The
Śaiva teacher belonged to the lineage (santāṉam) of Tiruchchattimuṟṟattu-
mudaliyār. Among the boundaries of the lands obtained from the twelfth
taram in the west side of the lake called Mahēndrapērēri of the above village
are to the east of the eastern bank of the channel flowing into the lake
from the Gaṇḍarādittaṉ-vāyttalai (head-sluice of the channel called
Gaṇḍarādittaṉ), to the south of Vāḻavandanallūr, a dēvadāṉa of the deity
Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiya-ṉāyaṉār, etc. This arrangement of the nāṭṭavar was
attested by several signatories including the nāṭṭu-kaṇakku Perujñar-kiḻavaṉ
Āḍavallāṉ and others (names mentioned).

inscription.head:
No. 219.
(A.R. No. 219 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the first prākāra of the Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This inscription may be assigned to c. 14th century on palaeographical
considerations.

It refers to an order (ōlai) of the mahāsabhai of Uśāttāṉam alias
Kēraḷakulāśaṉich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam issued on the fifth day of the month
Tai of the above year.

It records the a gift of 30 kalam of paddy as maḍakku from out of
18 mā of lands separated from the above village in the name of
Kūttāḍumnāyaganallūr as maḍappuṟam to the Śaiva teacher. This
arrangement (vyavasthai) of the nāṭṭavar, made at the instance of nāṭṭuk-
kaṇakku Perujñār-kiḻavar Kōvāṇa[yā]r was attested by Rājamahēndrappiḷḷai,
ūr-kaṇakku Śēṟṟūr-uḍaiyāṉ Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷappiriyaṉ and several others.

inscription.head:
No. 220.
(A.R. No. 220 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the first prākāra of the Mantrapurīśvara temple.

The record furnishes the details of date viz., Regnal year 11, Vaikāśi
27 which are insufficient for verification. Moreover, the name of the
reigning king is not given in the record to clinch the correct date. But
palaeographically this epigraph may be assigned to c. 14th century.

It records an arrangement (vyavasthai) made by Ādichaṇḍēśvara-
dēvakaṉmigaḷ of the temple of Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār to provide
for the food-offerings, on three sandhis a day, lamps and worship on the
days of occasions like vishu, ayaṉa-saṁkramaṇa, kārttigai festivals, etc., to
the deity Aḻagiya-Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār set up in the
circumambulatory path (tirunaḍaimāḷigai) in the above temple by
Īśāṉaśivāchāriyar who belonged to the lineage (santāṉa) of the mudaliyārs
of Tiruchchattimuṟṟam. It is stated that the endowment was maintained from
out of 300 varāgaṉ-paṇam deposited into the temple treasury. The interest
(poliśai) accrued from this amount annually is 108 paṇam. The paddy
obtained for one paṇam was one kalam. Six nāḻi of rice was utilized for
the food-offerings made to the deity.

This document was attested by the temple accountant (kōyiṟ-kaṇakku)
Uśāttāṉam-uḍaiyāṉ Tiruvuśāttāṉappiriyaṉ, dēvakaṉmi Poṉṉambala-baṭṭaṉ,
māḍāpattiyam Tirumāligaip-piḷḷai and others.

inscription.head:
No. 221.
(A.R. No. 221 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the first prākāra of the Mantrapurīśvara temple.

It records an order (ōlai) issued by Rājanārāyaṇa Viḻupparaiyaṉ of
Paṉaiyūr granting 900 kuḻi of the dēvadāṉa lands of the god Tiruvuśāttāṉam-
uḍaiya-nāyaṉār called Buvaṉaikavīraṉ-tiruttōppu, obtained as kuḍinīṅgā-
dēvadāṉa for the establishment of a maṇḍapa in order to conduct the
festivals to the deity. An amount of ten paṇam accrued annually as
mēlvāram from out of the crops like coconut, etc., raised in the above grove
was endowed to meet the expenses of tiruppāvāḍai festival in the month
of Tai. The balance of mēlvāram was allotted to meet the services (tiruppaṇi)
of the deity.
This was caused to be engraved in the tirumāḷigai (temple).

The order (ōlai) was attested by Uyyavandāṉ Ambalattāḍi of Paṉaiyūr.
At the instance of Piḷḷai Rājanārāyaṇa-Viḻupparaiyar, Nārāyaṇaṉ Yōgappanmar
of Tirupperundurai drafted the order.

inscription.head:
No. 222.
(A.R. No. 222 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the first prākāra of the Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This incomplete inscription may be assigned to c. 14th century on
palaeographical considerations.

It records a deed of agreement on the sale of tax-free (iṟaiyili) lands
made over to the Ādichaṇḍēśvaradēvakaṉmigaḷ of the temple of
Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiya-ṉāyaṉār of Uśāttāṉam alias Kēraḷakulāśaṉich-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in [Puṟaṅ]karambai-nāḍu, in Rājē[ndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu*].
Vāḻavandanallūr is stated to be a tirunāmattukkāṇi village in the western
side of Rājēndraśōḻapērēri. The details are not clear as the record is damaged
in some places.

[ pūrvapakṣattu caturttaśiyu]-

inscription.head:
No. 223.
(A.R. No. 223 of 1908.)
KŌVILŪR, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Inner gōpura of the Mantrapurīśvara temple.

This record furnishes the details of date viz., Regnal year 17, Tulā
śu 14, Friday and Aśvati-nakshatra corresponding to 1262 A.D., October 27.

It registers a gift of lands (araikkāl-nilam) and 60 kuḻi of raised land
(tiḍar-nilam) to the south of the above lands by the tāṉattār of Uśāttānattut-
tānam to the āṇḍār for rearing a flower garden named Tirukkāḷatti-uḍaiyār-
tiruṉandavaṉam, raised by Adichcha[dēvar] of Vēḷūr. The āṇḍār were
enjoined to supply garland of flowers (tiruppaḷittāmañ-chātti) to the deity
Tiruvuśāttāṉam-uḍaiya-ṉāyaṉār, for which they were to receive 60 kalam
of paddy.
